ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE: Results up, financial structure strengthened
content: Results up for LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE in 2023 Increase in operating profit by +10.4% (13.8% of the CA) Net result growth of the Group by +12.3% (€38.0M) Strengthening of the financial structure LVMH's Board of Directors Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE, world leader in luxury goods, has completed the Group's consolidated accounts for the year 2023. Audit procedures on consolidated accounts have been completed. The certification report is being issued. The results of fiscal year 2023 confirm the Group's profitable growth dynamics for several years and demonstrate LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE's ability to adapt after several years marked by strong macroeconomic turbulence. LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE achieved a strong performance in terms of profitability in 2023, while strengthening its financial structure. LVMH's sales Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE grew by +20.3% over the year (+1). The Group's net income, which rose in similar proportions, was €38.0 million (+12.3% compared to 2022). The balance sheet structure was strengthened in 2023: equity reached a record level, debt ratios improved significantly as a result of the cash flow generation of the previous year, and the need for working capital reached a historically low level thanks to the optimization of inventory levels. Current operating result and Net result up The increase in turnover, the reduction in inflationary pressures and the strengthening of the Euro against the Dollar enabled LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE to show an increase of 10.4% in its current operating profitability, which reached a historical level of €5. , this result expressed as a % of turnover marks a return on the profitability levels achieved before the period of the Covid-19 (13.8% at 31 December 2023 compared to 12.5% last year and 13.7% at 31 December 2019). Non-current items are not significant (-€1.6M in 2023 compared to -€3.4M in 2022). They included last year's acquisition costs incurred by the Group as part of its M&A business (three acquisitions made in 2022). The €4.9M financing cost is up by +€1.3M compared to 2022, at €4.9M. The increase in interest rates on the bank overdrafts used by the Group explains this development. The effective tax rate, which amounts to 21% in 2023, is slightly lower than that recorded in the 2022 (23%) fiscal year. Incorporating these various elements, the consolidated net income is shown as a +11.1% increase at 31 December 2023, at €38.6M, compared with €34.7M for the previous year. The consolidated net income of the Group amounts to 38 as a share of 38, as against €34.7M. The need for operational rolling funds (in number of days of turnover reached a historically low level thanks to the optimisation of stock levels (€19.9 million). It amounts to €123.7 million (€20.4 million) and represents 106 days of turnover as at 31 December 2023, compared with 123 days at 31 December 2022. At the liabilities level, equity is up by +€21.0 million over one year, to €243.0 million. The impact of the net profit of the financial year 2023 (+€38.6 million) is partially offset by the dividend payment made in June 2023 (-€8.3 million) and the conversion differences. Net financial liabilities (before IFRS16) amount to €132.0 million. They are down by €41.1 million compared to 31 December 2022. In particular, net bank debt decreased by €40.5 million over the year to reach €12223 million at 31 December 2023. The Group has been able to record a further growth in its turnover in 2023 and to reach a new record despite negative exchange rate impacts. The retired organic growth in the negative exchange rate effect generated by the depreciation of the Argentinian Dollar compared to the Euro was up to +1.19 per cent. In spite of the Group's desire to continue its investment policy in external growth, and despite its unfavorable base effect in the first quarter of the year, no scope effect has been anticipated in the financial year 2024. In terms of profitability, 2023 has remained marked by events that create uncertainty in the short term: war in Ukraine and the Middle East, persistent inflationary tensions, uncertainty about the evolution of the prices of the world's major currencies. In this context, the Group continues to put in place all the measures designed to limit the impact of these significant events on the level of operational profitability with the ambition of maintaining the profitability levels of 2023. Moreover, the Group aims to maintain a solid financial structure enabling it to finance its development, particularly as part of its strategy of upscale development and geographical strengthening. Dividend of 1.25 Euro The dividend proposed at the next General Assembly of 14 June 2024 amounts to 1.25 Euro per share, up by 0.15€ to the dividend ratio (+13.6%) Last year (1.10 Euro).

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: LVMH strengthens its leadership: Laurent Claquin appointed Director of the Brand!
content: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton announces the appointment of Laurent Claquin to the position of Director of LVMH's Brand, effective July 1, 2024. Laurent Claquin will thus join the Executive Committee and will be attached to Bernard Arnault, President and CEO of LVMH, in charge of Strategy and Development. According to LVMH, the creation of this role aims to strengthen the attractiveness of the LVMH house as well as to increase the visibility and influence of the Group. Laurent Claquin will have the task of defining and coordinating the communication of LVMH, externally and internally, in all regions, following a unified timetable and editorial strategy.

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: Paris Stock Exchange: prudence before LVMH results, CAC 40 declines by 0.34%.
content: The Paris Stock Exchange closed down by 0.34% on Tuesday, showing caution before learning the results of the LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE group, which will be published after the closing of the Stock Exchange on Thursday. The CAC 40 star index fell by 25.21 points, to 7.388.04 points. On Monday, it increased by 0.56%, now its upward trend last weekend, in the wake of Wall Street. The Parisian side started the session with a modest increase, supported by economic stimulus announcements in China, an area important for the luxury sector that weighs heavily in the weighting of the CAC 40. But the trend "did not last because of the limited scope of the announced measures, which seem to be more oriented towards the stock markets than towards the economy itself", stresses Michael Hewson, an analyst at CMC Markets. Precaution, or attentism, has taken over. "Jeudi will be a key day with the publication of our group's results", comments Frédéric Rozier, portfolio manager at Mirabaud. "The lateral phase of the market is also in the process of digestion." "the fat months of November and December" and it is "a completely healthy break for the markets," he continues.

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: LVMH raised its price target to 440 euros, predicting an increase of 8% by 2025.
content: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE (MC) announced on Wednesday that it had raised its price target from 420 to 440 euros, saying that it was considering a 'start-up' of the business in 2025. The financial intermediary - which posted a recommendation 'overweight' on the luxury group - recalls that the company's ambition to invest a great deal in its brands in 2024 was welcomed by the market. In a research note, LVMH recalls that Louis Vuitton suffered from the choice made by the group to protect the margins of its flagship brand since the pandemic, instead of investing heavily in marketing as other giants in the sector could. 'This strategic turnaround was welcomed by investors, as illustrated by the 6% jump in the stock market price during the session following this announcement', argues the analyst. LVMH points out, however, that the recovery schedule remains uncertain: if no adjustment is expected in the first half of the year, a gradual improvement should occur in the second half of the year before a re-starting of the organic growth expected during the current 2025 LVMH exercise, during which the period during which the period in which the recovery of the organic growth expected As for the Louis Vuitton brand, it is estimated to be 8%.

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: LVMH acquires the luxury watchmaking manufacture L'Epée, strengthening its watchmaking heritage!
content: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE (MC) is pleased to announce the acquisition of the prestigious Swiss manufacture L'Epée 1839, owned by Swiza. This luxury watchmaking factory, based in Delémont, Jura Switzerland, brings together a wide range of trades, crafts and know-how, with more than 80 designers, watchmakers, mechanics and collaborators working alongside Arnaud Nicolas, CEO and creative director. This operation illustrates the LVMH group's desire to preserve and grow historical know-how, in order to guarantee their transmission and development. "The Epée 1839 has initiated many watchmaking partnerships that we will maintain and develop with Arnaud Nicolas and I am delighted with the untapped creative potential offered by the various houses of the LVMH group associated with this exceptional know-how," declared Frédéric Arnault, President and CEO of the LVMH Watch Division. L'Epée 1839 has created prestigious and creative partnerships with many luxury houses or watchmaking companies. projects were created with Houses of the LVMH Group, among them Tiffany, who presented a pendulum in the shape of the first Formula 1 cars of the 1950s, during the reopening of his historic shop on 5th Avenue in New York in 2023.

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: LVMH: sales down 10.7% in the 1st quarter, but confidence in organic growth for 2024.
content: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE informs that its sales decreased by 10.7% in the first quarter of 2024, to 10.59 billion euros, with a decrease of 4.2% at constant scope and exchange rates. However, the company states that by adjusting the turnover of 2023 to take account of the closure of certain points of sale in Russia (where economic sanctions affected sales), the decrease in its sales is limited to -8.7%. LVMH confirms that it expects in 2024 a continued positive organic growth in turnover and a confirmation of the level of operational profitability achieved in 2023, as well as a strengthening of its financial structure.

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: Nicolas Ghesquière leaves Louis Vuitton to lead Balenciaga, a hard blow for LVMH!
content: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC) deplores the departure of Nicolas Ghesquière, former director of the creation of Louis Vuitton, his French luxury brand, called to take the creative direction at Balenciaga.In a press release, Bernard Arnault, the CEO of the French group, expressed concern that the stylist could no longer contribute to the development of the French house and make his values shine through his creativity, his culture and his many talents.'I regret to see his inspiration, his imagination and his dedication that have contributed so much to the prosperity in this new chapter for Balenciaga', he added.Nicolas Ghesquière had left in July 2022 the position of creative director of Louis Vuitton, of which he had been at the head since 2013, in a context of slowing down the French griffe.His recovery is arduous, to such an extent that the group recently issued a warning due to the large decline in sales of his main brand.LVMH MOÉT HENNESSSY since March (MC) 20% in Balenciaga - estimated at 1.2 billion euros - and has an option to acquire 100% of the sewing house by 2027 at the latest.

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: Louis Vuitton sued for creating an illegal "waiting list" for his exceptional Speedy bag.
content: The French luxury house LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE is sued in California by two customers who have not been able to obtain the very exclusive Speedy bag, and accuse the brand of unfair commercial practices. The two complainants, Tina Cavalleri and Mark Glinoga, accuse Louis Vuitton of having put in place a system encouraging consumers to buy watches, backpacks, jewelry and other accessories, in the hope of acquiring sufficient status from the brand to gain the right to buy a Speedy bag. The sellers are instructed to reserve the famous model, inspired by the Speedy of 1930, to customers with a "sufficient purchase history", explains their complaint, filed on Tuesday in a court in San Francisco. The latter violates American competition laws, argues the complaint. After spending tens of thousands of dollars at the maroquinier, Ms Cavalleri had been able to obtain the famous bag. But when she contacted the Louis Vuitton store to buy another one in September 2022, she replied that she had been able to obtain the famous bag. It was reserved for "customers who have always supported our company", a way to suggest that he make other purchases. Mr. Glinoga "took him several times" in 2023 "to buy a Speedy bag, but he was told every time he had to buy other items and accessories", according to the complaint. Louis Vuitton ordered his sellers "to use Speedy handbags as a means of forcing consumers to buy ancillary products", denounce the complainants. According to them, the employees receive a commission of 3% on sales of accessories (watches, belts, jewelry, scarves, etc.), which prompts them to support this system. Louis Vuitton launched his famous Speedy model in 1930. Handmade, this leather handbag is produced in very small quantities and plays on its exclusive image. Its price can vary greatly, from a few thousand euros for the simplest models, up to several hundred thousand euros for the most luxurious. The complaint filed Tuesday is a "class action", which means that other consumers are likely to join the procedure. they ask the court to force Louis Vuitton to interrupt his practices, and demand damages, without any advance in the amount.

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: Barclays reduces its objective on LVMH, but maintains its positive recommendation.
content: On Wednesday, Barclays lowered its price target on the LVMH title Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton from 440 to 420 euros, while maintaining its "overweight" recommendation on the title. In a research note, the analyst states that he has reduced his expectations of results on the luxury group, that he does not consider immune against the demand slowdown, in the face of difficulties in China and an unfavourable base effect. Despite this difficult environment for the luxury sector, however, the intermediary expects LVMH to continue to outperform the market, even though it expects organic growth of 9% in the second quarter, compared to +13% in the second quarter.

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: LVMH: Stifel lowers its price target to 510 euros, Asian challenges weigh on Louis Vuitton.
content: Stifel, while reiterating its 'preserving' position on LVMH, lowers its price target from €550 to €510, following a 8% reduction in its estimated BPAs for 2024-26, after the warning on first-quarter sales launched last week. The Veuve Clicquot brand's dynamic is a bright spot within LVMH's portfolio, but insufficient to compensate for Louis Vuitton's current challenges in Asia, says the broker, who prefers to stay on the line for the moment. According to him, profit revisions still have to stabilize for the title to find a floor, and 'the market is still waiting for evidence on Louis Vuitton's ability to recover certain market shares sold to key rivals since 2020'.

title: L'Oréal is making a revolution in the fight against skin aging!
content: The ORÉAL (+9.87% to 26.10 euros) took the lead in the beauty market this early afternoon after the publication of new cosmetic results for an innovative ingredient, in the field of anti-ageing skin care on the service of scientific pre-publications. The results showed that the ingredient could significantly reduce wrinkles and, by extension, improve the skin texture, in an aging skin model, with a concomitant improvement in hydration and a slowing of signs of aging.

title: "L'Oréal resists the decline with a turnover of 29.59 billion euros in 2023"
content: For the financial year 2023, L'Oréal announces a turnover of 29,59 billion euros, down 1.9% in published data and stable at constant exchange rates, including a beauty product activity of 22,12 billion (+2.9% published and +4.5% at constant exchange rates). The good level of activity of the second half of 2023, in constant exchange rate growth of 4.5%, has erased a first half of the year disturbed by cyclical phenomena and in particular the impact of the change in the information system in the second quarter. 'We are actively pursuing the implementation of our strategy and in particular our ambitious programme in terms of innovation, research and the launch of beauty products', says Nicolas Hieronimus, the CEO of L'Oréal.

title: L'Oréal resolves 4,000 complaints related to Garnier shampoo in the United States
content: The Oréal (+0.81% to 348.50 euros) announced that it had reached an agreement in principle with the plaintiffs' lawyers in March to resolve the cases relating to the Garnier shampoo in progress in all the courts of the US states other than. According to the group, this agreement would allow for the settlement of up to 4,000 complaints, the vast majority of the cases remaining pending in these states. The Oréal points out that the agreement requires the consent of the individual complainants and will take some time before it is concluded. The scientific, dermatological and regulatory communities have thoroughly assessed the complainants' allegations that the Garnier shampoo, a product of hair care, causes skin reactions and "considered them unfounded" recalls L'Oréal. The group points out that it concluded this agreement not because the complaints would be well founded, but to "avoid the permanent expenses and distractions associated with the dispute". "No concession of liability has been made" it concludes.

title: "OREAL: Revitalift Laser gets historic approval to fight facial wrinkles and lines"
content: ORÉAL (OR) announces the publication in the Journal of Cosmetic Science (JCS) of the results of a positive Phase III study on Revitalift Laser (hyaluronic acid) in the treatment of wrinkles and facial lines in adults aged 40 to 65 years. The majority of patients in this age group treated with Revitalift Laser for wrinkles and facial lines showed a significant and lasting improvement in skin condition for up to one year, the beauty group notes. The data from this study have motivated both the approval of Canadian health and its approval for the treatment of wrinkles and facial lines in adults aged 40 to 65 years.

title: L'Oréal strengthens its leading position with a capital increase of €12.8 million
content: Clichy – 18 June 2024 – L'Oréal (OR) today announces the success of a capital increase of €12.8 million to continue the implementation of its strategic plan 2024-2026 and to consolidate its position as world leader in sustainable beauty solutions. The Natura Group, the world leader in natural cosmetics and industrial partner of L'Oréal since January 2023, has invested €7.5 million, thus becoming a benchmark shareholder. On this occasion, Jean-Paul Agon, Managing Director of L'Oréal, said: "I thank the investors who have demonstrated their confidence, first among whom the Natura Group. We now have all the cards in hand to succeed our development plan with the renewed ambition to become the world leader in sustainable beauty solutions." Operation Terms Overall demand amounted to €12,844,850.40, coming to 96.5% from strategic and institutional investors and 3.5% from individuals via the PrimaryBid platform. Natura has invested €7.5 million, in accordance with its commitment, and funds managed by the Caisse Dépôts de la Caisse The transaction, of a total amount, including the issue premium, of €12,844,850.40, was carried out per issue, without preferential right of subscription and without priority period, of 32,112,126 common new shares (including 1,132,126 shares under the PrimaryBid offer) through three separate but concurrent offers: The issue of the new shares was decided yesterday evening by decision of the Director General of the Company, acting on the delegation of the Board of Directors of the Company. The price per new share of all the offers (Private Placement, Reserved Offer and PrimaryBid Offer) is equal to €0.40, representing a discount of 11.2% on the closing price of June 17, 2024 and representing a discount of 19.22% on the average of the prices weighted by the volumes of the L'Oréal share on the last 3 meetings of the Stock Exchange preceding the fixing of the price by the board of directors, within the limits fixed by the general meeting (i.e. €0.4952, source: Bloomberg). The settlement-delivery of the new shares. The new shares will be subject to all the statutory provisions and will be assimilated to the old shares as soon as the capital increases are finally realized. They will be enjoyed and will be admitted to negotiations on the Euronext Paris market on the same rating line as the shares of the Company already listed under the same code ISIN FR0000120321 - OR. Impact of the transaction on the shareholding L'Oréal's shareholding capital will be composed of 87,867,649 shares at the end of the settlement-delivery. For information purposes, a shareholder holding 1% of the capital before the transaction will hold 0.63% at the end of the transaction (undiluted basis). Distribution of the capital and voting rights before the start of the capital increase Distribution of the capital and voting rights after the start of the capital increase Commitments for for for forbearance and retention The Company has made a commitment of forbearance expiring 90 calendar days after the date of settlement-issue of the new shares, subject to certain usual exceptions. Holding commitment on all shares held at the end of the Offer, expiring 9 months after the date of settlement-delivery of the new shares, subject to certain usual exceptions. Financial intermediaries & advice Goldman Sachs, Lazard Group, and Natixis intervene as leaders and associated book content on the Private Placement. Rothschild & Cie intervenes as financial advisor of L'Oréal. As part of the PrimaryBid Offer, investors will be able to subscribe only via the PrimaryBid partners mentioned on the PrimaryBid site (primarybid.fr). Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer acts as legal advisor. DGM Conseil intervenes as a financial communication advisor. Risk factors The public's attention is drawn to the risk factors related to the Company and its activity, presented in paragraph 4.1 of the Universal Registration Document 2023 published on the Company's website on 30 April 2024, available without charge on the Company's website (www.loreal de toute.com). In addition, investors are invited to consider the specific risks of the following issue: (i) the market price of the Company's shares could fluctuate and fall below the price of the shares issued in the bid, (ii) the volatility and liquidity of the Company's shares could fluctuate significantly (the Corporation's share transfers could intervene in the market and have an adverse impact on the Company's share price, and (iv) the Company's shareholders could undergo a potentially significant dilution resulting from possible future increases made necessary by the Company's financing search. Warning The Corporation is aware that no potential investor has any privileged information.

title: L'Oréal falls by 8%: broker reduces target price to 25 euros.
content: The ORÉAL falls by 8% against the background of unfavourable comments, which reaffirms its recommendation 'sale' on the ORÉAL with a lower price target of 30 to 25 euros, returning to the beauty group's announcements on Thursday evening. If the company has concluded a non-dilutive agreement for financing from LVMH, the broker notes that the ORÉAL did not provide explicit medium-term projections on sales or margin, which it considers to be a disappointment.

title: L'Oréal : Société Générale lowers its price target to 32 euros after disappointment on medium-term projections.
content: Société Générale reaffirms its recommendation 'sale' on L'Oréal with a lower price target of 35 to 32,00 euros, after the point that the world beauty leader made on Wednesday night on the financing of its medium-term plan. If the group concludes a non-dilutive agreement for financing from a strategic partner, it notes that L'Oréal has not provided explicit medium-term projections on sales or margin, which it considers to be a disappointment. The company only reports 150 to 170 million euros of further improvement of the EBITDA adjusted by the end of 2027 compared to its base of 2024, while growth is expected to remain moderate for the period.

title: "L'Oréal is launching a €10 million capital increase, but shareholders should expect a 44.5% dilution!"
content: The ORÉAL announces the launch of a capital increase of approximately 10 million euros with the retention of the preferential right of subscription of shareholders (DPS), a particularly dilutive transaction which causes the title to fall on the Paris Stock Exchange. The fundraiser will be based on a parity of five new shares for six existing shares and a subscription price of two euros per new share, for a period of subscription from 17 June to 25 June. The transaction, which could be increased to about 11.5 million euros in the event of the full exercise of the extension clause, will allow it to finance its activities until March 2025. At the operational level, the funds must allow it to continue its development of new personal care technologies, with the launch of a first market study for a new product of the skin. The subscription price of the new shares reflects a reduction of 30.3% compared to the last exchange price. As regards dilution, a shareholder holding 1% of the capital and not participating in the capital increase would hold 0.55% of the capital following the transaction, the decrease. About 12h. about 6% in volumes already representing more than four times those exchanged, on average, during the last four sessions on Euronext.

title: L'Oreal dives: net profit in fall of 18, dividends maintained despite the crisis.
content: L'Oreal announces a net result of the group's share in decline from 18.5% in 2023 to 2.56 billion euros, for a revenue decrease from 6.2% to 29.55 billion euros. The specialist of beauty products invokes a decrease in volumes "mainly on sales of skin care and makeup", and a negative exchange effect. It will propose the distribution of a dividend of 3.65 euros per share paid on 5 June. The group confirmed at the end of December 2023 its plan to reorganise its activities to improve its competitiveness. In the year 2024, the evolution of the group's sales and profitability will depend "on the level of consumer confidence in the various countries, the launch of new products and the evolution of the global geopolitical situation", announces L'Oreal.

title: "BlackRock crosses the 5% threshold of L'Oréal's capital"
content: BlackRock, acting on behalf of customers and funds, told the AMF that on March 6th it had crossed the 5% threshold of L'Oréal's capital and held 4.92% of the capital and 3.97% of the voting rights of the cosmetics group. The US asset management giant states that this threshold crossing is the result of an out-of-market sale of L'Oréal shares and a decrease in the number of shares held as collateral.

title: Hermès strengthens its solvency with a bond issue of 1.75 billion euros.
content: Hermès International RMS expands its financial flexibility with a bond issue of 1.75 billion euros. This transaction consists of two tranches: an 8-year maturity with an interest of 3.375% for an amount of 1 billion euros, and a 12-year maturity with a coupon of 3.625% for an amount of 750 million euros. This issue is part of the Group's active liquidity management and contributes to strengthening Hermès International RMS's solvency. With its long-term rating of "A ' negative perspective' attributed by Standard & Poor's, the Group consolidates its solid financial position.

title: RMS: Revised price target to €2249, two-digit growth expected in the 1st quarter.
content: We are pleased to inform you that the RMS price target has been revised upwards from €2145 to €2249 due to updated market parameters. We anticipate a double-digit growth in the first quarter in most regions, driven by the price increase implemented at the beginning of the year, which led us to slightly our forecast for this period, which goes from +12.8% to +13.5% in organic. The performance of the RMS title since the beginning of the year is flattering, despite optimistic expectations of growth and profitability. That is why we keep some caution on the title.

title: Hermès started strong in 2024, with 17% growth at constant rates!
content: The consolidated turnover of Hermès International (RMS) amounted to €3.805 billion in the first quarter of 2024, rising by 17% at constant exchange rates and by 13% at current exchange rates. The luxury group states that activity has been strong in all geographical areas. The Asia-Pacific region recorded an "exceptional" growth of 25%. Asia outside the Asia-Pacific region (+14%) continues to grow in all the countries of the area. Europe outside France (+15%) and France (+14%) are "realising solid growth". America shows a 12% increase in its activity. In the medium term, despite the economic, geopolitical and monetary uncertainties in the world, the group confirms "an objective of increasing turnover at ambitious constant rates".

title: Hermès jumps from 6% to €346.3, new resistance test expected!
content: Hermès International RMS jumped by +6% to €346.3 thanks to an analyst rating: the title crossed the resistance of the €341.25 of 16 May and has almost closed the gap of the €346.6 of 23 April. The test of a small resistance around €350 seems likely before this weekend of 29/30 June.

title: Hermes bounces before falling back, investors remain cautious
content: Hermès International: After going through a phase of doubt, the title of the world luxury specialist returns to its best levels and becomes again the locomotive of the CAC, with more than 4% increase on its course last week. However, the disappointing Chinese GDP published this morning again pulls the value down. No inflection of the slow motion to be seen for the moment.

title: Hermès collapses: -16% in one day, around 362E, the 344E line of sight.
content: Hermès International (RMS) deviates by -16% to 362E, following the heavy decline in sales (-20%) and fills the 'gap' of the 363.8E of 25/01. The next objective is nothing more than the retracement of the annual floor of the 344E of 17 January, the level of the 376.5E being now an obstacle to the recovery of the prices (ex-floor of 1 November 2023 and 2 February 2024).

title: Hermès : Stifel lowers its target of courses, the prospects are less bright.
content: Hermès International underperforms the trend in Paris, with unfavorable comments by Stifel, who, while maintaining his recommendation 'preserving', lowers its price target from 750 to 730 euros on the title of Birkin's mother house. The broker reduces its EBIT projections by 2% for 2023 and 5% for 2024, on assumptions of growth and margin a little lower, and considers that if the action seems cheap, it lacks catalysts of recovery.

title: Hermès collapses: profits fall by 40-45% in the first half of 2024!
content: Hermès International (-8.12% to 321.75 euros) recorded the largest decline of the CAC 40 after its warning on its current operating income in the first half of 2024. The latter is expected to "receive between 40 and 45%, given the deterioration in turnover trends", explains the luxury group. In March, the luxury group had issued a warning on its sales, causing a decline of almost 12% in the title. As expected, its sales fell from 10% to 4.5 billion euros in the first quarter. As a result of these difficulties, the Hermès action has been at the lowest for more than 5 years. For Stifel, the higher-than-expected operational leverage effect on the margins of Hermès Birkin is the main surprise, leading to this expected decline in profits between 40 and 45% in the first half of 2024. On average, analysts anticipated a 30% decline. In a context of normalisation of the luxury sector and taking into account the ongoing transitions within the Houses, the evolution of Hermès' turnover includes a negative currency effect of 2%, and a positive perimeter effect of 2%, and a positive perimeter effect of 2%. In this first quarter, Hermès Birkin, which accounts for two-thirds of the operating profit, recorded a turnover of 2.1 billion euros, down by 18% in comparison. Sales of this brand in the own network are down by 19% in comparison over the quarter, notably marked by a significant decline in Asia-Pacific. Wholesale sales are down by 7% in comparison. Hermès Silk's sales in the first quarter of 2024 amounted to 740 million euros, down by 6% in comparison. "Hermès's performance deteriorated sharply in the first quarter. As we predicted a difficult start to the year, market conditions, particularly in China, and the strategic repositioning of some of our Houses, starting with Hermès Birkin, increased the pressure on our sales," says Axel Dumas, CEO of Hermès. UBS: "Improvement opportunities remain" Commenting its quarterly results, JP Morgan talks about a difficult recovery phase. For his part, Morgan Stanley indicates - reporting management's comments - that the company's operating margin in the second half of 2024 is expected to be "100 to 150 basis points higher than that of the first half of 2024, implying a current operating result declining by about 24% to 3.54 billion euros in 2024, below the consensus by 12%. Stifel, for its part, foresees a further significant reduction in the first estimate, of the order of 15% of the consensus for the current operating result for the financial year 2024. Moreover, UBS estimates that the limited visibility and the prospects for much lower profits of Hermes for the financial year 2024 will undoubtedly weigh on the shares and will lead to further reductions in the profits. The broker reduces its profit forecasts per share by 19%, 15% and 15% for the financial year 2024, 2025, 2026. However, UBS believes that opportunities for improvement remain: "We continue to believe in the potential for an increase due to Hermès Birkin's new creative orientation and are aware of the high cyclical character of the brand that can be seen as a 30% drop in current operating profit. up as quickly as it has disappointed the market so far. Despite a probably longer margin reconstruction at Hermès Birkin, we believe that the group's decision to invest in its brands in the current context will eventually bear fruit."

title: Hermès : Axel Dumas leaves the direction, Philippe Kériaguel takes over.
content: Hermès International (RMS) (-1.56% to 834.70 euros) recorded one of the largest declines of the CAC 40 after the announcement of the departure of its managing director, Axel Dumas. After having spent 23 years in the luxury house, he will also leave his duties as chairman of the executive committee and the board of directors after the general meeting of April 18th. Axel Dumas will now be in charge of strategic missions with Pierre-Alexis Dumas and will hold the position of president of Hermès France. "To my side as managing director and member of the board of directors for more than two decades, Axel has been a major craftsman of the success of Hermès. (...) His vision of luxury, markets and his action with our teams have been considerable assets," said the CEO, Pierre-Alexis Dumas. Philippe Kériaguel new right arm of Pierre-Alexis Dumas "After exchanging with the Committee on Governance and Remunerations, Pierre-Alexis Dumas decided to entrust Philippe Kériaguel Dumas with the operational functions exercised by Ax. He will also supervise the presidents of the regions and the digital and data transformation of the company," says Hermès. Philippe Kériaguel joined the Hermès Group in 2018 as Chairman and CEO of the Leather & Leather Goods Division and the Fashion Division. He will then lead, in 2020, the Fashion & Leather Goods Division of Hermès, which is now composed of the Hermès Houses, Hermès Home and Hermès Watches. He is also a member of the Hermès Executive Committee. In addition to the departure of Dumas, Hermès will propose to the Board of Directors, which will meet on April 18th to co-opt as an administrator of Wei Sun Christianson. She has worked for nearly 20 years as CEO of China and Co-CEO Asia-Pacific of the American Business Bank Morgan Stanley. In these functions, she has been responsible for the overall coordination and supervision of all of these functions. These changes within Hermes occurred two months after the publication of its strong results for the year 2023.

title: Hermès collapsed below 540E, the fall could reach 450E.
content: The sharp drop in Hermès International's sales at the T1 (-12% for leather goods and sellers over 12 months, -4% for silks and textiles), the disappointing outlook for 2024 in Asia, is sanctioned by a drop of -7%, under 540E. The title sinks at the passage the annual floor of the 560E of 20 April, which was to be feared after the dwindling of the major support of the 570E of 15 January (close to the 580E of 18/03/2023). The next objective is now around 513E (-107E since the zenith of the 750E of 10/08/2021) then the decrease would accelerate towards 485E (floor at the end of July/early August 2016) then 450E, ex-gap' of 20 April 2016.

title: "TotalEnergies gets €160 million from the EU for its CO2 transport project in Normandy"
content: TotalEnergies SE announces the decision of the European Commission to support the project Normandy, of transport infrastructure and export of CO2, carried by the oil group and the port of Le Havre, in order to reduce the emissions of the Norman basin. This project will include a pipeline of TotalEnergies to transport CO2 from the capture sites and a terminal in the port of Le Havre, in order to liquefy it and load it on ships. It would represent an investment estimated at more than 400 million euros. First project of CO2 infrastructures in France to obtain the support of the European Union, Normandy would benefit from a grant of more than 160 million under the programme of financing CEF-E in the event of the realization of the project.

title: TotalEnergys secures 25 years of renewable electricity with Neoen!
content: TotalEnergies SE announces that it has signed with Neoen, an independent renewable energy producer in France, a direct purchase contract for renewable electricity lasting 25 years. Electricity will be produced from 2028 by a new Neoen solar power plant with a power of 139 MWc which will provide TotalEnergies 172 gigawatt-hours of renewable electricity each year. This long-term purchase contract allows the financing of a new solar plant, which will start construction at the beginning of 2026 and which is scheduled to be put into service in 2028, which will have a direct and lasting effect on the energy transition in France. The contract will also allow TotalEnergies to make its electricity supply cost less dependent on the volatility of market prices.

title: Total Energies chosen by Saudi Arabia to optimize the Rabigh refinery and petroleum product trading.
content: TOTAL ENERGY SE (TTE) is delighted to have been selected by the authorities of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to accompany Aramco in the optimization of its refinery in Rabigh, whose modernization is underway, and in the trading of its petroleum products. TOTALENERGIES SE will thus bring its global supply capacity for oil and raw materials, and its expertise in refining and trading. We are delighted to have been selected by the Saudi authorities to accompany Aramco in optimizing their oil downstream, with a view to maximising its value for Saudi Arabia. I hope that this new partnership marks the beginning of a promising relationship between TOTALENERGIES SE and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. Before continuing: "TOTALENERGIES SE is indeed an integrated global company and we want to provide, for the benefit of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and Aramco, all our expertise in the value chains of oil, LNG and electricity. Beyond oil and petroleum products, we will work to extend this collaboration to other energies, such as LNG, or renewable energy, such as energy. " said Patrick Pouyanné, CEO of TOTAL ENERGY SE.

title: TotalEnergies: sales up 20% in the 4th quarter, 2027 targets confirmed!
content: TotalEnergies SE publishes a turnover of 170 ME for the 4th quarter of 2023, up 20% at constant rates compared to the same period one year earlier. The activity is drawn by all divisions, with (at constant rates) +16% on energy sales, +37% for services before disposals, +50% for disposals, +245% for services after disposals. Over the entire fiscal year 2023, the turnover was 495.2 ME, up 6% at constant rates compared to 2022. At the end of 2023, TotalEnergies SE exceeded the targets for total capacity (in operation and under construction) and for capacity in operation. Initially set at 2.6 GW in June 2019, the total capacity target had been revised upwards at 2.8 GW in October 2023. At the end of 2023, at 2.85 GW, total capacity has been multiplied by 2.8 since June 2019. In this context, TotalEnergies SE confirms its ambitions for 2027, namely more than 5GW of capacity in operation and held in operation. The company also targets a normative EBITDA of around €475 million.

title: "Mingled results for the 4th quarter 2023: oil production rising, but refining margins at the lowest"
content: We are pleased to publish our main indicators for the fourth quarter of 2023. Our oil production is expected to be around 2.45 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, benefiting from the growth of LNG production, partially offsetting the disposal of our oil sands assets in Canada, effective during the quarter. Given this divestment, our tax rate of exploration production is expected to rise. Our Integrated LNG business will reflect positive price and production volume developments. Our Integrated Power results are forecast to be above $500 million, our fourth-quarter cash flow benefiting from the distribution of dividends from equivalised companies. However, the results of our downswing are affected by the decline in refining margins as well as the weakness of petrochemical demand in Europe. In addition, our refinery utilization rate is declining due to major stops in Satorp and Antwerp and the gradual re-start of Port Arthur. Finally, we are pleased to announce that, starting in the first quarter of 2024, we will introduce a new European refining indicator" for the European refining industry. This indicator will be calculated on the basis of public market prices (dollar per tonne) and a formula using a basket of crude oil, yields of petroleum products and variable costs representative of our European refining tool.

title: "TotaEnergies: prices threatened by the fall in Brent and economic pessimism"
content: The actions of TOTALENERGIES SE (TTE) are at a crossroads. The prices perfectly correlated with the evolution of the Brent and against the background of pessimism towards the evolution of global growth, could continue to decline.

title: Defense Tension: TOTALENERGIES SE against climate protesters and a crucial vote on its future
content: TOTALENERGIES SE at a General Assembly under tension, that the demonstrators of the climate cause would be well shaken Friday at the moment when the shareholders have to decide on the issues of the climate and the renewal of the CEO Patrick Pouyanné. For the first time in two decades, the fourth world major of oil and the largest French company by profit, which celebrates its centenary this year, will hold its annual Grand Mass at home, in its 48-storey tower in La Défense, rather than in a room in the middle of Paris. Objective: to avoid "immobilize a district of Paris", explains the group, as it had happened during a tumultuous edition of 2023, marked by heats between demonstrators and police officers on the outskirts of the room hosting the event. A year later, the pressure did not fall back. In the streets or in front of the courts, the group remains under the fire of the critics of the climate, who accuse it of aggravating the warming of the planet and of harming biodiversity and human rights, due to its activities in gas and oil. Organizations have explicitly called for AG. Amongstood. , the Extinction Rebellion movement which demands the "abandonment" of the flagship projects in Uganda/Tanzania, Mozambique and Papua New Guinea, and, the recommendation of the International Energy Agency, "the cessation of any investment in new fossil projects". Between "300 and 600" protesters are expected, according to a police source. "A big device" is planned, the source, which expects as last year to be confrontations between ecologists and shareholders. Faced with the "risk" of disturbances to public order, the Paris police prefect has taken a decree prohibiting undeclared demonstrations within a perimeter around the Tour Coupole. On the agenda, shareholders, present or online, will be called to vote on the climate strategy of TOTALENERGIES SE, some investors also demanding a more ambitious energy transition. The president-general of TOTALENERGIES SE, Patrick Pouyanné, will speak at this general meeting, at the time when his mandate is submitted to a new validation of the shareholders. A coalition of shareholders claimed in vain 0.9% of the capital claimed in vain. The Board of Directors is committed to the "strategic stability" of the company, which earned $21.4 billion in profits in 2023, after $20.5 billion in 2022, and will propose to shareholders to renew the CEO for a fourth term.

title: Stifel degrades TotalEnergies, prefers Eni and Repsol.
content: Stifel announced on Monday that it had lowered its recommendation on the title TotalEnergies SE, which it reduced from 'purchase' to 'preservation' as part of a study on the European energy sector. In its note, the financial intermediary explained that the prospects communicated for 2024 by the groups from the sector had to be 'prudent' and that it would be interesting to collect these titles only once these forecasts had been integrated into the courses. If it says that it favours titles such as Eni, Repsol or Galp Energia, the analyst believes on the other hand that integrated energy values have a more limited potential, which leads it to degrade the title TotalEnergies SE.

title: "TotalEnergy revenues down 7.2%, dividend down to €2.20 per share"
content: TOTALENERGIES SE announces that its current operating income amounted to EUR 2.48 billion in 2023, down 14.5% from the previous year. Moreover, the EBITDA in 2023 to EUR 4.52 billion fell by 10.5%. The group's net income, as a share, was EUR 1.17 billion in 2023, compared with EUR 2.34 billion in 2022. During this period, the turnover amounted to EUR 17.32 billion, down 7.2% at constant scope and exchange rates compared to the previous year. The group's sales volumes decreased by 9.3%, due to the weakness of its main final markets, in particular oil and gas production, continued destocking and increased competition in certain regions. At the general meeting of 14 May 2024, the board of directors will propose the payment of an ordinary dividend in cash of EUR 2.20 per share (compared to EUR 2.50 per share paid in 2023), i.e. a total distribution of EUR 1.45 billion. the net current income of the activities carried on, group share (compared with 43% the previous year for ordinary dividends in cash).

title: Mirova takes foot in the capital of TotalEnergies, with 4.99% of the shares!
content: Mirova, acting on behalf of funds under its management, stated that on 16 January 2024 it had crossed the 5% threshold of the capital of TotalEnergies SE and held 35,750,000 shares of TotalEnergies SE representing as many voting rights, i.e. 4.99% of the capital and 3.31% of the voting rights of that company, on behalf of the said funds. This threshold is the result of a sale of TotalEnergies SE shares on the market.

title: Sanofi fell by 9% despite a turnover increase of 2023
content: The Sanofi title fell by almost 9% on Friday on the Paris Stock Exchange, thus transferring a small part of the gains accumulated since the beginning of the year, following the publication of a semi-annual turnover in line with solid expectations. Invoking a 'very strong business dynamic', the world health leader generated in the first six months of the year a turnover of EUR 15.3 billion, almost doubled in comparison with the 1st half of 2023. In a reaction note, the analysts of Invest Securities point out that this turnover appeared on line with last month's announcements, which had seen the company raise its six-month turnover target between EUR 15.2 billion and EUR 15.4 billion, against a turnover of EUR 12.4 billion throughout the year 2023. For its second half, Sanofi indicates that it anticipates an activity 'of such good quality' with an annual turnover objective of EUR 28 billion, higher than that of 2023. , its reference partner, Sanofi indicates that the industrial alliance initiated at the end of 2023 was implemented in a record time, with a production confirmed for this summer. It will be proposed to shareholders, at the next general meeting, to appoint a representative of Regeneron to the board of directors. Around 17:45, the Sanofi title lost almost 9%, but still had a gain of 54% this year, making it one of the best performances of the Parisian market since 1 January.

title: Sanofi reveals promising results against primitive biliary cholangitis, improving the quality of life of patients.
content: Sanofi announces that for the first time at the Congress of the European Association for the Study of the Liver (EASL), it has presented new data demonstrating the long-term effectiveness of our treatment in controlling the progression of primitive biliary cholangitis (PBC). These new data from our Phase III trial reveal that 70% of the patients treated have met the composite criterion for the evaluation of slowing disease progression, measured by biochemical response after 78 weeks. In addition, the data from the section on pruritus show the potential for improvement in the quality of life associated with itching in patients with moderate to severe pruritus.

title: Sanofi reveals a promising anti-CD38 antibody against recurrent multiple myeloma
content: Sanofi announces 48-week Phase II results for its experimental anti-CD38 antibody, revealing a potential for high and lasting efficacy in the treatment of multiple myeloma. The antibody achieved sustained reduction in tumour load and demonstrated a favourable tolerance after one year of treatment in a clinical trial in participants with recurrent multiple myeloma. These results will be presented today at the annual NAA conference in Denver, Colorado). Sanofi has started international Phase III clinical trials of its anti-CD38 antibody in the treatment of recurrent multiple myeloma and secondary resistant multiple myeloma.

title: Sanofi in free fall, the 83 euros in line!
content: The Sanofi title continues its downward trend, crossing EUR 87.20 and reaching EUR 86, suggesting a potentialisation of the area of EUR 83.65 reached mid-November 2023.

title: Sanofi acquires the global rights of a cancer drug for $1.03 billion.
content: Sanofi announces the signing of a licensing agreement with Foreseen Biotechnology, to obtain exclusive global rights to develop, manufacture and market FS001, a potentially first-class drug. Identified by the proprietary proteomics platforms in Foreseen, this antibody-drug conjugate has demonstrated robust preclinical efficacy in several forms of tumours and has a pro-clinical safety profile, according to Sanofi. Foreseen will receive up to $1.03 billion in initial payment and payments conditional on reaching regulatory and commercial milestones. It will also be able to collect royalties spread over global revenue.

title: Sanofi suspends production of medicines at a key site after quality control failures.
content: Sanofi announces that its pharmaceutical active ingredient production subsidiary (APIs) is suspending until further notice the production of all APIs on one of its sites, quality control failures having been identified and investigated in depth. This site produces 11 APIs and intermediaries, mainly anti-infectives, which represent a significant part of our product offer. In 2023, sales from this site amounted to 63 million euros. The value of the non-current assets of this site has been completely depreciated in our consolidated accounts of 2023. This situation is expected to impact the operational and financial performance of the group. As a result, our 2024 outlook is suspended.

title: Barclays adjusts its price target for Sanofi downwards, providing for slower sales of medicines in Europe.
content: Barclays reiterates its recommendation 'overweight' on Sanofi, but with an adjusted price target of 75 to 72 euros, pointing to a slowdown in sales of consumer health medicines in Europe since last August. 'The recent comments of a major European health retailer have reinforced investors' concerns about the consumer health category in Europe', stresses the broker. Using data from IQVIA and Pharmatrend, Barclays analyses the underlying trends and forecasts organic sales growth of 4.5% for Sanofi over the first three months of the current year.

title: Sanofi leaded by sales in Latin America, but maintains his confidence for 2024
content: Sanofi (-4.21% to 85.15 euros) held the last place of the CAC 40 after having achieved disappointing growth in the fourth quarter. The world health leader was penalized by the decline of 5.5% of its income in Latin America, to compare with a consensus of +1.1%. This region "remains affected by an atonous health market in South America and by the reset of pharmaceutical trade following the change in regulation", explained the company. The latter refers to governments buying abroad on behalf of their citizens. However, the governments of the region have taken measures against these practices. From the point of view of the divisions of the group, Sanofi Genzyme has disappointed by posting a growth of 0.7% with data comparable to 2.53 billion euros, knowing that the market was anticipating a growth of 3.5%. Between October and December, the sales of the health group increased by 5.2% in comparison to 9.51 billion euros against a consensus of 9.3%. Commenting on these results, Jefferies remains at "Sub-Performance" on Sanofi with a goal of prices maintained at 65 euros. As a result, Sanofi's growth in the health market is still at a normal level." Stifel also responded to the performance of the owner of the Pasteur brand. He explains that Sanofi's growth with comparable data (+6%) over the fourth quarter is below the consensus Visible Alpha (+8.5 %). Sanofi, the new world health leaderDespite its disappointing performance over the last three months of the year 2023, the signals are green for Sanofi. In a context of geopolitical tensions, inflationary pressures and stagnation of the health market in South America, its annual turnover increased by 8.1% in 34.15 billion euros. On comparable figures, it grew by 10.2%. The Sanofi Genzyme division grew by 3.2% with comparable data and by 2.5% with published data. It becomes world leader in specialised health thanks to its strong dynamics in both developed and emerging markets. Its operating result increased by 8.5% to 6.512 billion euros, increasing by 25 basis points. The net share of the group stands at 5,234 billion euros. In addition to its results, Sanofi has indicated that its board of directors will propose to the shareholders' general meeting on 23 April 2024 a dividend of 3.40 euros, an increase of 12%.

title: Sanofi: allocation of free shares, price target unchanged.
content: Oddo BHF maintains its 'neutral' rating on the Sanofi title, with an unchanged price target. The broker recalls that the Sanofi Board of Directors has decided to allocate shares free of charge, due to a new action for ten inmates. Detachment of the allocation right is scheduled for June 26 at the opening of the Stock Exchange and will be followed by a consequential adjustment of the price. Oddo BHF indicates that these allocation transactions are an annual ' milestone' of Sanofi's financial agenda, which confirms its value status 'market friendly'. 'We will adjust our figures (theoretical impact of 10%) accordingly, as of June 28th. Following this operation, our BPA estimates would mechanically emerge, for an estimate of 3.95 E for the financial year 2024/25 (vs. 4.35 E previously) and 4.55 E for 2025/26 (vs 5.05 E previously)', concludes the broker.

title: Sanofi closes 2023 with a stable turnover and an increasing dividend.
content: Sanofi's turnover for the financial year 2023 was 34.8 billion euros, stable at constant exchange rates and a decrease of 1.9% in published data. The group's operating income was 5.53 billion euros, compared with 5.51 billion euros in 2022. Ebitda fell by 4.3% to 7.35 billion euros at 31 December 2023, or 21.3% of turnover compared to 21.9% in 2022. Its net result was 4.25 billion euros, up 15.7%, or 12.2% of turnover 2023. Its cash flow was 18.45 billion euros, up 53.6 million euros in 2023. The board of directors will propose to the shareholders' general meeting on 28 May 2024 the payment of a dividend of 3.40 euros per share.

title: Airbus improves aviation safety in Kazakhstan with its advanced guidance system.
content: Airbus SE announces the modernisation of air traffic management at Astana and Almaty airports in Kazakhstan. In collaboration with its local partner Aerospace Engineering, the European company has in fact deployed its Advanced Surface Movement Guidance and Control System (A-SMGCS) at Astana Airport, Kazakhstan, after the success of a similar first implementation at Almaty Airport. Both systems offer air traffic controllers a solution that strengthens air traffic monitoring both on the ground and in the air. 'This module acts as a watchdog, alerting controllers quickly in case of potential conflicts between vehicles - be it aircraft or other types of vehicles - on runways and unauthorized intrusion of vehicles into restricted areas', stresses Airbus.

title: Airbus and Skyguide join forces to revolutionize air traffic management with open architectures
content: Airbus SE announces that it has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Skyguide to promote open architectures in air traffic management systems (ATMs), building on Airbus' Skywise platform. This collaboration will allow Airbus and Skyguide to work together on a common innovation effort focused on an open architecture for flexible solutions integrating third-party applications and components. Airbus recalls that its 'Skywise' platform is a unique solution that offers 'a modern and open architecture for air traffic management while ensuring that safety and security are at the highest level'.

title: Oddo BHF raises its price target for Airbus SE to 154 euros despite EBIT's forecast decline.
content: Oddo BHF reiterates its view of "overperformance" on Airbus SE with a price target increased by 154 euros, despite the downward adjustment of its short-term EBIT forecast for the aircraft group. The study office reduced its EBIT assumption adjusted on 2023 by 3.8%, mainly due to a higher than initially expected weight of the acceleration of recruitments aimed at ensuring the upcoming escalation of civilian activity of Airbus SE. However, Oddo BHF justifies its raising of price target by the effect of the largest generation of free cash flows, the integration of its new assumptions (risk premium and risk-free rates) and the revalorisation of peers.

title: Airbus signs a 7 ME contract with ESA to develop the Harmony mission's space radar.
content: Airbus SE announces the signing of a first 7-ME contract with the European Space Agency (ESA) to develop the synthetic aperture radar instrument (SAR) that will equip the two Earth observation satellites of the Harmony mission, ESA's 10th Earth Explorer programme. These satellites will contribute to the provision of a wide spectrum of unprecedented ocean, sea ice and land observation data. This interim contract is a first step towards the final contract covering the entire phase of the SAR radar. The launch of the two Harmony satellites on Ariane is planned by 2029.

title: Airbus and its partners are launching a global satellite surveillance system to improve aviation safety.
content: Airbus SE announces the signing of a cooperation agreement with Spire Global and European Satellite Services Provider (ESSP) to introduce a range of innovative global satellite monitoring services in the air traffic management (ATM) sector and in the aviation market in general. These services will be provided by a specialized constellation of more than 100 satellites that will collect messages from the automatic surveillance system (ADS-B) issued by aircraft and transmit data to the Earth in real time. Airbus will develop the ground air traffic management system and the surveillance infrastructure of services, while Spire Global will be responsible for the development of the space segment and ESSP will be responsible for the certification and provision of the service for air traffic monitoring purposes, ensuring 24-hour operations and supervision. The partners plan to certify, market and start the operation of the service by 2027.

title: Airbus fell by 10% after a downward revision of its financial and delivery perspective.
content: Following a downward revision of its financial and delivery outlook, Airbus SE (AIR)'s title fell from 10.06% to 132.82 euros, negatively influencing the CAC 40 index, which fell from 51.70 points to 7.655.19 points. This also affected other values in the sector, notably Safran (-3.54% to 198.70 euros), Thalès (-2.50% to 154.15 euros) and Dassault Aviation (-3.01% to 174.30 euros). The European aircraft manufacturer encountered difficulties in its aircraft deliveries and its space division, forcing it to lower its financial outlook by 2024. Investors are eagerly awaiting the inflation data to be published on Friday and the speeches of central bankers, particularly American ones, which could influence the economy and markets.

title: "Airbus pays an extraordinary dividend, but its conservative forecasts are holding back its title."
content: The AIRBUS SE title started Thursday's sitting in a slight downturn, the announcement of the payment of an extraordinary dividend after results of 2023 'solids' failed to compensate for forecasts deemed prudent. The adjusted EBIT - a key indicator measuring the business margin of the group - increased by 3% to reach €2.21 billion in the fourth quarter, a figure slightly lower than the consensus that was aimed at 2.27 billion. The turnover increased from 11% to 22.89 billion in the last three months of the fiscal year, while the market was aimed at €22.25 billion. At the operational level, our company said that it had delivered 735 commercial aircraft over the whole of fiscal year 2023, compared to 661 aircraft in 2022. We plan to offer our shareholders a dividend of €1.80 per share, plus an extraordinary dividend of one euro per title. 'Our dividend proposal reflects both our strong financial performance in 2023, our growth prospects and the solidity of our balance sheet', said Guillaume Faury, our general manager. For 2024, we have given 800 aircraft as our civilian target, and we have given it as our target for 2024, we have given it as our civil objective. that an Ebit adjusted between 6.50 and 7 billion euros, which proves to be less than the 7.20 billion consensus. On the Paris Stock Exchange, our title declined by about 0.2% on Thursday morning following this publication.

title: "Annual targets reduced due to supply problems"
content: We are now forced to revise our annual targets downwards due to continuing difficulties in the supply chain. We are now anticipating an adjusted EBIT of approximately EUR 5.5 billion and a cash flow before customer financing of about EUR 3.5 billion. We had previously anticipated an adjusted EBIT of between EUR 6.5 billion and 7 billion and a cash flow before customer financing of about EUR 4 billion. We will also have to record charges of about EUR 0.9 billion on certain telecommunications, navigation and space observation programmes in the first half of the year. These charges are mainly related to the updating of assumptions on schedules, workloads, sources of supply, risks and costs over the life of certain programmes in these fields. As regards our commercial aircraft activities, we are facing persistent specific difficulties in the supply chain, mainly with regard to engines, aerostructures and cabin equipment. As a result, we now plan to deliver about 770 commercial aircraft in 2024. the production rate, at 75 A320 aircraft per month, is now scheduled for 2027. The semi-annual results will be published on 30 July.

title: Airbus fell by 2.2% on the Paris Stock Exchange after disappointing results in the first quarter.
content: The AIRBUS SE (AIR) title drops by more than 2% on the Paris Stock Exchange on Friday morning, recording the sharpest drop in the CAC 40 index, after a start of the year considered disappointing by analysts.We evoke an operating context that shows no sign of improvement, characterized by persistent tensions both at geopolitical level and in the supply chain.Our consolidated adjusted Ebit, a measure of our commercial performance, stands at 577 million euros over the first three months of the year, compared with 773 million euros a year earlier.The turnover increases by 9% year-on-year to 12.8 billion euros on the basis of 142 deliveries of commercial aircraft, to be compared with 127 in the first quarter of 2023.Stifel's analysts describe "deciding" figures, while recalling that the first quarter of the group is still complicated.At Oddo BHF, we deplore a "pusssive" year start but also an "unequal" visibility with annual targets 2024 that have been strongly repeated.Over the whole of the exercise, we are determined to reach about 800 quantities. According to RBC, the Group's weak performance in the first quarter is likely to compromise the annual forecast, but the Canadian broker also says it is relying on a re-acceleration of activity in the second half of the year.As a result, our title fell by 2.2% Friday morning following this publication.

title: Airbus drops sharply, lowers its forecasts and worries the aviation sector.
content: After issuing a warning yesterday, Airbus SE (AIR) recorded the largest decline in CAC 40. The aircraft manufacturer now anticipates an adjusted EBIT of approximately EUR 5.5 billion and a cash flow before customer financing of about EUR 3.5 billion. Airbus previously anticipated an adjusted EBIT of between EUR 6.5 billion and 7 billion and a cash flow before customer financing of about EUR 4 billion. The aircraft manufacturer has lowered its 2024 target for aircraft deliveries. It now aims at 770 deliveries compared to 800 previously, to compare with a consensus of 786 and 735 aircraft delivered in 2023. "This reduction is linked to persistent problems in the supply chain, mainly with regard to engines, aerostructures and cabin equipment," explains Airbus. "This warning and the comments accompanying it weigh on the sector: Safran falls by 4.71% while Dassault Aviation loses 3.45%. According to the CEO of Airbus Guillaume Faury, the situation has deteriorated in particular in recent months. As a result, the target of 75 monthly deliveries of A320 family aircraft is now extended to 2027 instead of 2026. In addition, Airbus has warned that it would record charges of approximately €0.9 billion on certain telecommunications, navigation and space observation programs in the first half of the year. These are mainly related to the update of assumptions about schedules, workload, sources of supply, risks and costs over the life of certain programs in these areas. Deutsche Bank has lowered its Achat recommendation to Keep, while RBC believes that "many contrary winds in space and commercial activities are reflected in Airbus's action." Citigroup speaks of disappointment by reducing its profit forecasts for 2024-2027 from 12% to 19%. Berenberg believes that "the cycle of revision now seems to be downwards in the course of the change." While UBS expects an Ebit of 5.4 billion euros in 2024, Airbus's semi-annual results will be announced on 30 July.

title: Schneider Electric and renowned partner launch innovative automation solutions for cutting-edge manufacturing!
content: Schneider Electric, a leader in energy and automation solutions, has established a global technical partnership with a renowned industrial partner to provide innovative automation solutions for advanced manufacturing applications. Schneider Electric's automation skills, combined with its partner's state-of-the-art technology, have given rise to a new range of automation solutions installed in advanced production systems. They aim to deliver exceptional efficiency and productivity to the additive manufacturing, cutting, surface processing and intelligent production sectors.

title: Schneider Electric starts 2024 on wheel caps with 8.2% growth in the first quarter!
content: Schneider Electric SE has a turnover of 7.43 billion euros in the first quarter of 2024, up 8.2% in published data and 5.5% at constant exchange rates and scope. In organic, the energy management specialist claims growth of 4.2% for its Infrastructure division, driven by an increase of 12.5% in the Industries segment, and 6.5% for its Building division. With the gradual lifting of the few obstacles encountered at the end of 2023 and the rise in the new energy efficiency contracts, the group says it is a strong growth dynamic and confirms that it is aiming for organic growth of at least 9% by 2024.

title: Deutsche Bank maintains its target on Schneider Electric, waiting for results to rise in the first half of the year.
content: Deutsche Bank reiterates its recommendation 'maintaining' on Schneider Electric with a target of 240 to 242 euros, pending the results of the energy specialist in the first half of the year, expected on 31 July. The broker forecasts for this period an increase of 15%, an EBIT increase of 30% and a FCF of 2.2 billion euros, while the forecasts for the financial year 2024 should at least be reconfirmed. However, it warns that the current growth forecasts for the industry at 5 to 7% could be revised upwards, while the growth of the Building division, currently guided by around 10%, could be pushed upwards.

title: Schneider Electric and Schindler sign a historic contract to modernize critical energy infrastructure.
content: At Hannover Messe, Schneider Electric and the German company Switzerland Schindler signed for the first time a contract for the supply of energy management and automation systems for critical infrastructure in Germany. The total cumulative quantity is around 500 systems. Schneider Electric's solutions have been selected for the German transport and energy distribution infrastructure modernization programmes. They will provide accurate and reliable features to critical installations, even in the event of disruptions to the electricity grid, as well as frequency references to the various control stations. The signing of this contract also includes the transfer of the production of these energy management systems to Schneider Electric: Schneider Electric will produce the main components; Schindler will carry out their assembly and the necessary controls before delivering the systems to the German infrastructure.

title: Schneider Electric acquires Xpedition, AI leader for energy and automation, for 220 million euros.
content: Schneider Electric announced that it had entered into exclusive discussions to acquire 100% of Xpedition's capital, one of the leaders in artificial intelligence (AI) dedicated to the energy and automation industries, with an estimated business value of 220 million euros. Founded in 2016, Xpedition offers proven AI-based solutions to analyse energy data, measurement signals and monitoring data. Based in Europe, Xpedition achieved a turnover of 28 million euros in 2023 and employs 220 employees, including 140 engineers for the Research & Development division. This acquisition would represent a unique opportunity to add AI capabilities to its offer to the state of the art, while accelerating the roadmap for its digital transformation, in particular that applied to Industry 4.0. With its international presence, Schneider Electric would also support the development of Xpedition in the world, particularly in the Asian market.

title: Stifel reduces its expectations for Schneider Electric, but maintains its purchase recommendation.
content: Stifel reiterates its recommendation 'buy' on Schneider Electric while lowering its price target from 170 to 155 euros, reducing its estimates by 25% for 2024 and 11% for 2025 after an update of its model for the group.The broker explains that it wishes to 'reflect weaker dynamics than previously expected for Schneider Electric over the rest of the year, especially in energy solutions and automations'.Stifel points out, however, that with a P/E 2025 of the order of 15 times after this revision, the valuation is attractive and below the average of its peers. It remains to 'buy' on the file, 'seeing the case of unchanged long-term growth'.

title: "Schneider Electric predicts a profit in sharp decline, Siemens and ABB also affected"
content: The electricity industry's companies are falling back in Europe while Schneider Electric SE (SU) has indicated that its operating profit is expected to fall by 35% to €2800 million in the fourth quarter, according to preliminary data. The FactSet consensus was €3800 million. Revenues are expected to decrease from 4.9% to €67000 million. At the Paris Stock Exchange, Schneider Electric SE has lost 2.40%. In Frankfurt, its competitor Siemens is yielding 2.47% to €134.945. Another bad news in the sector, ABB has issued a warning on its sales due to the deterioration of the economic environment. In the third quarter, ended at the end of December, its revenues are now expected to fall by around 22% compared to the previous quarter, compared with a decrease of 15% to 20% earlier. Its financial results will be published on 1 February.

title: Schneider Electric: CA in decline, EBITDA margin at 15.6%
content: Schneider Electric SE recorded a turnover of 23.4 billion euros in the 4th quarter of 2023-2024, a decrease of 2% at constant scope and exchange rates compared to the 4th quarter of 2022-2023. The annual turnover is 85.6 billion euros, down 10% at constant scope and exchange rates, and in published data, in line with the latest indications given by the group. EBITDA's margin reached a level of 15.6%, also in line with the latest indications given by the group. The net result was 5.2 billion euros (compared with 6.3 billion euros in the previous year), i.e. a net margin of 6.1%. The objectives for the financial year 2024-2025 are confirmed: Schneider Electric SE anticipates stable turnover at constant scope and exchange rates and a margin of EBITDA around 16%.

title: Schneider Electric under pressure: surprise fall of title due to difficulties in renewable energy
content: SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC SE (SU) : Impacted by the difficulties of the renewable energy subsidiary, the prices of the electric equipment manufacturer opened a downside yesterday. Simple epidermal reaction or real background movement?

title: Schneider Electric reduces its revenue forecast due to the weakness of the electricity infrastructure market.
content: Schneider Electric SE (SU) issues a profit warning on the occasion of the publication of its quarterly revenues. The annual turnover is expected to decrease by about 10% at constant scope and exchange rates compared to the financial year 2022-2023, and the margin of EBITDA around 34%. The French specialisation group previously aimed at a decrease of about a percentage of "mid-single digit" (5%) and a margin of EBITDA to about 35% of the turnover. The ambition of a turnover of 30 billion euros, initially expected for fiscal year 2025-2026, is postponed by about one year. The manufacturer of energy management systems pointed out that "the weakness of the electricity infrastructure market continued to weigh on the activity Energies" in the third quarter, ended at the end of December, of the financial year 2023-2024. The turnover of this period reached 5.5 billion euros, declining by 12% at constant perimeter and exchange rates. It decreased by 13% in published data. "As indicated in our last publication of the electrical infrastructure market, the continued weakness of the market has reached 5.5 billion euros, declining by 12% at constant perimeter and exchange rates. "We expect to continue to face a difficult market dynamics for our energy products for a few more quarters," said Jean-Pascal Tricoire, Managing Director of Schneider Electric.

title: Air Liquide is launching a share buyback of 168.2 million euros.
content: Air Liquide announces the signing of a share buyback agreement with an investment services provider covering a volume of 905,000 shares, representing 0.17% of the Company's share capital as at 31 December 2023. The maximum unit purchase price will not exceed 250 euros. The initial buyback price, fixed at 185.86 euros per share, corresponds to the share price at the close of the stock exchange on the day before the conclusion of the contract, giving rise to a total initial purchase price of 168.2 million euros.

title: "Air Liquide gets 160 million euros for its emission reduction project in Dunkirk"
content: Air Liquide welcomes the decision of the European Commission to support the project CCUS (Carbon Capture, Use and Storage) which we are partnering with other industrial players and Dunkirk LNG, aimed at reducing emissions from the Dunkirk basin. This project will include a pipeline to transport CO2 from the capture sites and a terminal in the port of Dunkirk, in order to liquefy it and load it on ships. It would represent an investment estimated at more than 400 million euros. First CO2 infrastructure project in France to obtain the support of the European Union, our project would benefit from a grant of more than 160 million under the CEF-E financing programme in the event of the project being carried out.

title: Air Liquide crosses the course of the 3 GW renewable power in France!
