content: On the occasion of the commissioning of a 20 megawatt (MW) green hydrogen production unit in France, Air Liquide has announced that it will exceed the 3 gigawatts (GW) of installed renewable energy production capacity in the country. With more than 1,200 wind, solar, hydraulic and battery-storage plants, Air Liquide's renewable energy fleet meets the energy needs of the equivalent of 2.5 million people and avoids the release into the atmosphere of 1.2 million tonnes of CO2 each year. Air Liquide thus indicates that it is "among the leaders of renewable energy in France".

title: Air Liquide: Net profit up 11.6%, operating margin doubled, dividend up 8.5%!
content: Air Liquide (AI) publishes a net income share of the group increasing from 11.6% to 3.08 billion euros in 2023. The recurring net income is 3.32 billion euros, an increase of 13.3% excluding foreign exchange effects. Current operating income is 5.07 billion euros, an increase of 4.2%. With comparable data, it has increased by 11.4%. The operating margin has increased by 80 basis points excluding energy effect to 18.4%. We are ahead of our Advance plan with an increase of 150 basis points out of 2022-2023. At the mid-point of our Advance strategic plan, we have almost achieved our margin ambition to 2025, which was an increase of 160 basis points. We have benefited notably from the "efficiencys", which amount to 466 million euros in 2023, an increase of 23.2%. The turnover reaches 27.61 billion euros, an increase of 3.7% with comparable data. The gas & services activity, which represents 95% of revenues, is increasing from 4.2% to 26.36 billion euros. proud to point out that in two years we have almost achieved the ambition of increasing the margin that we had set ourselves by 2025 as part of our Strategic Advance Plan. As a result, we are announcing today doubling our initial ambition," said our Managing Director, François Jackow. "We are now aiming at an increase of 320 basis points in our operating margin. 170 base points of improvement are expected for the remaining two years of the plan. We are translating our confidence in the future with a dividend that will be submitted to shareholders' vote next April, amounting to €3.20 per share, an increase of 8.5%. A free share allocation is also planned in June 2024, due to a share allocation for 10 shares held. In 2024, we are confident in our ability to further increase our operating margin and achieve a growth in recurring net income, at constant exchange rates, said François Jackow about the outlook.

title: Air Liquide becomes world leader in green hydrogen with the acquisition of Greenstat in Norway
content: Air Liquide announces the acquisition of a majority stake in Greenstat ASA, a green energy operator based in Norway. This operation will strengthen Air Liquide's position as a world leader in the production of hydrogen and low-carbon energy. This company specializes in the production of green hydrogen and renewable electricity. In 2023, the company achieved a turnover of approximately 6.2 million euros. Since its creation, Greenstat has developed a portfolio mainly in the fields of hydrogen and solar, with a gross capacity of about 450 MW. François Jackow, Managing Director of Air Liquide, said: 'The acquisition of Greenstat is a logical step in the continuous development of Air Liquide in Europe and more particularly in Norway, as the world leader in green hydrogen and more globally of green energies'.

title: Air Liquide: The value drops by 3.3%, the hopes of rebound disappointed.
content: AIR LIQUID : The value of the gas and technology group has plunged by 3.3% yesterday. For 1 year, Air Liquide has been in the red, but has been trying to recover for 1 month. The courses are trying to build a dish and had hung a hope of rising, for the time being dissatisfied.

title: BlackRock takes foot in Air Liquide with 4.88% of the capital
content: BlackRock, acting on behalf of customers and funds, reported to the AMF that on June 21, it had crossed 5% of Air Liquide's capital and voting rights and held 4.88% of the capital and voting rights on behalf of the said customers and funds. The US asset management giant explained that this cut-off was the result of a sale of Air Liquide shares in the market and a decrease in the total number of shares held as collateral.

title: "Tension at the Air Liquide General Assembly: climate, human rights and controversy over the renewal of François Jackow"
content: Air Liquide is preparing for a new general assembly under tension, which the demonstrators of the climate cause would be well shaken Friday at the moment when the shareholders have to decide on the issues of the climate and the renewal of the CEO François Jackow. For the first time in a decade, the first French company of industrial gas and health, which is celebrating its 120th anniversary this year, will hold its annual Grand Mass at home, in its 30-storey headquarters in Paris-La Défense, rather than in a room in the middle of Paris. Objective: to avoid "immobilising a district of Paris", explains the group, as it had happened during a tumultuous 2023 edition, marked by heats between demonstrators and police officers on the outskirts of the room hosting the event. A year later, the pressure did not fall back. In the street or in front of the courts, the group remains under the fire of the critics of the climate, who accuse it of aggravating the warming of the planet and harming biodiversity and human rights, due to its activities in the gas and health. Organizations have explicitly called for AG to push it. Extinction Rebellion that requires "the abandonment" of flagship projects in India, Japan and China, and, the International Energy Agency's recommendation, "the cessation of any investment in new fossil projects". Between "300 and 600" protesters are expected, according to a police source. "A big device" is planned, the source, which expects as last year to confrontations between ecologists and shareholders. Faced with the "risk" of disturbances to public order, the Paris police commissioner has taken a decree prohibiting unreported demonstrations around the headquarters of Air Liquide. On the agenda, shareholders, present or online, will be called to vote on Air Liquide's climate strategy, some investors also demanding a more ambitious energy transition. Tuesday, 22% of Shell's shareholders have thus rejected its climate plan, a relatively high proportion. - Jackow looks to Singapore - Last year, at Air Liquide, a purely consultative resolution from militant shareholders had even won 30.4% of the votes. She asked the company to align its gas reduction targets. A coalition of shareholders claiming 0.9% of the capital claimed in vain, to the point of justice, a non-binding resolution "to end the cumulation of the functions of president and general manager" held by François Jackow, in order to keep him in the position of general manager alone. An unthinkable choice for the board of directors, attached to the "strategic stability" of the company, which raised €5.4 billion in profits in 2023, after €5.2 billion in 2022, and will propose to shareholders to renew the CEO for a fifth term. The boiling leader of the company for the last seven years will preside over this meeting on the basis of controversy after his statements on a possible relocation of the main listing of the Paris Group to the Singapore Stock Exchange. As to appease the criticisms, he mentioned on Thursday in the Figaro a "translation error": he did not want to talk about a main listing in Singapore but about a transformation into classic shares of the securities already traded in a form reserved for foreign companies. Jackow points out that Asians buy more shares than Europeans, more constrained by sustainable investment rules. As the CEO repeats, Air Liquide is "the industrial gas group most involved in the energy transition". One third of its investments is devoted to low carbon energy, 95% of which is renewable electricity. But in September, the think tank Carbon Tracker considered that "only" the Japanese company Asahi Glass had emission reduction targets "potentially" aligned with the Paris agreement. Air Liquide came second before Linde and Praxair, far ahead of the Indian Tata Steel or the American Air Products.

title: Air Liquide collapses: investors flee after disappointing results!
content: AIR LIQUIDE (AI) published disappointing quarterly results, which led to a massive sale of securities by investors at the opening of markets this morning. Is this reaction in line with a short-term logic or is it the beginning of a sustainable downward trend?

title: Air Liquide: historical results but disappointment due to comparison with Linde
content: The Air Liquide share (-2.64% to 145.71 euros) occupies the place of the red lantern of the CAC 40 index due to a disappointing performance, even if it shows historical results in 2023. The French group also suffers from the comparison with its competitor Linde, who revealed on Tuesday higher profits than expected. Air Liquide had benefited from it. UBS remains at the Achat on Air Liquide now its target price at 160 euros. Air Liquide's adjusted net income 2023 stands at 4.1 billion euros, down by 29%, or 8.20 euros per share (down by 27% over one year). The adjusted Ebitda 2023 fell by 38% to 12.2 billion euros. However, the gas group achieved a record annual net profit of 3.98 billion euros, an increase of 5%. In 2022, the company had recorded a net profit of 3.79 billion euros. This increase in the net result is due to "the growth of sales of industrial gas and health, particularly medical gas." In the same period, its adjusted Ebitda was EUR 2.83 billion, down by 10%. It is 1% lower than the Visible Alpha consensus of EUR 2.86 billion. UBS forecasted an Ebitda of EUR 3.1 billion. The fourth quarter gas production was EUR 10.3 million cubic metres per hour (Mm3/h), benefiting from an increase in industrial gas production of 5% over the quarter. In the context of a declining gas price, industrial and health gas sales are achieving a solid quarter, with an adjusted net operating income of EUR 1.43 billion and a cash flow of EUR 2.35 billion. In the year 2023, the company bought out EUR 3.5 billion of shares, of which EUR 1 billion was linked to the proceeds of the sale of US assets. It continued to reduce its net debt by 4.5 billion. The Board of Directors, meeting on 6 February 2024, decided to propose the distribution of a dividend in 2023 of €2.81 per share, an increase of 7.2%. In addition, "the Board of Directors confirms its policy of returning to the shareholder at more than 40% of the cash flow by 2024, which will combine an increase in dividend payments from 6.5% to €0.73 per share and stock buybacks for €1.5 billion in the first quarter, which will remain the basis of quarterly buybacks in the current environment." On the outlook, investment expenditure is expected to be in the upper half of the long-term range in 2024, at EUR 14-15 billion. This amount is higher than the estimate of UBS: EUR 13.9 billion and EUR 13.8 billion of consensus. Production is expected to be greater than 10.5 Mm3/h in the first quarter of 2024 (substantially lower than the estimate of EUR 13.9 billion and EUR 13.8 billion of consensus). 10.7 Mm3/h) and increase by 2% year-on-year for fiscal year 2024, after adjustment for the U.S. divestment (compared to +3% according to UBS).

title: EssilorLuxottica strengthens its presence in China with a majority stake.
content: EssilorLuxottica, a specialist in quality bezeling, announces that it has signed a letter of intent to acquire a majority stake in its distributor EssilorLuxottica in China. After successful acquisitions of distribution in the United States in 2020 and 2022 and in France in 2023, EssilorLuxottica SA wishes to extend its direct presence in this country of high potential in which the brand is present since 2004. This acquisition is expected to be effective in July 2024. After 20 years of presence in China, EssilorLuxottica SA has signed a letter of intent for a majority acquisition of 51% of the company Shanghai Optics Co., owner in China of the distribution of the brand EssilorLuxottica), which operates live 3 sales centres (1 in Beijing and 2 in Shanghai) and manages 23 sub-distribution centres in various medium-sized cities. In 2023, the overall volume of business carried out in China amounted to €23 million, including €8 million on the 3 sales centres located in Beijing and Shanghai. Shanghai Optics Co. is thus of a higher level than that of the United States/Canada zone (which is already the Group's first contributor to EBITDA with 27.1% EBITDA margin in 2023).

title: EssilorLuxottica: sales up 5.5% in the first quarter, despite negative impact of exchange rates
content: In the first quarter, EssilorLuxottica (EL) made sales of EUR 4.23 billion, up 5.5% in organic data and 4.1% in published data. The effect of exchange rates remains detrimental over the first three months with a negative impact of EUR 90 million, but this trend is expected to decrease over the months. The perimeter effect has a positive impact of EUR 1.4% or 59 million. The latter includes the consolidation of Persol, Oliver Peoples and Maui Jim. In the Western European region, the group recorded a decrease of 2.5% in sales at constant exchange rates and scope (- 1.3% in published data). This decline is mainly due to an unfavourable base effect resulting from significant loyalty programmes in the first quarter of 2023. Corresponding to this impact, current activity is very slightly positive (+ 0.5% at constant exchange rates and scope), in a market of vision and mode in progressive recovery, supported by online sales. In the other EMEA countries, sales rose sharply (+30.1% at constant exchange rates and scope), in dynamic markets in almost all countries. The increase in sales was reduced to 17.3% in published data mainly due to the depreciation of the Turkish pound, the Russian ruble and the Egyptian pound against the euro. The operational profit of activity (ROPA) of the group rose in the first quarter of 2024 to 243 million euros (vs. 193 million euros in the first quarter of 2023, +25.9%), including a negative currency effect of 51 million euros. The operating margin thus amounts to 5.6% against 4.7% a year earlier. On the basis of this robust publication, EssilorLuxottica confirms its ambitions for the year 2024, announced when the annual results were published last February. The company expects a gradual recovery over the year in the emerging countries and a continuation of good dynamics in the developed countries. of the growth of its activity Retail on a high historical and expected operating margin close to 12%.

title: EssilorLuxottica strengthens its presence in eye health with the acquisition of 120 million euros.
content: EssilorLuxottica announces on Thursday evening that it has finalized the acquisition of Vista Optics, a French company specializing in the manufacture of high-quality lenses and optical equipment. In a press release, the group of glasses and optics specifies that the operation is part of its strategy of becoming a reference player in the markets of eye health. Created at the beginning of the 20th century, Vista Optics produces 'premium' lenses and state-of-the-art optical equipment, notably under the brands Vista and Zenith. Present in more than 50 countries around the world, the company has an annual turnover of 120 million euros for a workforce of 500 employees. EssilorLuxottica explains that Vista Optics will now be able to rely on the strength of its group, both in France and internationally, in order to accelerate its development.

title: EssilorLuxottica set a new turnover record in 2023!
content: ESSILORLUXOTTICA announces for its financial year 2023 a new record of turnover, growing from 5.1% (+6.1% at constant exchange rates) to 4.29 billion euros, in line with its annual target of a CA of more than 4.25 billion euros. The world vision specialist specifies that this performance was based on a very good fourth quarter (+7% at 1.09 billion euros, +9.2% at constant exchange rates), marked by a sustained sales dynamic across all geographical areas. With this good level of activity and a portfolio of orders at 31 December 2023 at a solid level of 1.37 billion euros, ESSILORLUXOTTICA confirms its objective of an EBITDA growing for the year 2023.

title: ESSILORLUXOTTICA joins GANT in launching global fashion glasses.
content: ESSILORLUXOTTICA and GANT announce the signing of an exclusive licence agreement for the design, production and worldwide distribution of GANT (Swedish fashion group) glasses. This agreement, which takes effect immediately, will continue until 31 December 2029, with an automatic renewal option for an additional five years. The first collection resulting from this agreement will be launched in the first quarter of 2025.

title: De Rigo Invest withdraws from EssilorLuxottica, sells 5.15% of the capital for 543 million euros.
content: ESSILORLUXOTTICA (-4.12% to 154.50 euros) recorded the second largest decline in the SBF 120 index, while De Rigo Invest sold a little more than 3,567 million securities at the price of 152 euros per share. The specialist in optics and ophthalmic equipment had closed yesterday to 160,80 euros. The holding which represents the interests of the De Rigo family has sold the balance of its shareholding, i.e. about 5.15% of the company's capital, for 543 million euros. After an initial investment in 2009, De Rigo Invest had already sold 1.5% of the capital of the specialist in optics and ophthalmic equipment in October 2020.

title: EssilorLuxottica: Revenues in the dark, sales down 7.9% in the 4th quarter.
content: ESSILORLUXOTTICA's sales decreased from 9.3% to almost 10.7 billion euros in the year 2023, with sales falling from 7.9% to 2.55 billion euros in the fourth quarter alone, in an environment that is still difficult. During the fourth quarter, demand for eye care products remained sluggish in all regions, in a context of fluctuating inflation and consumer confidence in the dark. The group reinforced the implementation of its strategic plan by focusing on the omnichannel customer experience, and continued its proactive management of the store portfolio with nine net closures and five transfers to affiliate.

title: EssilorLuxottica dives by more than 1% after a recommendation degradation of Stifel!
content: In contrast to the trend on the stock market, our company EssilorLuxottica is falling by more than 1% due to a deterioration in the recommendation of Stifel, which changes from 'purchase' to 'preserv', with a price target adjusted to 201-203 euros. Although the broker acknowledges that our Franco-Italian group has once again demonstrated its defensive status in its latest publication, we are faced with some caution regarding our management's objectives in terms of operating margin by 2026.

title: EssilorLuxottica: sales in the 1st quarter in line with expectations, but price target down 11%
content: The analyst estimates that the sales growth of ESSILORLUXOTTICA in the first quarter is broadly in line with expectations, currencies and the Americas region could be at the centre of the concerns. UBS reiterates its neutral advice on value with a price target of 134 euros, which represents a potential decrease of -11%. The group's total turnover in the first quarter, of 5,542 million euros, lower by 1.2% than the consensus (in line with the UBS estimate), with a growth in the first quarter of +4.2% (compared to +4.5% for consensus and +4.2% for UBS). In the first quarter, the activity Optical Network is up by +3.8% (compared with +4.4%) indicates UBS.

title: EssilorLuxottica: downward revision of growth for 2024 and 2025
content: We are lowering our expectations for EssilorLuxottica. We are maintaining our price target at 145 euros, but we are lowering our growth forecasts for 2024 and 2025. We are counting on a y/y tcc growth of +4.5% for the fourth quarter, slightly lower than our previous estimate of +5%. For 2024, we are forecasting a growth of +4.2% to tcc, slightly below our previous expectations, due to a deteriorating macroeconomic environment in Europe and the United States. We are also revising our forecast for 2023 downwards, due to an EBIT margin that is expected to suffer from negative exchange rate effects and cost inflation. We believe that this revision could weigh in the short term, but we believe that its more defensive profile in the current context could help to reduce the discount.

title: "Our ambition is to become the number one leader of payment processors in France and among the top three in Europe!"
content: Through our strategic partnership project, we are committed to taking a new major step in payments by providing us with the best payment processing technology for our customers and business partners. This processor will be dedicated to processing all card payments in Europe, i.e. 17 billion transactions, and will also be able to address other financial institutions. We aim for the status of number one leader of processors in France and aim to be among the top three processors in Europe. This partnership is accompanied by an exclusive agreement on the creation of a target business model in the form of a joint venture, which will strengthen our position on the payment market.

title: BNP Paribas: Target of 58 euros, expected results up 20% in 2023.
content: BNP Paribas retains its higher valuation status with a slightly raised price target, from €55.8 to €58. While the banking group will publish its net profit (RN) on February 8, the analyst anticipates revenues of 10,521 ME in the fourth quarter, up 5.2% in one year and slightly higher than the consensus (10,321 ME). 'We expect an RN of 8,209 ME (+20.1%) in 2023, the group reaching the goal of its strategic plan of an RN > 8 MdE in 2024', adds the broker. According to the analyst, BNP Paribas SA will continue to benefit from favourable business trends in the medium to long term, particularly in asset management, and its organic growth will continue to be reinforced by targeted external growth operations.

title: BNP Paribas and EIB are strengthening their green alliance to support the ecological transition of local businesses and communities.
content: BNP Paribas strengthens its collaboration with the European Investment Bank Group in 2024 with joint investment projects in strategic areas. The two partners thus confirm their willingness to support the ecological transition of French and European SMEs and ETIs as well as the public sector in France from 2024 onwards, after having already financed more than 1.6 MdE in 2023. This dynamic of cooperation between BNP Paribas and the EIB Group allows to identify several avenues of collaboration, notably in the fields of energy transition, education and public health, or bioeconomy. This partnership is in full coherence with the commitments made in the framework of the BNP Paribas Social and Environmental Responsibility (SR&E) Project, in particular for the support of our clients companies and individuals in their energy transition approach, explains Jean-Laurent Bonnafé, Managing Director of BNP Paribas.

title: BNP Paribas escapes the Helvet Immo case with a cost of 400 to 600 million euros!
content: BNP Paribas recorded a good performance during this first session of the year, with an increase from €0.67 to €63.01 in the Paris square. According to the "Parisien", the bank proposed a friendly agreement to the consumer association CLCV, which could cost it between €400 and €600 million. After being convicted on appeal of misleading and concealing commercial practices, for concealing the risks of its loan in Swiss francs Helvet Immo, the bank took steps to solve this case and to continue its growth.

title: BNP Paribas becomes Deutsche Bank's favourite bank!
content: BNP Paribas has become Deutsche Bank's favourite French bank, according to a sectoral note published today. It is ahead of Crédit Agricole and Société Générale. At the Paris Stock Exchange, BNP Paribas gains 1%, Crédit Agricole 0.24%, while Société Générale is up 0.33%. Deutsche Bank has noted its recommendation to Keep to Buy and increased its target price from 74 euros to 78 euros. "Historically, BNP Paribas has outperformed its performance in a low-rate and steady economic growth environment thanks to its good diversification, its better resilience and its lower volatility," notes the study office.The recent cyclical trends of the market, the imminent declines in rates and the more favourable economic outlook have further reinforced the analyst's belief that BNP Paribas should achieve its 2025 objectives. Finally, the comfortable position in terms of capital and the distribution plans to shareholders reinforce Achat's recommendation.

title: BNP Paribas slams the door of the fourth quarter in a 50% drop!
content: We inform you that BNP Paribas has published its fourth quarter accounts, which are below expectations. Between October and December, the net result, the group's share, fell from 50% to 1,069 billion euros, below the consensus of 1,74 billion euros. This result was penalized by the extraordinary negative elements, including extraordinary provisions for disputes related in particular to our mortgage portfolios in Poland and Personal Finance. Provisions on mortgage loans in Poland amounted to 320 million euros and provisions for litigation related to Personal Finance, to 221 million euros. Gross operating income fell from 8.8% to 3.11 billion euros, while the cost of risk increased from 39.4% to -972 million euros. At the same time, the net banking income increased from 0.1% to 10.9 billion euros, with management costs rising by 4.2%. We announced a dividend 2023 of 4.60 euros in cash, increasing by 18%, as well as a share buyback programme of 1,05 billion euros. BNP Paribas closed in 2024 billion euros. 23 with a hard equity ratio (CET1) of 13.2%, up 90 basis points over the year. Finally, we would point out that, due to different decisions of public authorities, profitability on tangible equity (ROTE) up to 2025 is expected to rise between 11.5% and 12%. It was expected to rise to 12%. However, we are confident in our ability to issue a ROTE of 12% by 2026.

title: BNP Paribas: recommendation lowered, target of 50 euros, the rise will take time
content: BNP Paribas has seen its Achat recommendation lowered to Keep and its price target from 55 euros to 50 euros as part of a sectoral study. The study office justifies its decision by its weak conviction that progress in the non-essential asset disposal plan has accelerated. On the operational level, it could also take 15 to 18 months to make significant progress in the recovery plans. For the analyst, the discount of the action alone is not sufficient to justify a favourable position.

title: BNP Paribas falls by 5.5% on the stock exchange after results below expectations.
content: Among the largest declines in the CAC 40 at the beginning of the session, BNP Paribas (-5.51% to 45.12 euros) switched in the red at the middle of the session due to concerns about the net margin of interest in France this year. At the conference call with analysts, the financial director, Lars Machenil, said: "Our forecast was a rebound or the level of 2022. Given the first quarter, (...) we are today at the bottom of the range of this forecast and our projections". At the purchase on the value, UBS recalled in a note published this morning that the recovery of income in the retail bank in France was a key factor for the growth of profits. Between January and March, BNP Paribas recorded a net result in decline of 20.5% to 750 million euros and a net banking product (PNB), the equivalent of the turnover, in decline from 0.6% to 7.35 billion euros. It recorded a decrease of 5.2% at constant scope and exchange. The consensus was 510 million for the result and 7.15 billion euros for the income. At the same time, management costs fell from 1.3% (-5.9% in comparable terms) to 5.22 billion.The financing and investment bank stands outUBS points out that the bank on Rue Bergère has revealed revenues that are 3.5% higher than expectations and costs that are 0.5% lower, allowing it to display a profit before provisions that is 19% higher than the consensus.The cost of the risk has also come under expectations, even though it has increased in a year from - 150 million euros to - 350 million euros.The analysts attribute the overperformance of BNP Paribas to its division Banque de Grande Clientèle and Solutions Investisseurs. If its revenues have decreased from 4.2% to 2.8 billion euros due to an unfavourable base effect, its net result, part of the group, has increased from 24.5% to 720 million euros. Within this division, the brokerage shares - a historical franchise of the bank - has distinguished itself. Its revenues have increased from 2.5% to 900 million euros, clearly exceeding the consensus of 860 million euros. BNP Paribas has highlighted a "strong commercial dynamics in derivatives". In the case of other activities, UBS finds it disappointing that the income and profit adjusted before the retail bank's provisions in France and insurance. The income of the Retail Banking in France, Private Banking and Insurance Division fell from 3.8% to 2.2 billion." The net margin of interest continued to be affected in the first quarter by the impact of short-term hedges (about -0,2 billion euros) and by the transfer of a portion of sight deposits to financial savings and paid deposits, the proportion of which continues to grow ", said the bank. It fell from 3.1% to 850 million euros.The retail bank in France and the private bank suffered a loss of 70 million euros this quarter, according to BNP Paribas' presentation. The consensus was -10 million euros. Jefferies points out the higher than expected increase in the cost of the division's risk to 290 million euros, "a subject which, according to him, will raise questions from investors." UBS considers that the strengthening of this ratio - which would make it possible to offer stock buybacks with powerful effects, given a valuation of 0.45 times the book value - is the main driver of an expansion of the stock take-up multiples.This year, the bank aims to achieve income growth of 5% or more, an operating factor of less than 70%, a net risk cost of 25 and 30 basis points, a profitability (ROTE) of more than 7% and a ratio of CET1 to about 14%.

title: BNP Paribas closes its share buyback program for €322 million.
content: On 26 January, BNP Paribas announced that it had completed its share buyback programme, which began on 6 October: 26,835,641 shares were purchased via an independent investment services provider for a total price of 322.122,626 euros. As a reminder, this transaction aims to offset the dilutive effect of the 2023 capital increase reserved for employees, the shares purchased being cancelled. The impact of the transaction on BNP Paribas' CET1 ratio is -8 basis points.

title: BNP Paribas disappoints to the T4, net profit in fall of 50% over the year
content: We must inform our shareholders that BNP Paribas experienced a disappointing performance in the fourth quarter, which led to a decrease of 8.35% in its price to €57.32 and a relegation to the penultimate place of the CAC index 40. Our results, which precede those of our competitors, in particular Société Générale and Crédit Agricole, which will publish their results on February 8, show a 50% drop in our net result, share of the group, to €1.069 billion, well below market expectations to €1.74 billion. However, over the whole of 2023, our net profit increased by 11.4% to a record level of €10.98 billion. However, the extraordinary negative elements have weighed on our results. We have been penalized by 645 million euros of net charges for "risk on financial instruments". These are broken down into 320 million euros of provisions on mortgage loans in Poland, 221 million in provisions for litigation related to Personal Finance and 104 million in provisions for risk on debt. The net banking product has increased from 0.1% to 10.9 billion euros, but with charges of €10.9 billion. We will pay a 2023 dividend of 4.60 euros in cash, up 18%, and a share buyback program of 1.05 billion euros in 2024. We have finished 2023 with a ratio of hard equity (CET1) of 13.2%, up 90 basis points over the year. However, due to different decisions of public authorities, we are forced to reduce our profitability of tangible equity (ROTE) by 2025, which is expected to rise between 11.5% and 12%, compared with 12% originally expected. However, we are convinced to issue a 12% Rota by 2026. Our net income share of the group is expected to increase by about 8% annually between 2022 and 2025, compared to at least 9% before. Our targets of a distribution rate of 60%, of which 50% in cash, an average growth of at least 12% of earnings per share and a 12% CET ratio have been confirmed.

title: Kering gets up: 900E, the next upward goal?
content: While analysts are overbidding in terms of price targets, Kering sets the stock records, and the price is rising beyond 850E, and even 860E. After a last oscillation between 690 and 790, the next upward target is clearly around 900E. A RIP of 30 to 35 times the profits (and why not soon 40 to 900E) is no longer a brake for a long time.

title: Kering breaks the record, the title takes over the 550E!
content: Still in the 'top-5' of the managers' favourite values, Kering accompanies the race to the CAC40 records and the title has a new absolute record at 550E. After a last oscillation between 420 and 490, the main upward goal of the 520E is largely overwhelmed, with a RP of 35 that has not been a brake for a long time... the managers now hope to see Kering test the 580E.

title: Kering acquires La Fabrique 1784, jewel of French high jewellery!
content: Kering announces the acquisition of Sedeo, owner of La Fabrique 1784, a prestigious French manufacturer dedicated to the manufacture of art objects and high jewellery. No financial details have been communicated regarding this transaction. "This acquisition of a jewel of high jewellery is a new proof of the will of the Kering group to preserve and grow historical know-how, in order to guarantee their transmission and development", explains the luxury group. La Fabrique 1784 has made prestigious and creative partnerships with many luxury houses or jewellery, in order to imagine exceptional pieces. Several projects have been created with Maisons du Groupe Kering, among which Yves Saint Laurent, who presented a jewel in the shape of the Eiffel Tower, during the reopening of its historic boutique of the rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré in Paris in 2022. Maison Bottega Veneta has also called on the know-how of the artisans of La Fabrique 1784 to create a unique object of art, an emblem regularly used by the Maison.

title: Oddo BHF raises its goal for Kering to 740 euros after exceptional 2023 results.
content: Oddo BHF maintains its 'neutral' rating on the Kering title with a price target raised from 640 to 740 euros to take account of the increase in estimates and more favourable market parameters. Kering published a CA 2023 of 15,428 ME in annual growth of 14.2% y/y with a T4 to + 15.9% y/y tcc that shows an acceleration after the +13.5% observed in the T3. EBIT 2023 stands at 6,350 ME (margin 41.3% against 40.2% in 2022). 'Our CA/EBIT 2023 forecast was 15,250 ME / 6,200 ME', reports the analyst. In this context, Oddo BHF indicates that it has increased its organic growth forecast from 9.5% to 11.2% for 2024. The 2025 growth is still expected to be +9.5% with an EBIT margin also adjusted upwards from 40.5% to 40.7%. 'This leads us to raise our EBIT forecasts by +4.5% on 2024 and +3.5% on 2025', stresses the broker.

title: Kering jumps to 760E, resistance of 790E in line of sight!
content: Kering progressed by +4% in 48H (+1.6% this June 6) and was close to 760E, to some fractions of the floor of the 763E of May 2. The title could shortly fill the 'gap' of the 767E of May 21, before tackling the resistance of the 790E that dates back to May 10.

title: Oddo BHF downwards revises its estimates for Kering, weighed by exchange and costs.
content: Oddo BHF maintains its 'neutral' rating on the Kering title with a target of 620 euros. If Oddo BHF still relies on organic growth close to 8% for the group in 2024, it reports a decrease of 4% in its estimates EBIT 2024 and 2025, considering that 'change and cost increases should weigh'. 'We still think that the group is not completely immune to the slowdown that is affecting the luxury industry', stresses the broker who says that its new forecasts are now more than 7% under the Visible Alpha consensus.

title: Barclays reduces his expectations on Kering, but maintains his confidence in luxury.
content: On Wednesday, Barclays lowered its price target on the Kering title from 640 to 620 euros, while maintaining its 'overweight' recommendation on the title. In a research note, the analyst states that he has revised downward his expectations of results on the luxury home, that he does not consider immune against the slowing of demand, in the face of difficulties in China and an unfavourable base effect. Despite this difficult environment for the luxury sector, however, the intermediary expects Kering to continue to outperform the market, even though it expects organic growth of 9% in the second quarter, compared to +14% in the second quarter.

title: Kering: reduced price target to 400 euros, still blurred visibility for Yves Saint Laurent.
content: We need to inform our shareholders that we have lowered our price target on the Kering title from €440 to €400. We regret the lack of visibility around the recovery of our flagship brand, Yves Saint Laurent. The arrival of a new creative director more attached to the brand's tradition should bear fruit, as well as the appointment of a new general manager and the increase in marketing expenses. We are thus integrating the scenario of a return to double-digit growth for the Yves Saint Laurent claw in the 4th quarter, while recognizing that we lack conviction about this hypothesis. We recognise that the valorisation of the Kering title may seem attractive at current levels, with a PER 2024 of 17.5x, a discount of 29% compared to its comparables in the luxury sector, but we do not perceive any catalyst in the immediate future. Our advice therefore remains to be 'conserved'.

title: Deutsche Bank adjusts its target on Kering, but maintains its purchase recommendation)
content: Deutsche Bank maintains its 'purchase' recommendation on KERING, while lowering its price target from €460 to €450, following an adjusted BPA 2024 forecast of -3% to €16.9 and small changes made in subsequent years. KERING offers a potentially convincing recovery story, with an ongoing reinvention of the global mega-brand Yves Saint Laurent, combined with an expansion in a number of adjacent categories. However, considering that the external environment is not favourable with a limited recovery in the United States and a new weakness in China, Deutsche Bank adopts a more cautious outlook for Yves Saint Laurent in the second half of this year.

title: Kering collapses: -16.6% in 5 days, -70% in 1 year!
content: KERING : Strong decline in 5 sessions for the luxury group, which has slid by 16.6%. A dip in a context of profitability diminished due to inflation, and despite a record turnover. Over 1 year, the prices of Gucci's mother house, Yves Saint Laurent, Bottega Veneta and others have dropped by 70%. The state of weakness observed since last summer persists, with the chain of hollows and descending peaks. The downward dynamics is installed.

title: "VINCI: UBS sees a potential increase of 42%, is the title underestimated?"
content: VINCI jumped by more than 1% today in Paris while UBS indicated this morning that the title was 'too cheap to be ignored'. The analyst thus confirmed his advice on the purchase of the title and raised its target price to 104 E (instead of 98 E) which represents a potential increase of 42%. VINCI continues to appear very cheap with a potential increase of 13% of TRI and 48% of TSR: are the progress made in international expansion and diversification underestimated ? indicates UBS. The Group expects in 2024 a current operating result in the Contracts, driven notably by a further increase in the operating margin of VINCI Energies. In the Concessions, the new tax on long-distance transport infrastructure will have a significant impact on the results. Overall, the net result share of the Group could be of the same order as in 2023 indicates VINCI's management.

title: VINCI fails to obtain €1.6 billion but can still claim compensation.
content: The VINCI Group (DG) takes note of the decision of the Nantes Administrative Court in the context of the airport project at Notre-Dame-des-Landes. The Court rejected the group's request for the conviction for fault of the State to pay it 1.6 billion euros, considering that the State has not committed any fault by abandoning, on grounds of general interest, the airport project at Notre-Dame-des-Landes. However, the VINCI Group (DG) will be entitled to compensation for the cancellation and the loss of profits. The amount of this compensation will be determined at a later date, the court having to take into account the gains obtained to the concession company by their possible designation as new concessionaires at the present Nantes Airport. The VINCI Group (DG) will continue to defend its interests in this case, considering that the termination of the concession is justified on grounds of general interest. We are confident in the continuation of the procedure, which may take several years, and are determined to defend the interests of our group and our shareholders.

title: VINCI wins €200 million contract to electrify Senegal by 2026
content: VINCI announces that it has signed a €200 million contract with the Société nationale d'électricité du Senegal (Senelec) for the construction of a set of electricity transmission and distribution infrastructures, lasting three years, which include the deployment of 1350 km of overhead and underground power transmission lines in high and very high voltage, as well as the construction of eight high voltage transformer stations. The project, whose funding is supported by the French authorities, aims to strengthen Senegal's energy capacity in a sustainable way by 2026 and to strengthen universal access to electricity.

title: VINCI and Google Cloud join forces to boost artificial intelligence!
content: VINCI and Google Cloud announce a strategic partnership to develop and implement VINCI's Artificial Intelligence (IA) strategy, in order to increase efficiency and simplify the work of the teams of the group of concessions and mobility services. The partnership includes the development of a technology platform internal to the company based on a set of Google Cloud solutions, such as the Gemini, Vertex AI, BigQuery and Apigee models. Furthermore, VINCI's IT platform will benefit from the reduction of the carbon footprint of Google Cloud's infrastructure. Finally, the collaboration also includes the creation of a 'data and IA' curriculum within the VINCI Institute for Talent.

title: VINCI rebounded: +8.7 per cent in construction sites and concessions in May.
content: In May, all the construction sites and concessions of the VINCI network saw their activity increase by 8.7% compared to last year. Overall, the level of activity is higher than that of the previous health crisis. However, activity is showing a decrease of 0.4% for the VINCI motorway concessions in May over a year.

title: VINCI: Turnover fell by 12.5% on 1st 2024, but profitability maintained.
content: Press release - Rueil-Malmaison, 7 May 2024 - 17:45 FIGURES OF BUSINESS OF THE 1st TRIMESTER 2024 : 5.23 MILLIARDS D'EURO The production of the 1st quarter is in line with the VINCI Group's 2024 objectives. For the first quarter of the current year, the VINCI Group has a turnover of 5,23 billion euros, down by 12,5% (at constant scope) compared to the same period of last year, which constitutes a high basis of comparison. By activity clusters, the production of the first three months of the financial year 2024 is broken down as follows: * The Construction activity has a turnover of 3,82 billion euros, in line with the Group's forecasts for the first quarter. This decrease in production, which will gradually increase over the year, is the result of a significant decrease in commercial activity in the last 18 months in the context of a severe crisis in the real estate sector. * The Energy activity will achieve a turnover of 0.6 billion euros. The turnover of the "Tertiary" activity is EUR 0.45 billion. * The production of the "Infrastructure" activity is EUR 0.32 billion. Quarterly orders reflect a still difficult market context. Orders made at the end of March 2024 reflect, as expected, a market which remains difficult for all the players in the sector, although there are signs of a slight improvement for the coming months (first fall in interest rates, relaxation of the conditions for granting loans by banking networks, stabilisation of prices). Maintaining a good operating profitability in 2024. For fiscal year 2024, taking into account the start-up at the end of April, VINCI anticipates a decrease in its output from 20 to 25% compared to 2023. Measures to adjust the cost structure to market conditions already largely in place in 2023 are continuing. The Group is expected to maintain a satisfactory operating profitability of between 2% and 3% of its annual turnover. Next press release: 1st half 2024, August 8, 2024 after the stock exchange. About the Group Since 1899, VINCI has built a strong reputation worldwide, by developing construction, energy, infrastructure, transport and urban services activities, as well as a financial services subsidiary. The Group now employs more than 220,000 people in more than 100 countries. VINCI has been working for the Group since 1899.

title: VINCI: Half-mast traffic on motorways and airports in May 2024
content: VINCI (DG) announces that traffic on its motorway concessions has decreased by 0.4% in May 2024 in comparison with the year, with a decrease of 0.3% for light vehicles and a decrease of 0.9% for heavy vehicles. With regard to its airport concessions, VINCI (DG) regrets to note a stagnation in passenger traffic and a moderate increase of 2.5% in commercial movements (ATM) between May 2023 and May 2024.

title: DG Highways: 6.7% reduction in traffic in January 2024, but airports are up 11.8%
content: We regret to inform our shareholders that DG registered a decrease in traffic of 6.7% in January 2024 compared to January 2023. Traffic on the networks of DG Autoroutes was greatly disrupted in January 2024 due to the numerous blockages of farmers during the last 10 days of the month. On the other hand, passenger traffic at the airports of the DG network recorded an increase of 11.8% in January 2024 compared to January 2023, in the continuity of the good trends of the 4th quarter 2023. In particular, traffic in Portugal and Mexico remains very strong and has continued to recover in Japan. Overall, traffic reaches 98% of its pre-crisis level.

title: VINCI submits its safeguard plan to the Tribunal de commerce de Paris.
content: Update on the accelerated safeguard procedure Deposit of the draft safeguard plan at the Tribunal de Commerce The VINCI Group announced on 19 April the opening, at its request, of an accelerated safeguard procedure on 17 April 2024, by the Tribunal de Commerce de Paris with the aim of guaranteeing its operational continuity while preserving the best interests of its trading partners, its employees and all its customers. At the hearing on 11 June, the Tribunal de Commerce de Paris confirmed the extension of the observation period until 17 August, 4 months after the opening of the procedure, in accordance with what had previously been communicated. During the first phase of this observation period, the Group continued its activities and finalized, in collaboration with the administrators of the courts, its draft safeguard plan which was deposited on 19 June 2024 at the Tribunal de Commerce de Paris. Draft safeguard plan This draft safeguard plan provides, as previously announced, for a resizing and restructuring of the group around its activities with a higher added value. The main measures concern: --> in Concessions, a refocusing on the most profitable commercial development activities, the restructuring of the company around its activities with a higher value added value. the other regions and the finalisation of the works under construction on these areas concerned; --> in Energy, the completion of the ongoing operations, the divestiture or abandonment of other operations in the portfolio before the activity ceases; --> a concentration of efforts on the development of the industrial activity "Renewable Energy", the profit centre of the group comprising all the networks of concessionaires and with, in particular, the launch of the energy ranges, the extension of existing housing and the professional premises. This draft safeguard plan takes account of developments in the energy market, still in sharp decline by -41% at the end of April over 12 slippery months and the new forecasts of activity, results and free cash flow of the group over the financial years 2024 and 2025. Indeed, the group was mechanically penalized by the market context, but also by the current procedure and the suspension of certain delivery guarantees on the activity Concessions (including networks) on the beginning of the year 2024 which have impacted the order book. In the context of the accelerated safeguard procedure, the independent expert, appointed by the Paris Commercial Court, confirmed that the proposed accelerated safeguard plan is of best interest to all classes of affected parties. • 19 June 2024: deposit of the draft safeguard plan and convening of the classes of affected parties to vote on the safeguard plan • 5 July 2024: vote of the classes of affected parties on the draft safeguard plan • from 17 July 2024: hearing to examine the draft plan with a view to its adoption or rejection • 17 August 2024 (at the latest): end of the period of observation of the accelerated safeguard procedure. VINCI will communicate at the end of each stage of this calendar. At the end of May 2024, the group's cash flow amounted to €10.5 million and debts amounted to €23.7 million (including €4.1 million related to IFRS 16 debt), i.e. a net debt (excluding IFRS 16) of €13.6 million. During this transition period, all VINCI's teams are mobilizing to continue the group's activities, finalize the ongoing work projects and work to preserve business relations with all the group's partners. VINCI wishes once again to thank its customers, trading partners, collaborators and shareholders for their continued support and confidence.

title: VINCI: sales are falling, but operating profitability is maintained.
content: In the first quarter of the current year, VINCI (DG) recorded a turnover of 221.8 ME down 13.2% (at constant scope) compared to the same period last year, which was a high basis of comparison. In detail, the activity decreased by 14% in concessions, by -1.5% in road works, by -50% in urban developments and by +11.4% in energy. For the financial year 2024, VINCI (DG) anticipates a decrease in its production of the order of 25 to 30% compared to 2023. The group believes that it should maintain a 'satisfactory' operating profitability, between 3% and 4% of the annual turnover. 'With a strong base and a very healthy financial structure, VINCI (DG) is confident in its ability to seize the opportunities that will arise and to bounce back as soon as market conditions become more favourable', adds the firm.

title: AXA and Worldline create a giant of business insurance services in France
content: AXA and Worldline announce the creation of their joint venture in the field of insurance services for companies in France. Both groups received unconditional authorisation from the European Commission. During the first Board of Directors, held on 19 March 2024, Pierre-Louis Dupont, Managing Director of AXA France, was elected Chairman of the Board of Directors and Cécile Villet, appointed Managing Director. The commercial brand of the joint venture will be unveiled shortly. The joint venture has the ambition to become a major player in insurance services for companies in France. The joint venture is owned to 50% of the capital plus one share by Worldline and will thus be consolidated by global integration. AXA owns 50% of the capital minus one share and will ensure the supervision of the joint venture. "Our joint venture, which combines the technological expertise of Worldline on the profession of insurance with the performance and business dynamics of AXA, brings in fact our common ambition to offer the best of insurance solutions to our corporate customers." Economic and Payments.

title: AXA explodes at +1.8% and aims 50% increase!
content: AXA dominates the CAC40 with a gain of +1.8%: the title redeems the €24.5 and gets closer to the resistance of the €25.5. In case of crossing, the title would open the path of €31/32, i.e. +50% was its floor of €21.2 on February 9.

title: AXA and IFC join forces to accelerate sustainable finance in developing countries
content: AXA and IFC (the International Finance Corporation), a member of the World Bank Group, have signed a collaborative agreement to accelerate sustainable finance in developing countries. Under this agreement, the two institutions aim to facilitate the development of diversified financing solutions, such as co-financing projects or risk-sharing agreements, contributing to the mobilization of private investment for climate transition. The agreement aims, among other things, to support sustainable finance projects that promote access to energy, water or other infrastructure, and contribute to sustainable agriculture or women's entrepreneurship in small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). Over the past 10 years, the two institutions have co-financed nearly 60 transactions with other partners, representing over $20 billion in new capital flows to developing countries. IFC has also provided approximately $1.3 billion to AXA, for example, to enable the development of green vehicle fleets. Thomas Buberl, Director General of AXA, said: 'By joining forces, our ambition is to strengthen our contribution to sustainable projects in developing countries, for example, to enable the development of green vehicle fleets. Sustainable development of the United Nations. Building partnerships with the most relevant actors, which is part of our strategic plan and our ESG commitments, enables us to design the best solutions to meet the challenges of environmental transition and the needs of sustainable infrastructure in emerging countries.'

title: AXA takes a 7% stake in Gan Assurances, strengthening their strategic insurance partnership.
content: AXA announces the acquisition of a minority stake of 7% in the capital of Gan Assurances, in the continuity of the strategic partnership between the two groups, for which the signing of an undertaking agreement was announced in July 2023. As a reminder, this strategic partnership in the field of property and personal insurance 'would allow it to create a joint venture with the ambition to become a reference player in insurance services in France'. AXA's investment thus demonstrates the willingness of the insurance group 'to support the development of Gan Assurances and to enable it to implement its strategy as a reference European player in the insurance market'. The preparatory work for the creation of their joint venture is carried out in accordance with the announced schedule, with an operational launch in 2024, as soon as the regulatory authorizations necessary to start its business are obtained.

title: AXA sells its health activities to Allianz for 1.1 billion euros and takes the lead in CAC 40!
content: AXA (+2.52% to 25.79 euros) takes the lead of CAC 40 thanks to the decision to divest its health and welfare insurance financing activities (AXA Santé) to the Allianz group for 1.1 billion euros. AXA's Managing Director, Thomas Buberl, was very much expected on the divestment of non-essential assets since the presentation last September of a new strategic plan. The lack of detail about this was then disappointed. Rumours about the divestment of AXA Santé have been running for several months. AXA's Board of Directors has approved the signing of a memorandum of understanding with the Allianz group for the divestment of the health and welfare insurance financing activities carried out by AXA Santé. It would take over most of the activities of AXA Santé, which offers tailored health and welfare insurance solutions for individuals, businesses and communities. AXA points out that "this divestment project would mark an important step in the implementation of its strategic roadmap" "to shape a simplified, more synergistic and efficient model of the group, while strengthening the start-up of the capital". In December, AXA announced the divestiture of its shares in 2 local African subsidiaries. At the end of March, Deutsche Bank had lowered its recommendation on value due to "its weak belief in accelerating progress in the plan for the divestiture of non-essential assets." "We believe that the market would like more clarity on the assets put on sale, as investors currently have to wait until the divestiture actually takes place before they start to take into account the valuation of the insurer," explains Morgan Stanley. "This transaction would have a positive impact estimated at about 25 basis points on the group's hard equity ratio (CET1) at the expected completion date in the first quarter of 2025. The outstanding credit of the activities covered by the Memorandum of Understanding amounts to almost EUR 15 billion at the end of December 2023, or about EUR 8 billion of risk-weighted stock (RWA). The transaction would allow the Allianz Group to become the European leader in health and foresight insurance solutions for individuals, companies and communities. "This project will enable the Group's growth ambitions to be realized and to strengthen its international income, to diversify its income, to become the European leader in health and provide health insurance solutions for individuals, the ability to create value" specified the insurer.

title: AXA in freefall: 19.05E in sight!
content: AXA validates a clear break in the 19.6E support: the sinking in the wake of 19.4E (19.25E at the bottom) is the prelude to a relapse around 19.05/19E (ex-support of 25/06 and 04/07).

title: AXA in difficulty: downgraded to "neutral" with a reduced price target.
content: AXA downgraded to 'neutral' with a lower price target of 25.8 to 21.5 euros, leaving a potential increase of 4% for the insurer's security. 'AXA is cheap in absolute terms, but we think it is too dependent on the Commission's growth to achieve its ROTE target, the broker believes, which anticipates for the CPA insurer group below 8 to 12% to consensus.

title: High rate, Axa drop: pressurized securities!
content: The rise in rates is sometimes good news for insurers (increased margins) and sometimes bad news (risk of economic slowdown, increase in claims): it is the second version that prevails with an AXA title that falls under the 22E (floor of April 5) and that risks at least a fall to 21E (floor of March 26).

title: AXA in freefall: action at 24.5E, new threat on 23.5E!
content: AXA fell by 2.5% and plunged back to 24.5E: it recorded a 5% decrease over the week, after having Alreadylost 10% the previous week. Annual losses now close to 15% year-on-year, while this insurer remains the preferred value of managers. But the most worrying thing is that AXA is firmly lowering the 25.4E floor of June 1, and especially that of the 24.9E of November 30, a major technical support threshold: the title is likely to fall to 23.5E, the floor of April 21, 2023.

title: AXA rebounded sharply after the first round of the legislative elections, benefiting from a drop in political uncertainty in France.
content: AXA bounces sharply after the first round of the general elections, like the other insurers who suffered strongly from the unexpected announcement of the dissolution of the National Assembly. AXP (+4.21% to 23.12 euros) is one of the main increases in the CAC index 40. This increase comes on the background of a certain decrease in political risk in France, as evidenced by the compression of the Franco-German spread. The latter has gone from just under 80 basis points on Friday to just over 74 basis points. Janus Henderson Investors explained recently that the increase in sovereign spreads could have a negative impact on insurers at the fundamental level, for example, on the valuation of their asset portfolio and their financing costs. The opposite is also true. "French insurers are heavily dependent on wholesale financing and more than half of their total financing comes from international investors", Fitch explains. "The financing costs are therefore particularly sensitive to the investor's sentiment towards the French insurance sector and France in general". In a note published today, Kepler Cheuvreux remains "neutral on European insurers, and neutral on French insurers", considering that it is still too early "to form meaningful positions in these companies". "As long as political uncertainty prevails, French insurers will do absolutely nothing - they will not fall further in absolute terms, but they risk under-performing a European insurance sector that is always fashionable among investors", explains the analyst. "While acknowledging that fundamentals are not currently the priority of investors over French insurers, Deutsche Bank insists on their resistance, and in particular AXA's. Whatever the outcome of the elections, the impacts for French institutions will be "limited and manageable" for him. Finally, Fitch recalls that the uncertainty as to the political measures that a new government could implement could make the economic environment more volatile, which could undermine the confidence of companies and consumers. "Such a scenario could lead to a fall in the volume of contracts for insurers, which could delay the turnaround, which could make it more volatile. of the insurance sector we were waiting for, warns the rating agency.

title: Safran takes off, +3.6% on the Paris Stock Exchange after Berenberg's purchase recommendation.
content: Safran is experiencing the biggest increase in the SBF 120 index on Tuesday morning at the Paris Stock Exchange, supported by a note from Berenberg in which the broker initiates the follow-up of the motorist with a recommendation for purchase. At 10h00, the title gains 3.6% while at the same time the SBF 120 index declines by 0.2%. In his study, the German broker welcomes the progress made both in terms of profitability and cash flow by the new management team since its entry into office in 2016. 'Following the announcement of the possible reduction of orders in the civil aviation sector, the stock exchange price has fallen by about 7% in 2023', recalls the professional. 'We think this creates an interesting entry point for investors, knowing that we are optimistic about the company's long-term prospects', he adds. Berenberg, who believes that the group should indeed take advantage of the trend towards electrification of the aviation industry, aims at a price of 135 euros. According to market sources, JPMorgan analysts would have improved their target on their side. 125 to 130 euros. Safran's market capitalization currently stands at more than 22.5 billion euros.

title: Safran's flickering, risk diving to 99E!
content: Safran ricoches under 104.3E and threatens 101.8E, the support level preserved since 13 March (and ex-zenith of 14/12/2023): an acceleration of consolidation could start by 99.7E, the floor of 5 March, or 99E, the short-term oblique support.

title: Oddo BHF raises its target for Safran to 202 euros, attracted by the high visibility and generous dividend redistributor.
content: Oddo BHF reiterates its 'overperformance' opinion on SAFRAN with a price target raised from 173 to 202 euros, explaining that the favourable impact of cyclical tensions leads to an upward revision of its estimates for the group of high technologies. Certainly, following the recent increase in the price (18.6% since the beginning of the year), the valuation is going with a rolling PE 12 months of 26.5 times compared to 18.5 times for the five-year pre-Covid average, but the visibility is strong. Oddo BHF estimates the average annual growth of BPA 2023-26 to 19%, with a high degree of certainty, and to almost 11 billion euros the accumulated free cash flow over the next three years, of which a significant share will be returned to shareholders according to it.

title: Morgan Stanley raises his target price on Safran to 150 euros, waiting for a better entry point.
content: Morgan Stanley has raised his Safran course objective from 120 to 150 euros on Thursday, while renewing his recommendation "online weighting" on the title pending a better entry point.In a research note, the analyst points out that the French specialist in aeronautics, defence and security technologies is a well-known and widely held name by investors for good reasons.The intermediary thus indicates that it expects organic growth of 7% this year and in 2025 under the impetus of its activities in aircraft engines, propulsion systems, and security and defence technologies.In the immediate future, Morgan Stanley points out that the prudence surrounding its margin forecasts, less ambitious than the objectives of the consensus, prompts him to post a "online weighting" advice on value.

title: Moody's raises Safran's outlook to "positive" with a possible increase in rating!
content: The rating agency Moodys changed its perspective on Safran's credit from "stable" to "positive" and confirmed the rating A3. This follows the change made on 26 April 2024 by S&P with an increase in the rating to A/A1 with a stable perspective. "The positive outlook recognises the good organic growth, the continuous improvement of Safran's margins and the maintenance of a strong cash generation, which reflect the strengthening of Safran's business profile in recent years," said Matthias Heck, Vice-President of Moody's Ratings - Senior Credit Officer and Chief Analyst for Safran. "The rating could be raised in the coming quarters, if Safran continued to generate high levels of profitability, pursued conservative capital allocation policies and remained cautious in terms of mergers and acquisitions, leading to metrics of leverage consistent with a higher rating," added Matthias Heck.

title: BlackRock comes down under 5% of Safran's capital!
content: BlackRock, acting on behalf of customers and funds under its management, declared to the AMF that on January 10th it had crossed the 5% threshold of Safran's voting rights, at the result of a sale of shares outside and on the market. The US asset management giant specified that it held, on behalf of the said customers and funds, 4,123,456 Safran shares representing so many voting rights, that is 4,97% of the capital and 4,955 of the leader's voting rights in the propulsion systems and aircraft equipment.

title: Safran : Oddo BHF reduces its price target to 115 euros, but maintains its "overperformance" rating despite disappointing T1 results.
content: Oddo BHF maintains its 'overperformance' rating on the Safran title, with a reduced price target of 120 to 115 euros. Following the release of T1 24 results, also lower T2 guidance and a drastic downward revision of the guidance 2024, we lower our forecast by 32% on average. As a reminder, the turnover was 10.2 billion euros to -17.5% compared to the previous quarter and 15.3% year-on-year, lower than the consensus and guidance of 11.5 billion euros. Nevertheless, despite more conservative forecasts, the title remains very attractive, the broker estimates. We have revised our forecast for 2024 by 37% and we are instead positioning ourselves at the bottom of the guidance (25/26 billion euros), i.e. a rebound of about 9% to the S2 vs S1. This could be a conservative position, but we expect to see what the orders will be taken from the T2 to raise our figures.

title: Safran collapses by 4%: does the fall continue to reach 91.4 euros?
content: Safran, who had registered a closing price of EUR 108.5 on Monday 25 March and who was crossing the 106 euro bar this morning, reversed its upward trend during the day and dropped by 4% below EUR 102 (a decrease of EUR 6.5) and below the 104 euro floor from 6 to 12 March. The title could be ready to close the gap of EUR 96.04 on 14 February, even test the resistance of the 91.4 euro floor on 13 and 14 February.

title: Safran: Decrease in net income and operating margin in Q1 2024.
content: Safran recorded a net share of the group (RNG) decrease of 17.2% for the first three months of 2024, to 242.1 million euros, with an adjusted operating margin before acquisitions falling from 1.8 point to 19.8% of sales. The turnover of the aeronautics and space group decreased from 6.2% to 1.84 billion euros. In a market of the aeronautics sector in retreat, its sales decreased by 6.1% in organic, of which -6.2% in mature countries and -7.1% in new economies. Taking into account the current macroeconomic outlook, Safran confirmed that it aimed for 2024 an adjusted operating margin before acquisitions between 19.5 and 20.3%, and an organic turnover and by slightly increasing acquisitions 'low single digit'.

title: Safran falls by 0.87% after disappointing results for 2023-2024
content: Safran fell from 0.87% to 113.40 euros in the wake of the publication of disappointing results for the financial year 2023-2024. The specialist of innovative aeronautical systems and equipment had reaffirmed its annual targets at the end of March at the same time as he was delivering forecasts for the financial year 2024-2025 disappointing because of a longer-than-expected digestion of the stocks of the customers of the Propulsion activity. On Wednesday, the Safran action had been supported, which had gone from underweight to neutral on value, considering that the worst had gone for Safran. In fiscal year 2023/2024, the Ebitda decreased from 15% to 332 million euros, bringing a margin of 34% against 36% in the previous fiscal year. The specialist of innovative aeronautical systems and equipment was penalized by a 10% decrease in its turnover to 978 million euros due to the difficulties of the Propulsion activity. The group recorded a negative free cash flow of 43 million euros against a positive free cash flow of 34 million euros in 2022-2023. In addition to these results, Safran indicated that it was going to provide electrical propulsion systems for the production of electric aircraft elsewhere in the production plant. "We believe that these are clear signs of a robust demand for our electric propulsion systems and of support for the planned rise in power," said Deutsche Bank. "We believe that this is a strong demand for our electric propulsion systems and that we are looking forward to the expected rise in power."

title: Pernod Ricard bounces: net profit multiplied by 10 in 2023!
content: Pernod Ricard (RI) publishes for 2023 a net share of the group of EUR 8.7 million, compared with a net loss of EUR 0.9 million in 2022, and an EBITDA increasing from 12.8% to 13.3 million, with the full positive effect of past restructurings. Despite the slowdown in the spirits market, notably in France, the group achieved sales of EUR 194.2 million, up 7.2% excluding exchange-rate effects, with an increase on both clusters, particularly on the international cluster. The year 2024 is part of a normalisation context with a resumption of the decline in consumption volumes, combined with the destocking of some importing customers, and a context of downward pressure on prices.

title: Pernod Ricard crossed the 15% threshold of social capital and reinforced its weight on the board of directors.
content: Pernod Ricard informs its shareholders that, on 21 June, we crossed the threshold of 15% of the share capital and now hold 15.10% of the capital and 21.95% of the voting rights, following the reduction in the total number of shares of the group of spirits. We will continue our stock purchases according to the market opportunities, but we do not plan to acquire control of the company or to ask for an appointment, given the mandates already held on the board of directors.

title: Pernod Ricard starts the financial year 2024-2025 on a positive note, with an increase of 0.5% in its turnover.
content: The consolidated turnover of PERNOD RICARD in the first quarter of the financial year 2024-2025 amounts to €8.514 billion, compared with €8.469 billion, up 0.5% compared to the financial year 2023-2024, combined with a 4.2% increase in sales volumes. This reflects the group's sustainable growth strategy and takes advantage of the contributions of the latest acquisitions in the areas of Spirit and Wine. On the same scale, the turnover rose to €8.354 billion (-2.1%) with volumes sold increasing by 3.5%. At a constant exchange rate, the turnover comes down from 2.5% to €8.291 billion. Following the trends observed in the last quarter of the financial year 2023-2024, the conditions of activity continued to normalise, favouring an increase in sales volumes. As announced, this trend also incorporates the gradual and voluntary intensification of marketing and marketing efforts. These actions promote the consumption of spirits and wine which remains well oriented in the context of persistent pressure on purchasing power in the first quarter. -2024, the volumes sold were down 3.9% on the same perimeter and 3.7% on a comparable perimeter. In terms of prospects, PERNOD RICARD pursues its annual objectives with confidence. For the financial year 2024-2025, the wine and spirits company still sets itself the objective of reaching a turnover of 34.5 billion euros. In terms of results, PERNOD RICARD confirms its objective of returning to a standard operating margin of around 20%, integrating the continuation of the marketing and marketing efforts necessary to support the dynamics of the market and guarantee the accessibility of its products to the greatest number.

title: Pernod Ricard: Deutsche Bank sees a potential of 24% appreciation, a strong upside share on the Paris Stock Exchange!
content: The Pernod Ricard title recorded one of the largest increases in the SBF 120 on Thursday morning at the Paris Stock Exchange, led by a recommendation increase from Deutsche Bank, which advised to take advantage of the recent low-value access. At 9:40 a.m., the action of the group of spirits climbed by 3.6% while the SBF 120 index gained 0.6% at the same time. Deutsche Bank announced this morning that it had raised its advice on the title of 'preserver' to 'purchase', with a price target set at 112 euros, i.e. a potential of appreciation of 24% compared to the previous day. In its note, the analyst referred to a tendency to improve the Group's deliveries on the American market, as well as a more favourable dynamic at the level of whiskey's sales in India. According to its calculations, the action could be worth up to 234 euros, i.e. two and a half times its current stock exchange price. Deutsche, which evokes a 'unique' and 'quality' value in a sector deemed 'attractive', believes that the recent underperformance of the title constitutes an entry point for investors to be of interest. On the Stock Exchange, the Pernod Ricard title still shows a decline of around 21% since the beginning of the year, compared to a gain of 8% for the SBF 120.

title: Pernod Ricard sells its organic spirits in the United States to Platinum Equity
content: PERNOD RICARD (RI) today announced the signing of an agreement to sell its organic spirits business in the United States to Platinum Equity, an investment company based across the Atlantic. This sale is part of the portfolio review and the asset rotation program announced in March 2022 as part of the Transform & Accelerate strategy. The amount of this transaction was not disclosed. PERNOD RICARD will retain a minority unconsolidated interest in the asset. The completion of the sale is subject to the usual preconditions. In 2022, the activities of organic spirits in the United States, consisting of the brands Avion and Absolut, accounted for approximately 3% of the total sales of PERNOD RICARD, and had a dilutive impact on the organic growth of sales and on the current operating margin of the group. Alexandre Ricard, Director General of the PERNOD RICARD Group, stated that his company "has engaged in active portfolio management and rotation for activities no longer falling within its growth and value creation priorities, in the framework of the plan, in the framework of the transformation Today's announcement marks an important step towards achieving this goal, while giving the Avion and Absolut brands the opportunity to prosper under a new direction." "This sale, once completed, will allow us to focus more on our portfolio of strong brands, centered around quality and diversity, and to reinvest in our priority growth segments," says the Managing Director. "In addition, PERNOD RICARD is certainly not finished with the divestiture operations. Last December, it was learned that a company linked to Campari (Italian agri-food company) had initiated exclusive negotiations to acquire fully the Chivas Regal brand held by PERNOD RICARD. The proposed transaction will be submitted to the relevant staff representative bodies for consultation. It should be closed in the coming months, subject to the usual closing conditions.

title: Pernod Ricard: sales down, but better than expected.
content: Pernod Ricard (+8.3% to [...] euros) has a significant place in the CAC 40 index after reporting a decrease in its sales of spirits and wines slightly less than expected by analysts in the third quarter of 2023-2024. During this period, the turnover of this activity fell by [...] % in published data and by [...] % on an organic basis (against a [...] % consensus) to [...] million euros. The group's total revenues are [...] million euros, down [...] % over a year in published data. They are down [...] % at constant exchange rates and scope. The Americas region recorded a slight decrease in sales in the third quarter, pointing to a significant sequential improvement compared to the second quarter. In the United States, the strong promotional intensity (reinforced at the end of the year) and the continuation of destocking have weighed on the performance of its liquor division. Depletions (sales of wholesalers) have improved sequentially but still remain below expectations. The company also explains that Asia-Pacific has shown that the performance of its region.
