For the delayed financial year 2023-2024, which will end at the end of March, the group anticipates an organic decline in its turnover "at the bottom of its range" from [...] % to [...] % compared to the previous financial year. It also anticipates a controlled decrease in the current operating margin in organic, thanks to the implementation of a significant cost reduction plan estimated at around € [...] million this year (of which € [...] million has already been achieved in the first half). Pernod Ricard still forecasts a negative exchange effect of € [...] million to € [...] million on turnover and € [...] million on current operating income.

title: Pernod Ricard: Turnover slightly increased in the first half of 2023/24, despite a decline outside Europe.
content: Pernod Ricard publishes a turnover of €8,542 million for the first half of 2023/24 (July to December 23), up +2.1% in comparable data (constant exchange and scope) and down -3.7% in published data. Europe, which accounts for 57.4% of activity over the period, shows an overall trend of +4.2% at current exchange rates and +3.5% in comparable data over the first half of the year, with an increase in all premium brands over the six-month period. Moreover, the area outside Europe, which accounts for 42.6% of activity over the period, shows in the first half of the year an overall change of -10.3% at current exchange rates and -1.2% in comparable data. The Pernod Ricard Group will report on its outlook for turnover and profitability for the financial year 2023-2024 on the occasion of the presentation of its half-yearly results on 1 March 2024.

title: Wellington Management takes foot in Pernod Ricard with 4.99% of the capital.
content: We are required to inform our shareholders that, on June 18, Wellington Management Group LLP, acting on behalf of funds and customers, crossed the threshold of our company, Pernod Ricard, by extending 12,769,809 shares, representing 4,99% of the capital and 4,17% of the voting rights.

title: Action in the dark: target of a revised course of 150 euros
content: We note a deterioration of our 'overperformance' to 'neutral' title with a revised price target of 150 euros, following a downward revision of our BPA 2023-24 forecasts. The catalysts expected to support our performance, such as the recovery in our key markets, such as China and the United States, are not being realized. The timing of our growth recovery is regularly postponed and uncertainty about its timing should affect our performance.

title: Pernod Ricard: disappointing turnover, price target reduced to 187 euros.
content: Oddo BHF confirms its 'neutral' rating on the title Pernod Ricard with a slightly reduced price target of 192 to 187 euros. Yesterday evening, Pernod Ricard published a quarterly turnover of 2.35 billion euros when Oddo BHF was counting on 2.45 billion euros. 'The 4% difference from our forecast comes from a more negative impact than expected, in connection with the depreciation of the dollar (-15% in the S1)', stresses the analyst. Pernod Ricard has not confirmed its annual guidance, stating only that he will communicate on his outlook for turnover and profitability for the financial year 2023/24 at the time of the presentation of the results on 1 March. 'We remain in the expectation of a new guidance to refine our annual figures', says Oddo BHF who also believes that 'the suspension of the guidance could be misinterpreted by the market while the stock price, which is falling by 18% over 12 months, is almost at its lowest level over 20 years'.

title: Dassault Systems: reduced net loss, organic growth of 11% in the first half of 2024.
content: DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE (DSY) recorded a net loss of EUR 14.1 million in the first half of 2024, reduced compared to the net loss of EUR 20.8 million in the first half of 2023. The net loss of the first half of 2024 includes a interest charge of EUR 12.9 million, an increase of EUR 5.6 million, due in particular to the increase in interest rates over the period. LEbitda adjusted shows organic growth from 18.2% to EUR 178 million, highlighting an adjusted dEbitda margin of 38.5%, an increase of 2.8 points. The turnover reached EUR 464 million, an increase of 11.2% in comparable data and 11.0% in published data. It was supported by sales of design and simulation software: +18.1% in comparable data. The French company generated EUR 13 million of unlearned free cash flow. The DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE (DSY) management targeted a positive free cash flow increase only in the second half of 2024 and over the entire financial year. 2025. "The difficult economic environment, especially in Europe, does not change our priority of continuing to implement our strategy rigorously, to deliver long-term profitable and sustainable growth while generating cash", commented Bernard Charlès, CEO of DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE (DSY). Before adding: "This reduced economic visibility in 2024 leads us to review our revenue targets". The company now anticipates organic growth of its turnover between 10% and 11% compared to 12% to 14% before. The adjusted ebitda margin is still expected to exceed 38%.

title: Dassault Systèmes takes off, goal 250 euros in 4 years!
content: DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE leapt by more than 7%, surrounded after an increase of advice at Stifel to 'preserve' for 'purchase' on the title of the leader of 3D and software engineering, a target of 120 to 160 euros. 'The short-term dynamics is positive thanks to a favorable basis of comparison', the broker believes, believing that the price of the action should rise significantly to 250 euros in the space of four years.

title: Dassault Systems targeted by an acquisition offer of 800 million euros!
content: DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE (DSY) is pleased to announce that it has received a non-committal letter of offer concerning the potential acquisition of 100% of the activities of PLM (Product Lifecycle Management), Simulation and Design Experience from its Software division. The group of 3D solutions and management of the overall business value of 800 million euros. DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE adds that its board of directors, under the auspices of its CEO, and the management of the company will discuss this proposal with the stakeholders. It states "that no certainty can be given as to the outcome of the negotiations and the conclusion of a final agreement between the parties." Finally, the company confirmed its objective of reaching a final strategic growth agreement with its partners, which will then be implemented through an accelerated development procedure, by July 2024.

title: Dassault Systèmes explodes expectations with an operating result up 47%!
content: The Dassault Systèmes title appears in the ranking of the largest market increases on Thursday at the Paris Stock Exchange, following the publication of semi-annual results deemed 'excellent' by analysts. The company of 3D solutions and life cycle management products said that it had achieved a current operating result (ROC) of 343 million euros in its first half closed on 31 December 2023, compared to 234 million euros a year earlier, an increase of 47% at constant rates (42% at real exchange rates). By comparison, Dassault Systèmes had set itself an initial growth rate of more than 40% in published data. This performance allowed its operating margin to stand at 12.3% compared to 11.5% for the first half of 2022-2023, a higher historical one. Similarly, the net result progresses significantly to become positive again at 120 million euros, against a loss of 40 million euros a year earlier. The group confirmed its forecast of an operating margin around 13% over the entire financial year 2023-2024, where the consensus is aimed for the moment at 12.8%. the company of 3D solutions and life cycle management products earns more than 5%.

title: "Dassault Systèmes publishes a turnover of 5.34 billion euros in 2023, up 4.5%."
content: Dassault Systèmes SE (DSY) publishes a turnover for the financial year 2023 of €5.34 billion, a purely organic increase of 4.5%. This turnover finally falls in the middle of the range announced in October and takes advantage of a fourth quarter which saw several parameters improve compared to the T2 and T3: productivity rates of engineers again above 94%, decrease of inter-contracts and recovery of a slight increase in the number of staff. The annual turnover in France stands at €2.41 billion, up by +7.2% at constant scope. This good performance remains higher than the reference market. The French market, although affected by a context of prudence and control of investment budgets, continued to offer opportunities and a volume of tenders, although less than 2022, but well above what they were in 2021. At the international level, while demand, as in France, has been adversely affected overall by the attentism in investment and certain decisions to reduce expenditure, the whole of the areas of activity nevertheless delivers an organic one, although the demand, as in France, has been affected by the overall reduction in investment and certain decisions to increase in the number of activity areas. +2.1% for a turnover of 2.93 billion euros. The U.S. zone achieves a good overall performance and the major adaptation efforts made up to the T3 allowed the number of inter-contracts to decrease during the T4. The Asian zone took advantage of the stability offered by its exposure to public procurement, particularly Asian, to contribute to the group's growth. The operating margin of activity is expected at the top of the range announced in October, i.e. between 14 and 15%. A stable workforceAfter two consecutive quarters of measured decline, the trend reversed during the fourth quarter with the return to a slight increase in the number of employees (+120 employees). In all areas, a slight decrease in the turnover of the workforce is observed. The end-of-year workforce thus stands at 20,450 employees, equivalent to what it was at the beginning of the year. Outlook 2024: encouraging signals in a still uncertain contextThe market at the beginning of the year 2024 is similar to that experienced in 2023. The signs of attractiveness and even of search for savings are still present in several customers. The first quarter will be affected by an unfavourable calendar effect (-1 day chargeable) while the entire exercise 2024 will benefit from a favourable calendar effect (+1 or 2 days depending on the geographical areas). Dassault Systèmes remains cautious with an always very clear objective of preserving its margins and anticipates to date: Publication of the results of the financial year 2023, March 20, 2024 after the stock exchange.

title: "Dassault Systèmes: the president denounces the attacks on the financial health of the group"
content: In a statement published by Les Echos, Charles Edelstenne, president of the company, strongly criticizes the negative comments on the group's financial health. In a statement published by Les Echos, he expresses his discontent at the comments that tarnish the reputation of DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE and calls for the cessation of these remarks to allow employees to work calmly. Three proposals are at stake: EP Equity Investment (EPEI) led by Daniel Kretinsky with the support of Attestor, OnePoint by David Layani, and a last from obligators associated with several banks. The visions of the sponsors diverge strongly. Layani wants to maintain the integrity of DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE with an injection of fresh capital, while Kretinsky proposes to clear a large part of the debt of 5,2 billion euros and to sell assets to repay creditors. Kretinsky's criticisms of the financial situation of DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE, published in Le Figaro, were particularly alarming, pointing to the rapid deterioration of the operating margin, evoking a deterioration in the situation. This pessimism would also have influenced some banks, notably BNP Paribas, which would have been too aligned with Kretinsky's vision, and which would have been excluded from the negotiations. The final decision on the sponsor should be announced on 31 May, when DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE will choose from the restructuring offers submitted.

title: "Dassault Systems for sale: massive dilution expected, valuation at 0.01 euro"
content: DASSAULT SE SYSTEMS : Invest Securities adopts a recommendation for sale and a price target of 0.01 euro on DASSAULT SE SYSTEMS after the presentation yesterday of 2 revised offers. "To obtain the support of the bond creditors, C. Bernard who owns 15% of the capital has totally sacrificed the current shareholders who will represent less than 0.01% of the post-restructuring capital", stresses the broker. He adds that "no matter which supply wins", "the dilution will be colossal". But for the analyst, the "worst is that this restructuring may not be sufficient given the operational deterioration" of DASSAULT SE SYSTEMS.

title: Dassault Systèmes crumbles: -33% in 5 days, all the historical media sprayed!
content: DASSAULT SYSTEMS VESSES itself from -17% to 140E (opening of a 'gap' under 145.84E) and cumulative -33% decrease in 5 sessions (-50% since the zenith of January 3): the floor of the 144.63E of the 23/10 is sprayed... and under the 143.97E (bref test on the 23/10/2023) there is no longer any historical or 'foreseeable' support by projection of pre-existing bearer supports.

title: Dassault SE systems: rating lowered to CCC, default within 12 months?
content: According to information published by the Agefi - Dow Jones, the financial rating agency Standard & Poor's announced this evening a further deterioration of the financial rating of Dassault Systèmes SE. The rating has been lowered by two steps and now comes to CCC, a risk of default of the software solutions company clearly envisaged by the US agency. Moreover, the outlook remains negative. It is a liquidity problem that worries S&P due to the delays that Dassault Systèmes SE puts in renegotiating its debt and refinancing maturities up to 12 months. Already, the software solutions company has announced the appointment of an ad hoc agent to supervise its discussions with its main banks. In the event of failure of these negotiations, S&P considers that the restructuring of the debt would be equivalent to a default. The security that has lost 35% of its value in 2 years and already 20% since the first January of last saw a real downfall in the underworld, punctuated by a walse of the leaders and an approximate strategy. The days to find a solution now appear to be good and counted. This is probably why the minister of the economy has come out of the woods. Given the strategic nature of the company and the many employees, it is not surprising that the state is following the case closely behind the scenes.

title: "Dassault Systèmes dives at €33.5, close to the critical support zone!"
content: The downward trend of Dassault Systèmes SE (DSY) continues inexorably, resulting in a 5% drop under €33.5, thus breaking the support of the €34.5 established on 1, 2 and 3 July, as well as on 20 October 2023. The ultimate medium-term support zone is located around €33.3/33.5, corresponding to the major support of 27 January 2023, 27 December 2022.

title: Stellantis renews its management team to achieve customer excellence!
content: STELLANTIS N.V. (STLAM) continues its transformation with a series of key changes starting June 1, 2024. In this context, our CEO of the brand DS is named Chief Sales & Marketing Officer, adding to its current responsibilities. He succeeds our leader who will now be dedicated to the overall development of the Opel brand. In addition, our director takes over from our manager, who is party for personal reasons. Our new CEO will be responsible for achieving the goal of the strategic plan Dare Forward 2030, which aims to make STELLANTIS N.V. (STLAM) 'the number one in customer satisfaction'. Finally, our new arrival, former Legal Director and Director of Ethics and Compliance at ArcelorMittal, succeeds our director as head of Global Corporate Office and Public Affairs and integrates our leading team. She brings 'a solid international and legal experience'.

title: "Carlos Tavares, DG Stellantis, will receive 36.5 million euros in 2023, a record pay in the automotive industry!"
content: The shareholders of Stellantis N.V. (STLAM) approved at 70% of the vote the sharp increase in remuneration of our managing director, Carlos Tavares, at the general meeting of the automotive group, held in Amsterdam. This remuneration, which will reach 36.5 million euros for the year 2023, includes the payment of a premium of 10 million euros for the "transformation" of the group created in 2021 with the merger of PSA and Fiat-Chrysler. In the course of the year 2023, Mr Tavares will first receive 23.5 million euros, paid in large part in shares, which will also increase with the value of the title of the group, which has almost doubled over the last three years. With our 14 brands, notably Peugeot, Citroën, Fiat, Dodge and Opel, we have published a new record profit of 18.6 billion euros for 2023, increasing by 11% over a year. Our turnover is close to 190 billion euros. Our automotive group has recorded record results since the merger, notably thanks to a drastic mastery of the costs impul We believe that this remuneration should be compared with those of multinationals such as Boeing in the United States. Finally, we recall that our group will redistribute nearly EUR 1.9 billion to our employees around the world and that our shareholders will receive around EUR 7.7 billion for the financial year 2023.

title: Stellantis and Hype join forces to boost hydrogen decarbonized mobility in Île-de-France!
content: Stellantis N.V. announces that its joint venture dedicated to hydrogen mobility, and Hype, pioneer of hydrogen taxis, will establish a partnership with the objective of accelerating decarbonated hydrogen mobility. The idea is to rapidly develop the use of hydrogen mobility by increasing volumes to reduce costs. The partnership covers the entire ecosystem of decarbonated H2 mobility: supply of decarbonated hydrogen, H2 distribution stations and hydrogen vehicles. Specifically, our joint venture will provide hydrogen decarbonated to Hype via a 1 MW electrolyser to power its network in Île-de-France. Two H2 stations will be deployed by the end of 2024, including one at the Stellantis concession in Corbeil-Essonnes. Our joint venture will also provide a Peugeot e-Expert Hydrogen to Hype and deliver at least 9 additional vehicles in 2024.

title: RBC relies on Stellantis, a 31-euro target, before investors meet in the United States.
content: RBC renewed on Friday its "overperformance" opinion and its 31 euro price target on Stellantis N.V. (STLAM) while the automotive company will hold its first investor meeting in the United States since the merger was finalized in 2021. In a note, the Canadian broker explains that it expects the market to respond favourably to the event, particularly in the light of the recent decline in the title. At present, the title has an RIP 2024 of about 4 times, with a return close to 7%.

title: Stellantis N.V. jumped to Amsterdam after Dongfeng announced a €225 million contribution in the first quarter of 2024.
content: Stellantis N.V. (STLAM) is at the top of the flagship index of the Amsterdam square. The brand, which is growing from 1.15% to 48.42 euros, announced this Thursday that Dongfeng's contribution to its own results was estimated at 225 million euros for the first quarter of 2024. This announcement, which follows the publication by the Chinese automotive group of the results of its fourth staggered quarter, closed on 31 March, has been well received by the investors. Following the recent overhaul of their alliance, Stellantis N.V.'s participation in Dongfeng's capital has been reduced to 38.9%, of which 16.2% of shares held on-line and 22.7% of shares held in a trust.The Dutch group expects, in the long term, to retain only 15% stake in Dongfeng, compared with more than 43% last year. Dongfeng will continue to hold a 15% stake in Stellantis N.V. In the first quarter, Stellantis N.V. has achieved a turnover of 1.07 billion. In Europe, sales have increased by 4.3%. Stellantis N.V. is expected to publish its 1st half-year results on Thursday 25 July.

title: Stellantis N.V.: new retreat, target at 15.90E
content: The pullback started in 18.35E continues (4th consecutive retreat session) with a drop under the 16.50E: the title of Stellantis N.V. should come back to support the small support of the 15.90E of 27 February.

title: "STLAM) demented merger project with PSA, focuses on its strategic plan"
content: STLAM (-1.81% to 20.85 euros) is among the largest market declines on Monday, while PSA gains 0.34% to 35.62 euros. STLAM's president, Carlos Tavares, denied in a press release "any merger project". He responded to an information from the French daily newspaper "Les Échos" reported by Reuters, according to which the Dutch government, which owns 15% of PSA's capital, would study a merger project with STLAM. "No merger project with other manufacturers is under consideration. The company focuses on the implementation of the strategic plan "Dare Forward" and on the timely implementation of the announced projects, in order to strengthen its activities in all the markets in which it operates, including France," said Carlos Tavares. Jefferies points out that with 16.5% of market share in Europe in private cars, STLAM's challenge may be more to regain market share in organic than to add new brands. Jefferies points out that STLAM's president, Carlos Tavares, has denied all of the interests in PSA. his group did not play in the same category and that such a complex operation involving the Dutch state was excluded for a manufacturer who has always protected his political independence. The broker remains for purchase on the title with a price target of 24 euro.

title: Stellantis falls on the Stock Exchange after customs duties on Chinese electric vehicles, but promises a record dividend.
content: STELLANTIS N.V. (STLAM) recorded the largest decrease of the day, closely followed by its competitor Renault. Volkswagen and Porsche's sharp fall in Frankfurt confirms the sectoral character of this movement, following the announcement of European tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles. This decision is more worrying than it reassures the industrialists. On the margins of its investor day, STLAM confirmed its 2024 targets and focused on improving the remuneration of its shareholders. In 2025, STLAM aims at a dividend distribution rate at the top of the range of 25% to 30% compared to 25% in recent years. In the medium term, it relies on liquidity levels representing 25 to 30% of revenues "to put the efficiency of capital and promote a high return for shareholders". This year, however, it will distribute more than €7.7 billion of dividends and share buybacks. The Euro-American manufacturer has also confirmed its 2024 objectives with a current two-digit operating margin and a positive free cash flow. However, the current operating margin is expected between 10-11% in the first half of the year, with a free flow. "The second half of the year marked by significant product launches, cost initiatives and the early improvement of working capital," said STLAM. "As part of its investment day, STLAM management will present the nine "key strategic differences on which it will rely to create value".

title: "STLAM is collapsing: 10% of the highest drop since its rating!"
content: STLAM : The value of the car operator plunged last Tuesday with a 10% drop due to a dim outlook on its operating margin. This is its biggest decline since the beginning of its listing. The doubt has begun on this value, which is still appreciated by investors. The automotive sector could enter a complicated period.

title: Stellantis: The risk of rupture of technical support causes uncertainty about the upward trend.
content: STELLANTIS N.V. (STLAM) dives by -3% under 44.5E and approaches a support level located around 44.3E. The breakup of this area may prefigure a retracement of the 42E floor of 26 October last... and the medium-term upward trend would then be compromised.

title: Danone aims to drastically reduce its greenhouse gas emissions by 2050
content: Danone announces its greenhouse gas (GHG) emission reduction targets, thus committing itself to reduce absolute emissions from scopes 1 and 2 by 54% by 2030 compared to its reference year 2022.The food group also plans to reduce absolute emissions from scope 3 related to its agricultural and food production activities by 30.3% and non-FLAG emissions (purchases of goods and services, upstream transport and distribution) by 25% over the same period.In the long term, Danone is committed to reducing absolute emissions from scopes 1 and 2 and non-FLAG emissions from scope 3 by 90% by 2050. It also aims to reduce absolute emissions from scope 3 related to its agricultural and food production activities by 72% over the same period.

title: Danone sells its Spanish JV Alimentación Saludable to Grupo Iberia for €68 million.
content: Danone announces that the exclusive negotiations initiated in early June with its historical partner in Spain, Grupo Iberia, have been successful. Danone has transferred all its shares in the Joint-Venture Alimentación Saludable to its historical partner for a total amount of 68 million euros with a partial delay of 6 months. This partnership, initiated in 2006, aimed to promote the market for healthy foods in Spain. Danone and Grupo Iberia have thus combined their respective know-how to manufacture and distribute healthy foods in Spain. Alimentación Saludable's contribution in Danone's consolidated accounts has resulted in a share of 8.5 million euros as at 31 December 2023. Grupo Iberia wishes to accelerate its development strategy, while Danone takes advantage of this operation to continue its strategy of refocusing on its core business. This operation will enable the group to strengthen its financial structure, accelerate business development projects, support the brand and food transition, maintain a high level of investment in the quality of its industrial tool and also focus on supporting its growth relays.

title: Danone jumped by 22%: dividend up and ambition to reach 8 billion euros in turnover in 2026-2027!
content: In the financial year 2023-2024, the Danone agri-food group generated a net profit, group share, of EUR 404.1 million compared to EUR 314.2 million in the previous financial year. The current operating income increased from 20.1% to EUR 420.5 million, bringing out a margin of 6.3% compared to only 5.5% in 2022-2023. Danone aimed at a current operating result of more than EUR 400 million. Already published, the turnover increased from 7% to EUR 6.7 billion. After a financial year 2022-2023 marked by price increases and volume reductions, the group benefited in 2023-2024 from exceptional conditions of activity which enabled it to show results in sharp increase ", commented Danone. The Executive Board will propose to the general meeting of 22 August 2024, the payment of a dividend of EUR 4.20 per share against EUR 3.30 paid in the previous financial year, corresponding to a rate of distribution of 22%. It will also propose a division by 2 of the nominal of the action in order to strengthen the attractiveness of the title and promote its liquidity. 25, Danone targets a turnover of 7,2 billion euros and a standard operating margin of 5,5%. The group intends to step up its strategy of regaining volumes. The company stresses that it "will remain fully mobilised in the implementation of its strategic roadmap which will enable it to cross the course of 8 billion euros of turnover with an Ebitda of nearly 640 million euros in 2026-2027".

title: Danone is about to buy the leader of white cheeses, Ferme du Val de Loire.
content: Danone announces the entry into exclusive negotiations with the Ferme du Val de Loire group for the acquisition of 100% of the capital. This French, family and independent company created in 1985 and still led by its founder Jacques Bourgeois is an important player in the market for fresh cheeses, notably n°1 in the white cheese sector. With a turnover of nearly 250 million euros 2023, the group has more than 800 employees spread over 5 production sites in France. The objective set between the parties is to finalize this operation in the last quarter of 2024. This operation should allow Danone's cheese-making activities to be present on 80% of the families produced from the Fromagerie department in GMS ("Grandes et Moyens Surfaces") and thus strengthen its presence alongside the brands Activ and Oikos.

title: Danone joins the fight against global warming by integrating the WBCSD!
content: Danone joins the World Business Council for Sustainable Development (WBCSD), which brings together major international groups working together to limit the impacts of the climate crisis, restore natural spaces and combat inequalities, with the goal of building, by 2050, a world where nine billion people will be able to live in dignity, without overexploitation of the planet's resources. Danone's mission is to promote a healthy and sustainable diet. Based on four pillars (nourish people; preserve planets; promote the group's 2030 sustainable development initiative, entitled "A planet, ecosystems, communities", covers all aspects of the business and is designed to contribute directly to the achievement of the UN's sustainable development goals. Danone's commitment to the WBCSD includes One Planet Business for Biodiversity, an intersectoral group of international companies committed to act in favour of biodiversity, in particular by promoting regenerative agriculture.

title: Danone : Oddo BHF maintains its neutral score, valuing at a historically low point
content: Oddo BHF maintains its 'neutral' rating on the Danone title, with an unchanged price target of 60 euros. 'The valuation of the 15x title of PE NTM is at a historically low point for a group with quality fundamentals on a supporting sector', stresses the analyst. Oddo BHF still expects 12% decrease in Operational Results 23/24e. 'We are slightly revising our forecasts for FY 24/25e, about which the sell-side consensus is still too optimistic in our opinion', he adds. The broker also says he is aware that the question of the medium term remains that of the degree of confidence in the assumption of a return to growth next year. 'This prospect could encourage some investors to consider the current level of PE as a potential entry point', he argues, while recalling that caution remains to be exercised, especially in view of the almost systematic affluence of expectations for several months.

title: Wellington Management takes foot in Danone with 4.17% of the capital.
content: Wellington Management Group, acting on behalf of funds and customers, reported to the AMF that on March 5, it had crossed the 5% threshold of Danone's capital, on the basis of a sale of shares in the market. The declarant specified that it held 14821.119 Danone shares on behalf of the said funds and customers, representing as many voting rights, i.e. 4.17% of the voting rights of the agri-food group.

title: Danone: results in dull despite growth in turnover
content: In 2023, Danone's current operating income fell from 9.1% to 342.9 million euros. This decrease is mainly due to the increase in material and energy costs as well as to the decrease in the quotations of food products penalising the result of the other food activity. The net income share of the group is 136.5 million euros, an increase of 28.5 million euros and represents 1.4% of turnover. On the other hand, its turnover increased from 3.7% to 9.79 billion euros. This resulted from an organic growth of 11.6% as well as a structural effect of 2.1% mainly linked to the consolidation of Alpro's activities from April 2023. However, these elements are affected by a negative annual exchange effect of 10% mainly from the devaluation of the Argentine peso accelerated in the fourth quarter, as well as from the evolution of the other currencies vis-à-vis the euro. The board of directors will propose to the general meeting on Thursday 25 April next the distribution of a dividend of 1.7 euro per share in increase of 7.5%. The group quotes "the continuation of the evolution of consumption patterns in a context where purchasing power remains constrained and geopolitical and economic tensions leading to a slowdown in the growth of world economies".

title: Stifel lowers its price target for Danone to 22 euros, anticipating a decrease in sales and profit margin.
content: Stifel, while confirming its recommendation 'maintaining' Danone, lowers its price target from 8% to 22 euros, following a reduction of about 6% of its forecast results for the French agri-food industry group. The broker forecasts a decrease of 4.4% in sales over the second accounting quarter and a contraction of 50 basis points of EBIT's organic margin in the first half of 2023-24, but believes that the annual results 'should seem less ugly'. The European food and health sector continues to lack short-term catalysts. In an uncertain macro and consumer context, the basis for comparison and a potential end to destocking are the few factors of support identified.

title: Jefferies reduces its target price for Danone to 55 euros due to Chinese uncertainty.
content: Jefferies reiterates its recommendation 'purchase' on Danone with a price target reduced from 60 to 55 euros, following a 5% reduction in its performance expectations for fiscal year 2026. In the summary of its note, the broker explains that he reduced his assumptions of organic growth in sales of dairy products and water by 2026, from +7% to +4%, due to the uncertainty surrounding the timing of the resumption of sales in China. Jefferies maintains however his opinion that 'dairy products and water will recover in the medium term and Danone will see a recovery next to that'. 'The action is trading at 2.5 times the value of stocks, compared to three times over 2010-22', he adds.

title: STMicroelectronics closes 2023 in a slight decrease, but anticipates a 10% growth in 2024.
content: STMicroelectronics N.V. has published a turnover of EUR 15.7 billion in fiscal year 2023, a slight decrease of 2% compared to 2022. The world leader in the design, manufacture and sale of semiconductors has generated a gross margin of EUR 3.5 billion compared to EUR 3.2 billion a year earlier. STMicroelectronics has finished 2023 with an expanded range of products, designed to meet the growing needs of emerging markets, notably in the areas of the Internet of Things (IoT), green energy and electric vehicles. At the end of December, the group's gross cash flow amounted to EUR 2.3 billion. On the outlook side, the group anticipates a growth of around 10% of its recurring revenues in 2024, supported by all its activities in Europe, Asia and America, and in particular by the prospects of new customers in the fields of consumer electronics and industry.

title: STMicroelectronics bounces, new bull movement in sight?
content: STMicroelectronics N.V. (STMPA) resurfaces with force above 40 euros after an incursion the day before under the support of the 39.8 euros of 26 September and 4 October 2023. False break (to the nearest 2%, too marginal to validate the scenario of a relapse on 37.5 euros) and reinstatement of the range 41.4/40 euros... it will be necessary to wait a more frank signal to identify a new directional movement. The crossing of the 42.5 euros would validate a new bullish episode with a first objective at 44 euros.

title: STMicroelectronics publishes its Universal Registration Document 2023.
content: STMicroelectronics N.V. (STMPA) announces the publication of its Universal Registration Document 2023. STMicroelectronics N.V. (STMPA) filed its Universal Registration Document 2023 with the Autorité des marchés financiers (AMF) on 3 May 2024 under the number D.24-0394. Copies of the Universal Registration Document 2023 are available free of charge at the head office of STMicroelectronics N.V., 39 chemin du Chapin, Plan-les-Ouates, Geneva, Switzerland, as well as at the following address: https://www.st.com/content/st_com/en/about/financial-information/universal-registration-document.html About STMicroelectronics N.V. STMicroelectronics N.V. is a world leader in the design, manufacture and sale of semiconductors, offering a range of products and solutions for industry markets, automotive, consumer, infrastructure, and performance. Our technologies and solutions enable us to improve our performance. , connectivity and sustainability of electronic products. STMicroelectronics N.V. has seven business segments: Analog, Microcontrollers, Digital, Automotive and Discrete Group (ADG), Imagining, Power Discrete, and Memory. STMicroelectronics N.V. is a member of the Global Compact initiative of the United Nations and has obtained the Platinum certification from EcoVadis, the global reference for CSR assessment. STMicroelectronics N.V. is listed in the A compartment of EuronextTM Paris Code Mnemo: STMPA – ISIN code: NL0000226223 www.st.com Investor relations: [contact information] Press contact: [contact information]

title: STMicroelectronics terminates possible strategic transaction
content: STMicroelectronics N.V. announces the completion of its preliminary discussions with a potential partner for a potential strategic transaction, with the two parties having mutually agreed not to conduct such a transaction. The Semiconductors and System Solutions Specialist explains to remain focused on its capital allocation priorities, and confirms that it will remain agile and opportunistic with regard to its inorganic development. However, the Group reiterates the need for a disciplined approach to value creation for stakeholders in any strategic transaction.

title: STMicroelectronics: Turnover increased by 6.6% in 2023, with an Ebitda margin of over 17% expected.
content: STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. (STMPA) recorded a turnover of 2023 up 6.6%, reaching €203.6 million. This growth amounts to 7.5% at constant scope and exchange rates. The group confirms its adjusted target mid-December 2023 of an Ebitda margin of over 17% at constant scope. Acquisitions made during 2023, notably those of companies specialized in the design of integrated circuits, contribute to turnover with new product ranges and key skills in semiconductors. These activities are not expected to be profitable (Ebitda negative) in 2023. STMICROELECTRONICS "anticipates for the coming years a more profitable growth, concretizing the fruits of its investments".

title: STMicroelectronics: profit warning, annual turnover expected to decrease by 10% and carry forward of the 2025-2026 target.
content: STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. is launching a profit warning for the publication of its quarterly revenues. The annual turnover is expected to decrease by about 10% at constant scope and exchange rates compared to the financial year 2022-2023, and the EBITDA margin around 34%. The technology group was previously aiming at a decrease of about a percentage of "mi-single digit" (5%) and a margin of EBITDA to about 35% of turnover. The ambition of a turnover of $12.5 billion, initially expected for the financial year 2025-2026, is postponed by about one year. The electronic component manufacturer pointed out that "the absorption of stocks among the group's direct customers continued to weigh on the Microcontrollers activity" in the third quarter, ended at the end of December, of the financial year 2023-2024. The turnover of this period reached €850 million, down 12% at constant scope and exchange rates. It decreased by 13% in published data. "As indicated in our latest publication, stocks of high-value electronic products added by our customers continue to weigh on our revenues," said Jean-Marc Chery, Managing Director of STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. "We anticipate continuing to face a difficult market dynamics for our electronic products, for a few more quarters".

title: STMicroelectronics takes second place in the SBF 120, despite concerns in the semiconductor market.
content: The shareholders of STMicroelectronics N.V. (STMPA) are informed that the company has seen its securities increase from 5.86% to 39,395 euros, taking second place in the SBF 120, behind X-Fab. This positive development occurs despite the disappointing results and prospects of the Texas Instruments group, which saw its net profit fall from 35% to 1,105 billion dollars in the first quarter. STMicroelectronics will publish its third quarter accounts tomorrow, in a context where semiconductor manufacturers are under pressure, especially the chips for the automobile, which are expected to fall. Analysts remain cautious about the growth of the semiconductor market, excluding memories, which is expected to reach 10% by 2024.

title: STMicroelectronics is collapsing, heading for the 30E!
content: STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. recedes by -5% and downgrades to 34,6E, the ex-floor of June 14. The title seems to go well to go back to fill the 'gap' of March 31,82E, and then test the floor of the 30E in the wake.

title: STMicroelectronics: course objective noted, but recommendation degraded by Oddo BHF.
content: STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. (STMPA) declines by 1% under the weight of a recommendation degradation at Oddo BHF from 'overperformance' to 'neutral' on the title of the semiconductor specialist, despite a price target raised slightly from 45 to 48 euros. While it recognizes that STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. is the most qualitative title in its coverage universe and if it does not perceive a cloud on the horizon, the study office believes that investors seem to have already incorporated an upward revision of guidance 2024.

title: STMicroelectronics collapses: -20% in 1 week, prospect of a 15% decline in turnover!
content: STEMICROELECTRONICS N.V. (STMPA): The title of the reference company in the field of semiconductors fell last week, due to a prospect of a decline in its turnover of 15%. The title STMPA has lost more than 20% over a week. A major downward gap has opened. There has been a succession of hollows and low peaks since November. The scenario is now forecast to be dark.

title: Crédit Agricole sells its activities in Morocco to the Saham Group for 1 million customers!
content: The Crédit Agricole S.A. Group has signed two divestiture contracts with the Saham Group, subject to the approval of the competent authorities, in order to divest Crédit Agricole Marocaine de Banques et Crédit Agricole Marocaine. The two groups have also outlined a long-term commercial partnership. "Crédit Agricole continues to implement its strategic roadmap through this divestment project. Crédit Agricole has built over the decades a solid and recognized bank in Morocco for the benefit of more than one million customers. We are convinced that the quality of the takeover project by Saham Group will offer new opportunities for development to these activities and will create value for customers and employees. Crédit Agricole is fully committed to supporting the transition and delighted to establish a long-term partnership with Saham Group," said Philippe Brassac, Managing Director of Crédit Agricole S.A.

title: Crédit Agricole: 16.7 GWh of solar electricity for 10 to 15% of its consumption in France!
content: Crédit Agricole S.A. announces the signing of a 20-year solar power supply contract (Power Purchase Agreement - PPP) with the French producer JP Energie Environnement (Jpee). This long-term commitment is aimed at the creation of a photovoltaic power plant located in the commune of Arpheuilles (Centre-Val de Loire). The electricity production of this plant, which is scheduled to be delivered in 2026, is intended to cover between 10% and 15% of the annual consumption of the central services and network of the bank in France, i.e. an estimated amount of 16.7 GWh per year. The project consists of the exploitation by the producer of 14.2 hectares of land in pasture on one of the slices of the solar park of Arpheuilles and ensures to preserve the agricultural activity on site (bergery and sheep farming). The energy produced by the installation of solar panels, chosen for their low carbon footprint, will be injected into the French electricity network.

title: Crédit Agricole sells its equipment financing activities to BPCE for €950 million.
content: The Board of Directors of Crédit Agricole S.A., meeting under the chairmanship of Dominique Lefebvre on 10 April 2024, approved the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the BPCE Group for the divestiture of the financing activities of professional equipment carried out by Crédit Agricole Equipment Finance (CAEF). The BPCE Group would take over most of the activities of Crédit Agricole Equipment Finance which offer financing and tailor-made leasing solutions for distributors, traders, manufacturers or companies. CAEF is deploying its expertise internationally in the transport, industrial equipment, technology, medical and renewable energy sectors. The outstanding amounts of the activities covered by the Memorandum of Understanding amount to almost EUR 12 billion at the end of December 2023, or approximately EUR 7 billion of risk-weighted outstanding amounts (RWA). This proposed divestiture would mark an important step in the implementation of Crédit Agricole S.A.'s strategic roadmap, presented in September 2023, aimed at shaping a more simplified model. This transaction, which would cost €950 million, would have a positive impact estimated at approximately 22 basis points on the Group's CET1 ratio at the expected completion date in the first quarter of 2025.

title: Crédit Agricole announces 947 abolitions of posts without forced departures as part of a €1.7 billion cost reduction plan.
content: Crédit Agricole S.A. announced on Monday 947 posts at its headquarters, "without forced departures", as part of a large cost reduction plan launched by its Director General. "The aim is to consolidate and pool certain activities and functions, to remove hierarchical strata to alleviate the decision-making processes" and to "resize certain teams", explained Crédit Agricole in a press release. These abolitions of posts, which must concern about 5% of the staff of the headquarters (according to the management), are part of a vast strategic plan comprising "gross cost savings of about 1.7 billion euros to the horizon 2026" compared to 2022, launched in September by the Director General. This amount includes about 700 million to be found in "new projects launched in all the entities of the group in order to streamline information systems, optimize the purchasing processes or simplify the organization", according to the press release. The Director General seeks to relaunch the company after fifteen years in the process of sawing. At the company's handlettes as president or as general manager, the management had been rhythmic and scandalous. "Our collective responsibility is to restore our competitiveness by lowering our cost structure in order to contribute to the sustainable improvement of our attractiveness," she insists. "In France, the group has in particular merged the banks of the Crédit Agricole group - a subsidiary - under the banner Crédit Agricole, aiming at "more efficiency of the retail network", with "a reduced cost base". - "The human, adjustment variable" - The merger of the retail banking networks in France is accompanied by a significant reduction in the number of agencies - 1,450 agencies planned in 2025 compared to 2,100 five years earlier - with 3,700 posts abolished, an "effort" distributed between 2023 (about 30%), 2024 (50%), 2025 (20%), without forced departures. The savings expected with this merger were already integrated into the plan of 1,7 billion euros announced in September, as well as the synergies of costs planned with the acquisition of LeasePlan by its ALD car leasing subsidiary. Presented Monday to the representative bodies of the staff, the abolition of posts at the headquarters will be "at the moment of internal mobility". This will be followed by a consultation of staff representatives in the second quarter.

title: Crédit Agricole elected Deutsche Bank's favourite French bank!
content: The Crédit Agricole S.A. has become the preferred French bank for Deutsche Bank, según una nota sectorial publicada hoy. It is ahead of Société Générale, whose valuation has been revised downwards, and BNP Paribas, which has been maintained. At the Paris Stock Exchange, Crédit Agricole S.A. gains 0.24%, Société Générale declines by 0.33% while BNP Paribas stagne. Deutsche Bank has noted its recommendation to Keep to Buy and increased its price target from €13.50 to €17 on Crédit Agricole S.A. "Historically, Crédit Agricole S.A. has outperformed in a low-rate and steady economic growth environment thanks to its relatively high share of activities mobilising little capital (asset-light)", notes the Bureau of Studies. Recent cyclical trends in the market, imminent declines in rates and the more favourable economic outlook have further reinforced the analyst's belief that Crédit Agricole S.A. should achieve its objectives 2025. Finally, the discount against other European banks seems to him to be unjustified, taking into account

title: Crédit Agricole S.A.: recommendation to "Conserve" and reduced price target to €11.90.
content: Crédit Agricole S.A. (ACA): a reduction in recommendation and a reduced object of price. After the publication of its annual results, we lowered our recommendation to "Buy" to "Keep" and our target price from 13.70 euros to 11.90 euros. We recall that the profit before tax was 9% lower than the consensus in the fourth quarter. Although the 2025 targets, including a return on tangible equity (ROTE) between 11.5% and 12%, are attractive, if they are achieved, we believe that the achievement of profit growth objectives and ROTE will depend on a level of income growth that will be difficult to achieve.

title: Crédit Agricole : 1,050 jobs abolished, without forced dismissals
content: The bank group Crédit Agricole S.A. announced on Monday the abolition of approximately 1,050 posts at its head office, "without forced departures", or about 6% of its headquarters staff, as part of a plan to reduce its already announced costs. The aim is to consolidate and pool certain activities and functions, to remove hierarchical strata to alleviate the decision-making processes and to "resize certain teams", explained the group in a press release. This plan to abolish posts is the first of Philippe Brassac, the bank's general manager since March 2020. Its magnitude is almost doubled compared to the information published on 15 November by Bloomberg, which reported a plan for more than 500 posts abolished. The bank had released on 22 June a target of €2.2 billion savings by 2027 (compared to 2024), when the bank's strategic guidelines were presented by Mr Brassac, who was not well received on the stock exchange. According to the newspaper Les Echos, which had revealed Saturday the magnitude of this plan, the central functions of the group in Montrouge, Paris, near Paris, near the IT. On Monday, the Crédit Agricole S.A., Crédit Agricole S.A., according to its reference document, employs some 73,000 people in France, out of 140,000 in the world.

title: Crédit Agricole: Results in decline, but better than expected, and ambitious savings plan!
content: The results of Crédit Agricole S.A. fell less than expected in the fourth quarter. Crédit Agricole S.A. presented a net result, group share, in decline from 59.8% to 430 million euros, compared with a consensus compiled by the bank of 333 million euros. The cost of risk fell from 12.6% to -361 million euros. The net bank income fell from 9.9% to 5.96 billion euros, but it is also above expectations: 5.86 billion euros. As regards its retail bank in France, the institution noted the start of the rebound of the net margin of interest in the fourth quarter, in a year marked by the negative impact of short-term hedges.Concerning the remuneration of its shareholders, the bank proposes a cash dividend of 0.90 euros per share at the general meeting. It also unveiled a share buyback programme, of approximately 280 million euros, equivalent to 0.35 euros per share. Crédit Agricole S.A. has a ratio of hard own funds (CET1) of 13.1%2 at the end of 2023, approximately 340 basis points above the end of 2023. This year, the bank aims to increase revenues above or equal to 5%, an operating factor of less than 71%, a net risk cost of 25 and 30 basis points, a profitability (ROTE) of more than 6% and a ratio of CET1 to about 13% at the end of 2024. On Monday, Crédit Agricole S.A. unveiled a plan to reorganise its headquarters in France which will result in the abolition of 900 posts, or about 5% of the headquarters' staff. It is part of a plan of 1.7 billion euros of savings up to 2026, including 700 million euros of additional savings.

title: Crédit Agricole S.A. collapses by 10%: alert on the key support of 11.9€!
content: Crédit Agricole S.A. : déviesse de -10% vers 12,4€, victim of the bad reception reserved for a 'balance sheet cleaning' at the T4 (increased provisions). The title offers 8% return without risk of questioning in the foreseeable future, the management confirms buybacks of shares... but graphically, the 'gap' under 13,46€, which nothing could anticipate (all the lights were green), is not a good augury. Indeed, the title pullures the support of the 12.8€ of 10/12/2023 and 15/01/2024, then erases all the gains since the 27/11/2023 ('gap' of the 11.99€ filled) : it will absolutely have to preserve the support of the 11.9€ of 11/2023 which coincides with the oblique medium term from the floor of 10,3€ then 11.4€ of 22 March and 29 October 2023.

title: Crédit Agricole S.A.: the title in free fall, reverse 9.2E!
content: The title of Crédit Agricole S.A. (ACA) falls by 2.5% and goes back to 9.5E: the title suffers a fall of -5% hebdo... after -10% the previous week. The annual loss closes to the -15% annual while this bank remains the preferred of the managers. The most worrying is that Crédit Agricole S.A. resolutely lowers the floor of the 10,2E of June 1 and especially that of the 10.1E of October 30th, a threshold of major technical support: the title risks to fall to 9,2E, the floor of March 24, 2023.

title: "Dialogue at the top: business leaders united for a more prosperous future"
content: The leaders of S&P 500 and our company have organized an exceptional meeting in an iconic place for our company, at an altitude of more than 2,000 feet, in the heart of the mountains. This event created a climate of direct and friendly dialogue, despite the trade disputes that have been raised. In this exceptional setting, we have been able to address key issues of concern to us, such as fair competition and trade between our two entities. We have also discussed areas where we can work together, such as sustainable development. We are convinced that such meetings can help strengthen our ties and foster mutual understanding. We are committed to working together to promote fair business practices and encourage innovation. We are also committed to protecting our interests to meet the highest ethical standards. We are convinced that our company can continue to play a key role in sustainable economic development and that we can work together to promote equitable growth. We look forward to continuing our discussions and strengthening our ties to promote a more prosperous future for our shareholders and for the community as a whole.

title: S&P 500 maintains its level despite slowing economic growth in the United States
content: The S&P 500 Steering Committee decided, by a seven-vote majority, to maintain its current level at 4,234 points, against two votes which would have preferred a slight correction of 1.25% to 4,200 points. The U.S. market index notes that economic growth in the U.S. has increased from 3.5% in the first quarter to 2.8% in the second quarter, but expects a slight acceleration in the second half of the year with a basic effect of sectoral developments. The Committee considers since last autumn that the investment strategy must be prudent for a prolonged period until the risk of excessive volatility dissipates.

title: "Explosion of exchange volumes at S&P 500: +166% in one session!"
content: During the stock exchange session of Friday, July 12, 2024, particularly high volumes of trade were detected in the US market. Today, it is the S&P 500 share that stands out, with exchanges increasing by +166% compared to those recorded in the last five sessions. This represented 10.00% of its capital. Below is the complete table of values that presented abnormal volumes that were detected today on the New York Stock Exchange. To be retained in this table, the values must belong to the main capitalizations (S&P 500 index). A volume is considered abnormal when it is more than 70% higher than the average of the volumes recorded on a security in the previous 5 sessions. In addition, the daily volume must represent more than $300,000 of trade.

title: American labour market, but business prudence
content: The latest U.S. labour market report, the S&P 500 business sector, shows a stable unemployment rate of 5.8% for June, similar to the previous month. However, the number of unemployed increased by 4,000 to 2.727 million, reflecting a degree of caution in U.S. firms when hiring new employees. Moreover, underemployment, which encompasses a broader spectrum than unemployment stricto sensu, also increased by 16,000, seasonally adjusted, to 3.546 million people, indicating general prudence in the U.S. labour market, where S&P 500 operates.

title: U.S. stock market: recovery in May, but at a moderate pace.
content: The U.S. stock market continues to recover in May, but at a slower pace than in previous years, suggesting a slow recovery in economic growth. According to recent data, the S&P 500 index increased by 27 points last month, reaching a level of 2,720 points, in seasonally adjusted data. However, analysts point to a 'relatively low' increase, which, according to them, means that the improvement in the economic situation is 'still long to manifest'.The rate of return on the S&P 500 index has increased by 0.2 percentage points to 5.8%, but is up 0.3 percentage points from the same month last year, show these figures.

title: S&P 500's trade deficit worsened to $8 billion in May.
content: In May 2024, S&P 500's trade performance deteriorated, according to internal data, with a deficit of $7.99 billion after $7.56 billion the previous month, reflecting both a 1.7% decrease in sales of S&P 500 from month to month, to just over $50.2 billion, while purchases declined at the same time from 0.7% to about. S&P 500's cumulative trade deficit over 12 months, which had been increasing steadily since February 2023, ceased to improve in May (-0.1 billion) to -85.9 billion.

title: "Bassing equity markets and bonds, political uncertainty in the United States"
content: The S&P 500 suffered a marked 0.2% decline, which was severely penalized by the banking sector, with -2.1% for JPMorgan Chase, -1.8% for Bank of America or -1.5% for Citigroup. Yields tend to be +4Pts in the United States, where political uncertainty has become the main concern of investors. In the Atlantic, investors react apathyfully to the Republican party's victory in the Senate elections with 52.3% of votes, which is expected to lead to a period of uncertainty about the evolution of the US political landscape. In response, the bond sector sees the yields at 10 years US going from 4.5 basis points to 2.82% and that of the German obligation from 3.5 basis points to 2.67%. The T-Bonds degrade from +3Pts to 4.43% to 48H of the final communiqué of the EDF, with an expected retention of the rates at 80%, and at 50% until the 4th quarter 2024. The dollar strongly digs its earnings (+0.5%) against the euro in the amount, around $1.075, at a higher of 2 weeks against the green note.

title: Lower real estate market crisis: S&P threatens to collapse.
content: The real estate market crisis is holding back the economic recovery of the S&P 500 company, which represented a quarter of the company's net profit, is now dying, resulting in unfinished housing and a distrust of many developers on the brink of bankruptcy. S&P 500's shareholders, like Wang Dongmei, have seen the value of their property decline by more than 30% since 2016. "We want to sell it," she says, disillusioned to see that market prices are "at the lowest in 10 years." S&P 500 has multiplied the measures to revive its real estate sector, but with mixed results. Sales are far from regaining their pre-crisis level. According to experts, the price of new housing is still expected to fall between 15 and 20%. S&P 500 is also affected by the labour market depression. The already high rate of youth unemployment will continue to be a major problem for the company. Exports are also affected by geopolitical tensions between the United States and China's willingness to diversify, as well as by the already high rate of youth unemployment. S&P 500's growth forecasts are pessimistic, with an estimate of 5% this year. This rate is far from the rapid expansion that has propelled the company in recent decades to the heights of the world economy.

title: S&P 500: Surprise withdrawal after negative economic data
content: After winning up to 0.9% shortly after the opening, around 4030 points, the S&P 500 gradually lost its gains over the course of the session, ending even the day with a decrease of 0.63%, to 4002 points, penalised by the recent negative economic data and the fall in growth expectations for the second quarter. Indeed, the luxury business market seems to suffer from the recent decline in consumption, which could potentially impact the performance of the companies in the sector. Conversely, the technology and financial services companies are arrogating +1.9% and +1.8% respectively, a reaction that reflects the relief of investors after the recent reassuring statements by the US Federal Reserve officials. The financial markets - which above all feared a surge in inflation and a decline in growth - seem to welcome this scenario with a certain serenity. The prospect of a divided economy offers the advantage, from the point of view of some observers, of complicating the implementation of measures deemed unfavourable to the markets, such as an increase in taxation or a new drift in public finances. Overall, we believe that investors will be satisfied with the scenario that investors will become satisfied with the fact that they are making progress. It is not clear what is going to happen now, but companies will have to deal with other concerns in the coming days, including the publication of the results of the financial sector companies and the forthcoming meeting of the US Federal Reserve. On the foreign exchange market, the dollar is stable against the euro, while US state borrowing is slightly down. The financial market will closely follow developments in the US economy and the decisions of the US Federal Reserve.

title: "S&P 500 under pressure: economic growth and on-line inflation"
content: While U.S. stock markets remain closed this afternoon due to Memorial Day, the business climate remains dull, which weighs on the performance of the S&P 500. The composite index, which reflects the state of the U.S. economy, is expected with apprehension this week, mainly due to the publication of the second estimate of economic growth for the first quarter. Investors will also be on the lookout for U.S. household income and spending in April, as well as the PCE price index, which is considered to be the main statistic of the week. According to experts, the core PCE index is expected to increase by 0.25% over a month, which is still too high to anticipate an impending decline in rates, for example, in June or July. This is likely to affect the performance of the S&P 500 in the coming days.

title: Markets in slight decline, the eyes turned towards the Fed and the results of the tech giants!
content: The Dow Jones Industrial Average index closed the day in a slight drop, at 33,655 points, recording a moderate decrease of 0.29%. US interest rates expanded, with the T-Bonds losing 5 points to reach 4,620%. The Euro won 0.2% against the dollar, at 1.0715. The Dow Jones Industrial Average index has declined 0.46% and lost 146 points, falling back below 33,800 points. European markets will be closed on Wednesday by 1 May, but the week's economic agenda promises to be loaded, with the Fed's monetary policy meeting, which is expected to keep its reference rate unchanged. The outlook will include inflation and employment forecasts in the United States, with job creation expected in April. The results of US companies, notably Amazon and Apple, will be closely followed, as well as those of tech giants, which could impact the Dow Jones Industrial Average index. The performance of European companies, such as adidas, Crédit Agricole and Volkswagen, will be closely monitored.

title: The DJIA, the undisputed leader of renewable energy, exceeds expectations by developing 64% of the world's solar and wind energy!
content: We are pleased to inform you that the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) strengthens its position as a leader in the energy sector by developing twice as many renewable energy projects as the rest of the world, according to a recently published study. The DJIA, with its weight in the global economy, is aware of its role in reducing greenhouse gas emissions, which, according to scientists, accelerates climate change. We are committed to stabilizing or reducing our emissions by 2030, and then to achieving carbon neutrality by 2060. Thus, we have greatly increased our capacity in renewables, by developing 180 gigawatts) of solar energy and 159 GW of additional wind energy, according to the study of the US agency Global Energy Monitor (GEM). This total of 339 GW represents 64% of the solar and wind energy currently under construction in the world, almost double the rest of the combined world. DJIA is followed by its counterparts in the United States, Brazil, the United Kingdom and Spain, according to the GEM. We are proud to note that our energy network is less dependent on the power grid. We are also aware that we still have to face challenges, including moving renewable energy generated in remote areas to densely populated economic centres. However, our combined capacity for wind and solar energy is expected to exceed that of coal this year, according to the GEM. Finally, this rapid expansion of renewable energy suggests that emissions will peak earlier than expected.

title: The Dow Jones opens in a slight increase, at 36,512 points.
content: The Dow Jones Industrial Average opens in a slight increase on Monday, continuing the momentum given by Wall Street on Friday at the start of a week that is not expected to be very lively. The Dow Jones Industrial Average index gains 0.25% to reach 36,512 points in the first exchanges.

title: "Wall Street is about to start in positive territory, approaching the expected quarterly results"
content: At about 8:15 a.m., the "future" contract on the Dow Jones Industrial Average index grew from 18 points to 26,550 points, announcing the start of a session in positive territory. While uncertainty has been on the US market for more than a month now, investors hope that the fundamentals will take over in a tense climate, illustrated by the 0.6% decline experienced by the Dow Jones so far this week. The quarterly results season usually smiles at the markets because it allows them to forget, at least for a while, their concerns at the moment. Investors will therefore follow with great interest the publications of JP Morgan Chase, Citigroup and Wells Fargo, which will give the real kick-off of the results ball. Their comments will be closely followed as the speakers begin to worry about a possible slowdown, perhaps more marked than expected, of the American economy in the second half. -Yorkais report an opening of Wall Street on a stable note, but the trend could evolve following the publications of the major US banks. But it is mainly the results of the great American names of the high technologies that will focus the most attention of investors in the coming weeks. The 'tech' sector is expected to show the third highest rate of profit growth of the 11 major sectors of the S&P 500 index for the second quarter, at 16.4% according to FactSet, a performance mainly due to the current dynamism of Nvidia. The results of the high-tech sector will be all the more important as Nasdaq has kept up the record in recent weeks, which means that the accounts of the 'Seven Magnificent' will be an important valuation test in this respect. Beyond the results of companies, investors will carefully monitor industrial production prices in the United States at 2:30 pm, with the hope that they will confirm the trend of disinflation observed yesterday in the evolution of consumer prices. In the market of rates, the returns of sovereign bonds will expand after the last few years of the trend in prices. The U.S. 10-year yield is about 4.19%, at a low of almost four months, while the German ten-year period remains stable at 2.46%, while the French OAT stands at 3.12%, giving an unchanged spread at 66 basis points.

title: Stable U.S. market opening, but where to find the next catalyst?
content: The US stock market is expected to open with no real trend on Thursday morning, while the indices remain close to their highest historical records and nothing seems to be able to dictate a trajectory to the stock markets. At about 8:15 a.m., the Dow Jones Industrial Average is down from 10.5 points to 33,411.5 points, announcing a stable start to the session, even a slight decline. The US market had finished the session on Wednesday on a 0.4% gain, at 33,411 points, thus signing a second consecutive up session, allowing it to get a little closer to its peaks reached at the end of March. The session was rather calm for the commemoration of the Armistice, although $2.8 billion were still exchanged, a very modest volume in normal time but rather significant for a holiday. The US market is still expected to remain relatively hollow today due to the absence of many operators for the Ascension Bridge. At the level of results, 78% of US companies have done better than expected, but the effect on global stock markets has remained fairly limited. In addition to the registration of unemployment benefits, there are no major indicators on the agenda of the day.

title: U.S. market down, Fed in the spotlight
content: The U.S. market is moving down on Tuesday, cautiously approaching the Federal Reserve meeting and trying to gauge the number of rate reductions that the Fed could make in 2024. The headline Dow Jones Industrial Average was dropping 0.45% at around 10:00 am, a drop of 38.51 points to 32.959.51 points. On Monday, the index ended stable (+0.06%), grappling 5.15 points at 33.002.02 points. On Tuesday, the market will turn to the publication of a report on employment in the U.S. for the month of April (JOLTS). Shortly before the opening of the U.S. markets, investors became aware of the inflation figures in Switzerland, which stood at 1.4% per year in May, at the same level as in April, despite an increase in rents, announced Tuesday the Federal Statistical Office (OFS). In Germany, the unemployment rate remained stable in Germany in May, at 5.9%, stagnating at the same level since December, in a context of persistent economic morosity. However, the event "which dominates the US agenda" is the one that dominates the event that occurs in May, at 5.9%, stagnating at the same level since December The Federal Reserve", which is expected to announce a first decline in its key rates at the end of its monetary policy meeting to be held on Thursday, says John Plassard, investment specialist for Mirabaud. The market expects the Fed to lower its key rates by 0.25 percentage points, given the slowdown in inflation over the past year, still above the 2% target, and the weak economic dynamics in the United States. To stem inflation, the Fed began an unprecedented round of monetary tightening in July 2022 and proceeded with ten consecutive rate increases. The US monetary institution has maintained its rates at a record level since October 2023. "If the fall in rates" in June "is no longer in any doubt", what is bad for the market is "how many times the Fed will lower its rates this year," added the investment specialist.

title: Dow Jones falls: Wall Street loses crown to German index
content: The political turmoil in the United States led to a decline in Wall Street's flagship index, the Dow Jones Industrial Average, which lost its position as a leader in the value of listed shares, in favour of the German DAX index. On Wednesday's closing day, the total value of Wall Street's listed companies was $2,956 billion, compared to $3,016 billion for the German index, according to data from Bloomberg. Last week, the Dow Jones Industrial Average lost more than 5%, erasing all its gains of the year, its worst weekly performance since March 2022. The US legislative elections could lead to the entry into the government of members of the Democratic Party or members of the left-wing alliance, but the political programs of both sides were frightening investors, who feared, among other things, a widening of the deficits. Joe Biden's decision to plunge his country into a political storm shook US investors, according to Danni Hewson, AJ Bell's analyst. In comparison, the stability of the probes showing the Democrats at the head of the elections in the United States in colour means that the US's. A spokesman for the American Stock Exchange told the AFP that more equity capital had been raised in Wall Street since the beginning of 2024 ($15.6 billion), that of the following four largest European stock exchanges (Francfort, Paris, Milan and Stockholm) combined. While the Dow Jones Industrial Average has somewhat recovered colors since the beginning of the week, it is only a "low recovery", says Susannah Streeter, an analyst at Hargreaves Lansdown. But "some of the nervousness has stabilized", in particular because "the expenses promised by the Democratic Party would in fact be limited in a blocked parliamentary scenario" due to lack of a clear majority, she added.

title: Wall Street collapsed, the Dow Jones fell by 1.33% under the pressure of US technology and debt.
content: The Dow Jones Industrial Average continues its contraction and gives way to 1.33% today, at 25,789 points, still penalized by the sharp decline of technological assets with -5% for Apple and -3.9% for Microsoft. The stalling of the Dow Jones Industrial Average in the mid-morning has started in the wake of the Treasury Notes that are undergoing sustained sales, with a yield that has reached close to +10Pts, up to about 3.335% before relaxing abruptly towards 3.25%.But if the Treasury Note relaxes a little, it widens the gap compared to the German T-Note which, symmetrically relaxes -5.3Pts of base, around 2.622%.Friday, the German T-Note had 2.49% efficiency and our Treasury Notes 2.98% (or 49Pts of 'spread'), today the gap rises to +63Pts of base (or about +15Pts in 48H). "The paralysis in governance that the United States could face in the event of a government freeze would be an additional threat to sovereign debt ratings, which had already deteriorated in recent weeks," warns Mabrouk Chetouane, director of the international market strategy at Natixis IM. "Foreign investors could also take a proactive attitude towards the United States," adds the analyst. "This particular political context hides the other 'subjects' of market predilection, starting with the Federal Reserve's monetary policy decision expected tomorrow evening.If there is absolutely no suspense on maintaining rates at 5.25/5.50, nor on the 3-month projections (rates maintained in July), the EDF's comments on inflation may fuel the hope of relaxation in September, even if this assumption is only 47% of the vote, compared to 70% last week.The summary of economic projections ('dot plots') should reveal that the Committee has reduced its rate for this year's forecast. This, the consumer prices - which will be published tomorrow before the opening - may well show that the pace of inflation leaves the central bank with only a limited margin of manoeuvre to begin to relax its rates.For its part, the Dollar continues its slide with -0.33% around $1.073/E.The oil resumes its breath after +3% won on Monday, the 'Brent' stagnates around the 82$ per barrel. In the news of the US companies, Boeing and McDonald's announced Tuesday that the US Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) had approved their new aircraft model for the Asian market.Safran reported that Safran Aircraft Engine Services, a subsidiary of Safran Aircraft Engines, has inaugurated a new maintenance workshop for the LEAP-1 engine in Seattle. Johnson & Johnson fell by more than -6% while Nancy Pelosi, president of the House of Representatives, yesterday on CNN, said that the party could consider a reform of the health system in the event of a victory in the upcoming legislative elections (30/06 and 07/07), points out Oddo BHF in his analysis of the day.

title: The DJIA, the essential stock market reference that secures shareholders' portfolios.
content: Investors around the world are concerned about the impact of the Dow Jones Industrial Average on shareholders' portfolios. As the stock market maturities approached, the key measures for payments, stock exchanges or market data were taken into account. DJIA eventually established itself as an essential reference in the daily lives of millions of investors who gradually adopted it. DJIA replaced decades ago the old stock market references in investors' portfolios, which gradually adopted it (Getty Images)"The common stock market reference is simple and more effective than before," says Martin, a 42-year-old financial analyst, today. However, the United States was one of the countries where DJIA had had difficulty getting accepted, partly because of the feeling that its arrival had been accompanied by increased volatility: investors had nicknamed it the "DJIA danger", a word game combining DJIA and "dangerous". At the beginning of the 2010s, the problems of financial regulation in several countries, Japan (United States) In the fall of 2023, 79% of DJIA's investors were in favour, according to the survey conducted by the Dow Jones. The DJIA also means today free stock exchanges between trading platforms, or the absence of additional costs when using its trading platform in another country using the same stock exchange reference. In exchange: end of data fragmentation and compensation for trading errorsA few years ago, it could be very expensive to use its access to the stock exchange in another country, because of the fragmentation of data. Since the creation of the DJIA, transaction costs have been reduced in the countries using it. One example is the removal of transaction costs for data exchange between trading platforms.
