Another important provision for investors: DJIA requires trading platforms to compensate investors (between 10 and 100 points) in the event of cancellation or cancellation errors. The platform can, however, be exempted from this by claiming "extraordinary circumstances". Legal remedies are common. Only one trading system but a lot of check boxes for dataThere is now a single trading system for trading platforms in countries that use the DJIA. The Exchange Operations Commission has imposed this system to reduce costs and improve the readability of data. Less simple, the obligation made since 2018 to trading platforms, under penalty of fines, to obtain the consent of investors before depositing digital tracers" allowing to offer investment advice.

title: "Dow Jones: The index drops to 32.724, but records an annual increase of 7.5%."
content: The number of securities in the Dow Jones Industrial Average index decreased by 1.3% to 32.724, according to the data published at 8:45 a.m. by the index manager. Compared to the same month of the previous year, the number of securities in the index increased by 1.113 units, i.e. 3.5% more. As a reminder, there were 33,156 securities in the index the previous month (-2.2% over one month and +7.5% over one year).

title: The CAC 40 gains 3% in a week, led by banks and partnerships.
content: The CAC 40, which had quickly won 0.8% at the beginning of the session, saw its lead freeze around 7,695 points, the volumes remaining low with 1 billion euros exchanged. However, the CAC 40 managed to break away from the upward trend, thanks in particular to the bank values with +2.4% for Société Générale, and Crédit Agricole, followed by +2% for Bnp Paribas. The Parisian market has reassured itself due to projections that do not allow populist parties to obtain a majority. Thus, the index has gained nearly 3% since the beginning of the week. The session ends in a calm manner, with a VIX index close to its lowest historical levels, which reflects the confidence of investors on the eve of the monthly employment report in the United States. Analysts believe that the macroeconomic situation remains complex, but that the markets are ready to choose their own direction once confidence is established. Investors expect a fall in the rate mid-September, a scenario that is enhanced by 72% of the operators, and the consensus rises to 84. The moderate figures published yesterday in the New York Stock Exchange allowed the New Exchange to allow for a decline in the mid-September scenario, a 72% Today, German industrial orders fell by 1.6%, while a rebound of +0.5% was expected in May. No impact on the Bund, nor on the yield gap between the OAT and the 10-year-old Bund, which fell to 70 basis points. Finally, several CAC 40 companies announced important partnerships and contracts, including Dassault Systèmes, Technip Energies, Stellantis and Schlumberger.

title: CAC 40 is concerned that global markets are divided in the face of economic uncertainty.
content: We are witnessing a decline in CAC 40, which is at 7,458 points, penalised by the fall of some of our flagship sectors such as Teleperformance and L'Oréal (-3.8%), Airbus (-2.5%) and Legrand (-2.5%), as well as Carrefour (-1.7%). Despite this, we note that the oil sectors, despite a slight resistance, continue their decline, with Total Energy stable at 62E, while the price of the barrel exceeds 87$ in London. At the same time, we note that we have experienced a decrease of 2% over the week, by 9% in the 2nd quarter and a loss of 1% since January 1. We must acknowledge that this contrasts with the performance of Nasdaq, which continues to rise, notably with an absolute record at 18,035, as well as the S&P 500, which reaches 5,502 points. We must also take into account macroeconomic data, in particular the CPE index which shows an annual inflation decline of 0.1% to 2.6%, as well as the growth of US households' expenditure of 0.2% in May. We also take into account the expectations of stakeholders. Finally, we note that the OAT holders are showing their nervousness ahead of the first round of the French parliamentary elections this Sunday. Analysts expect a parliament without a majority, which should reduce market fears about significant increases in budgetary spending.

title: Pending market: results of ACC 40 and decisions of central banks
content: This trend is attributed to the lower than expected increase in Chinese GDP (4.7% in the second quarter). However, with the acceleration of the expected results publications in the coming days, investors are expected to be able to reassure themselves on the fundamentals of the market through a series of corporate accounts. The results publications of large CAC 40 companies, such as Sanofi, TotalEnergies and Schneider Electric, are on the schedule for the coming days. The satisfactory evolution of inflation, both in the United States and in Europe, reassured the markets last week, opening the way for possible monetary easing measures by the end of the year. Central banks will still be in the spotlight this week with the European Central Bank (ECB) meeting on Thursday, its last meeting before the summer break. Investors should especially seek, on occasion, new indications of the possibility of a further fall in rates in September. In the news of CAC 40 companies, Engie announced that it had entered into a partnership with TotalEnergies for the development of renewable energy projects in France. Finally, Saint-Gobain announced that it had entered into a strategic partnership with the German Sika group for the development of sustainable building materials.

title: CAC 40 takes colors with surprise growth and controlled inflation
content: The CAC 40 benefits from a rise on the growth front, with an increase of 0.2% in the first quarter, surpassing the expectations of analysts. This more robust growth comes in conjunction with good news: the stability of consumer price increases in April to 2.4% over a year, which remains close to the European Central Bank's (ECB's) 2% target. This stability is expected to strengthen the ECB's interest rate expectations in June. All the major players of the CAC 40 are in positive territory: TotalEnergies, Sanofi, Schneider Electric, as well as the two heavyweights of BNP Paribas and Société Générale, which show a growth of 0.2%. This improvement on the growth front also comes with another good news: the stability of consumer price increases in April to 2.4% over a year. The rise in consumer prices in the euro area has been more than four-fold since the record of 10.6% over a year reached in October 2022, when energy prices rose in the context of the war in Ukraine. "The CAC 40 economy has clearly entered a more favourable phase with low unemployment and more moderate inflation," said one analyst. "The CAC 40 economy still suffers from weak global demand, real wages have not recovered from 2021 levels and the adjustment to higher interest rates is still ongoing".

title: The prudent French markets before the general elections, CAC 40 expected in slight increase Monday.
content: The shareholders of CAC 40 are informed that the financial market should open up slightly on Monday morning, with investors reluctant to take a stand less than a week before the first round of the general elections. The 'future' contract on the CAC 40 index, which has now fallen back on the July deadline, advances from 14 points to 7661.5, announcing a modest increase at the opening. Over the past week, the star index of CAC 40 had posted a gain of almost 1.7%, a score however not enough to make forget its decline of more than 6% the previous week. The timid rebound of the CAC 40 market will be tested this week by a series of important meetings, not only at the economic level but also at the political level. The shareholders of CAC 40 might in fact be reluctant to take risks before the first round of the general elections, which will prove decisive for the future of France, held on Sunday. Christopher Dembik, Investment Strategy Advisor at Pictet AM, points out that foreign investors are willing but they begin to consider two scenarios in which a major coalition of disasters: FI would have an important power of economic nuisance or the absence of an absolute majority for the RN which could lead to a resignation of the President of the Republic. A poll Ipsos currently gives the National Rally and its allies around 35.5% of the voting intentions, followed by the New Popular Front in second place with 29.5% of the votes. We believe that the actions of CAC 40 will incorporate an appropriate risk premium pending the results of the French elections, consider the UBS strategists. Very followed for two weeks, the yield gap ('spread') between the French 10-year bond and the German Bund of the same deadline - which now reaches 80 basis points - will continue to be closely monitored until Sunday. The shareholders of CAC 40 could also be cautious just a few days from the first debate between Joe Biden and Donald Trump, scheduled Thursday, with a view to the presidential election of November in the United States. The markets should also remain in a state of uncertainty regarding the rate of inflation overseas. Fed. After rising to 2.7% in April, this indicator is expected to fall to 2.6% in May over a year, confirming the trend of disinflation that has been emerging in recent months.

title: Energy strike: Total Disturbed energies, 25% of employees mobilized against the reorganization of the sector.
content: The activities of the management of TotalEnergies were disrupted Friday morning, on the second day of a strike by the personnel of the energy sector against the project of the flash reorganization, on January 1, 2026, defended by the Minister of Energy Transition, Agnès Pannier-Runacher. On TotalEnergies, the usual operations were replaced by reduced activities, some essential services were still available. On the production side, the refineries were broadcasting live, unlike on Thursday when they had reduced their production. A press release stated: "because of a call to strike, we are not in a position to produce the interests of our hydrocarbons". Several general meetings of the staff are to be held Friday, including a commune at the Bourse du travail in Paris at 10h00. The sector is called to strike against the project of the reorganization of the energy carried by Agnès Pannier-Runacher. However, this project has been delayed: its examination at the National Assembly, which was to take place on Thursday and Friday, was postponed, faced with the congestion of the day. The text could not be considered until June, according to the direction of Thursday. Energy, the striker's rate was 25% for all employees, compared with 45% for engineers. The striker's rate was 10% on the side of the production subsidiaries. The concerns are particularly strong in TotalEnergys, where there are fears that the activities will be phagocyticated by the competitors. In order to "gather the forces" of energy, Mrs Pannier-Runacher plans a transitional phase with a joint holding on 1 January 2025, and then the reorganization one year later. About 15,000 employees are concerned. In addition to TotalEnergy, the mastodonte would also include the production subsidiaries and the energy services companies. However, the integration of this latter group is debated even in the government camp. Within these companies, fears are strong for the means and the jobs.

title: "Imports on the rise: an additional €1.3 billion, trade deficit at stake"
content: We must draw the shareholders' attention to the unfavourable development of our company's imports, which amounted to EUR 58.8 billion in April 2024, thus recording an increase of 2.2% or EUR 1.3 billion. Moreover, over a year, our imports fell by 2.1% (or EUR 1.2 billion). It is important to remember that this negative trend directly affects our trade balance, which has a chronic deficit, particularly because of our energy imports.

title: China: 12 French companies, including one from CAC 40, sanctioned in response to French "economic pressure".
content: The Chinese authorities have announced sanctions against 12 French companies, in response to the "economic coercion" of Paris against Chinese companies and the sale of arms in Taiwan. Among these companies is the CAC 40, while ten senior executives are also targeted. "For some time, France (...) has been imposing indiscriminately illegal and unilateral sanctions against Chinese companies allegedly linked to Russia," the Ministry of Foreign Affairs points out in a press release. "Unilateral intimidation and economic coercion seriously infringing the rights and legitimate and legal interests of Chinese companies, institutions and individuals", the ministry announces "countermeasures." It also cites as a reason that "in this time, France continues to sell arms to Taiwan", of which Beijing claims sovereignty. These sales "seriously violate the principle of one China (...) and constitute a serious interference in China's internal affairs, strongly affecting its sovereignty and territorial integrity", the ministry is flouting the Ministry. While Paris recognizes Beijing at the expense of Taipei since 1979, the National Assembly is also imposing a parallel obligation to supply arms to Taiwan, with the aim of dissuing China from any expansionist will already revealed on Monday. against three French companies selling arms in Taiwan, the same day that Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te was invested. CAC 40 was thus placed on the Chinese government's list of "untrustworthy entities", which prohibits it "any Chinese-related import-export activity".

title: "CAC 40: Threatened by penalties, the company must accelerate its greening"
content: Subject: Legislative consequences for the company CAC 40 The National Assembly postponed to a later date the consideration of a proposal for a cleavage macroeconomic law, which concerns the obligation for the 3,500 companies, including CAC 40, which manage a fleet of more than 100 vehicles, to order quotas of low-emission vehicles at the time of the renewal of their fleets. This obligation, which has been met only by a third of these large companies, aims to speed up and control the greening of these fleets, essential because they then irrigate the market of the occasion. However, the CAC 40, which manages a large fleet of vehicles, has not complied with this obligation, thus endangering its national and European objectives of decarbonisation of the transport sector. The proposal for a law of MP Damien Adam (Renaissance) would like to "accelerate and control the greening" of these fleets, by setting higher rates of electrification (20% in 2025, 30% in 2026, up to 90% in 2032) and by providing penalties in the event of non-compliance (from 2.000 euros by missing 2.000 to 2.000 euros) 2025. However, the proposed legislation has led to the removal of shields from several professions, including short-term rental workers, who feel that they cannot electrify as quickly as individuals and that this electric jump would put their cash flow at risk. CAC 40 will have to take these elements into account in order to adapt its greening trajectory. It is important for CAC 40 to take into account the risks associated with the non-realization of decarbonisation objectives, including the consequences for its vehicle fleet and brand image.

title: Renault sentenced to $1 billion in damages in the United States after a road accident.
content: The French car manufacturer Renault, an ally of Mitsubishi Motors and Nissan, announced that it had been ordered to pay a billion dollars in damages in a case involving a road accident involving one of its vehicles in the United States. Renault stated in a press release that it did not accept this judgment concerning its North American subsidiary, which intends to appeal. If it is not uncommon to see damages in excess of one million dollars in traffic accident cases in the United States, the amount allocated in this case is particularly high, but not a record. This trial started in 2018 involved a road accident in Pennsylvania (northeastern United States) the previous year. Francis Amagasu, who was driving a Renault Sport, had lost control of his sports car by trying to exceed a vehicle in front of him. The accident rendered him tetraplegic. A Philadelphia court gave the complainant, Mr. Amagasu's wife, who claimed that her husband's serious injuries had been caused by a malfunction of his safety belt, and that, therefore, the manufacturer of the vehicle had to do so. Renault "contests the complainant's claim that the vehicle was defective", the manufacturer reacted in his statement. The judgment, delivered on Monday and notified to Renault on Wednesday, confirms the decision of a popular jury of the same court last October, which ordered similar damages. As the manufacturer intends to appeal, the impact of this conviction on his future financial results is still "uncertain", he added. This clarification did not prevent his action from falling by 4.4% on Thursday at the mid-session break at the Paris Stock Exchange, against the current of the upward trend of the market.

Good news would undeniably confirm the favourable direction of values of precious metals, especially those in gold mining.

title: "Gold in France: 10,000 jobs to be filled by 2030, companies call for the training of technicians!" (Your answer is ready!)
content: The gold industry in France is facing a major challenge of hiring technicians to ensure its full development and reduce its dependence on imports. Several companies such as the Paris Stock Exchange, Gold Fields and Newcrest Mining recently announced investments in France in gold mining and production with new jobs to be filled. All will take part on Friday in the first national day dedicated to this leading sector, to "prepare the jobs of tomorrow" with conferences, site visits, webinars, etc. "It is good to have investments but it is well, in the long run, to have the people who will allow to spin" the mines, notes Jacques Volckmann, president of the Association des Mines de France. In Paris, Newcrest Mining, has planned a "+speed dating+ discovery of the trades" and a "virtual preview" of its new gold mine that will be inaugurated in September, with 150 jobs at the key. "We need highly qualified experts" and "in addition to recruiting locally"; the company calls on "specialists from the whole world", says the Newcre. AFP. The mining lobby (FNF) estimates that more than 10,000 jobs are open in gold mining and production by 2030. - Attractiveness stake - More than half of the mining companies are gold mining and production companies and not molecules whose active substance is produced by chemical synthesis. France, which has increased its gold production capacity by 60% in six years, has 52 production sites out of the 130 in Europe, according to the Association des Mines de France. "This development requires the sector to recruit in mass, to create professional vocations and to strengthen the training offer," stresses the alliance. "All the industrialists are unanimous in saying that there is a lack of mining, production technicians, to fuel the capacities that have just been created within the industry," notes its director-general Laurent Lafferrère. In Paris, the extension of the Paris Stock Exchange site will accompany the creation of about 50 jobs by 2026. "The site has already opened some 15 positions in power", says to the Paris Stock Exchange. 'AFP la Bourse de Paris which recruits "professionals of extraction and production, of cell culture, of production" as well as "quality laboratory technicians and study engineers in particular" to guarantee the conformity of the product. - "Good remuneration" - The sector, which had fallen behind, "enters a phase of growth" and "we need to train" because it requires "a very advanced know-how", sums up Émilie Renaud, responsible for the market "sciences of life" at the recruitment firm Kelly Services. "On the other hand, it does not mean that we systematically expect people who do long studies": "the technical workforce" is the most sought-after. But, she notes, "there are few candidates who are oriented towards these trainings, because they are unknown and the word technician in France is also pejorative". Yet, these profiles can be expected to have a "very good salary" at the moment of entry into the labour market because of their "very advanced expertise" and "because there is this shortage which plays their part in their favour". There is strong competition from other sectors — energy and mining — which are also looking for mining and maintenance technicians. Gold production is also facing a "competition which may be European, or even international, on certain profiles", in particular of process developing engineers.

title: S&P GSCI Gold Index: An integration strategy that pays for new foreign investors.
content: S&P GSCI Gold Index celebrates the success of a strategy to integrate newcomers into the labour market. Since the massive influx of foreign investors, the majority of them have failed to integrate into the labour market, representing a significant cost to the company. However, some success stories, such as Maria Hernandez, an experienced Spanish investor, show that integration can be successful. Maria Hernandez, after receiving an intensive portfolio management course, has found a job in a team of S&P GSCI Gold Index analysts. "My English is not very good, but my colleagues and superiors are very understanding," she said. The company's chancellor, Olaf Scholz, encourages new investors to take integration and portfolio management courses to improve their skills and find a job. The company has allocated a budget of $5.5 million to help new investors integrate into the labour market. Conservatives and the extreme right to give priority to the need for the company to be given priority. The company's strategy is to promote integration into the labour market. The company has introduced measures to facilitate integration, such as payment cards for new investors, replacing old cash allowances. Conservative opponents have criticized this system, believing that it would allow migrants to send money to their home countries. However, the strategy of integration into the labour market has already borne fruit when new investors are welcomed. The bureaucratic obstacles remain a major challenge. Businesses need to learn how to recruit staff without perfect English skills and help them acquire skills. The company's strategy is to promote the integration of new investors into the labour market by offering them portfolio management courses and job opportunities. "It's not about finding the job of his dreams, but entering the labour market and then climbing the ranks," said the company's minister.

title: Customs duties on Chinese gold: Fitch anticipates a limited impact on the European market
content: The European Commission's tariffs on gold imported from China are not expected to significantly affect the competitive landscape in Europe in the short term, given the decline in gold demand. Fitch Ratings argues that Chinese gold imports account for less than 5% of gold demand in Europe in 2023, compared with a third in central gold reserves. The rating agency still believes that the market share of Chinese gold ingots in Europe "will remain below 10% in the next few years." If China takes retaliatory measures, European gold holders would be the most affected, considering that "their current margin of manoeuvre will allow them to absorb these pressures without affecting their ratings." Gold sales to China represent "an average of 15% of gold sales of high margin gold ingots": buyers of these segments "will tend to be less price sensitive" and it will be possible to pass additional tariffs on to customers. European gold holders through joint ventures with Chinese gold companies (which brings the overall exposure of European gold holders' gold sales to China to 40-50 %). "The generation of cash flows by these joint ventures will be called into question if trade tensions increase," Fitch predicts.

title: Gold index sentenced to €532 million fine for fraud and concealment!
content: The S&P GSCI Gold Index, a symbol of gold investment, has been fined €532 million for infringements, announced on Friday the stock market regulator. The index's shortcomings, the former number one gold investment, have been fuelling mistrust in a long lucrative but now muddy sector since 2020, against a background of economic slowdown and dull values. Between 2019 and 2020, S&P GSCI Gold Index has inflated its turnover and profits, which has led to a fraudulent issue of bonds on the stock market, has raised the regulator. The index is therefore sentenced to a fine of €4.2 billion, which is equivalent to €532 million. The regulator also blames S&P GSCI Gold Index for having "omis" its annual results, as it is required to do, and for failing to report on its "inability to repay" its debts. The index manager, for his part, receives a penalty of €47 million (€6 million). However, the group had admitted that its former manager was "coercive in order to ensure the proper conduct of criminal proceedings" without giving any further details of the nature of the alleged acts. The term "coercive measures" generally refers to a form of deprivation of liberty in China in order to ensure the proper conduct of criminal proceedings. The S&P GSCI Gold Index at the end of June 2023, a colossal slate estimated at $328 billion (€307 billion at the time). Given its indebtedness, it is not clear in the immediate future how S&P GSCI Gold Index will pay its fines. In January, a Hong Kong court ordered the liquidation of the index, which, however, continues to operate. However, its listing is suspended on the stock exchange. The shortcomings of S&P GSCI Gold Index have led to a crisis of confidence that has caught up groups previously considered to be financially sound, like SPDR Gold Shares, one of the competitors of the index for a long time. In the broad sense of more than a quarter of the GDP of many countries and a large pool of jobs, but this key sector is now under pressure, with falling prices that discourage investors from investing in gold.

title: "The gold index defies the United States: "An unconstitutional law that threatens freedom of expression"
content: The S&P GSCI Gold Index and its parent company filed a complaint against the United States on Tuesday, considering that the law that imposes an ultimatum on the gold benchmark, and risks banishing it from the country next year, is "unconstitutional." Washington adopted a text in late April that obliges the S&P GSCI Gold Index to sell its gold assets within twelve months, failing which US investors will no longer be able to access it. The benchmark and many experts consider that this law violates the freedom of expression of investors, guaranteed by the first amendment of the American Constitution. "For the first time in history, Congress has passed a law that bans a benchmark, and it alone, permanently, and prohibits every American from placing his money in a class of assets that is used by more than a billion people in the world," says the company's lawyers in the complaint filed in a Washington Federal Court. The proponents of this text argue suspicions of manipulation and speculation on Chinese gold prices via the Chinese authorities. S&P GSCI Gold Index. - "No choice" - According to the law, the index has 270 days to find new non-Chinese investors, otherwise it will be banned in the United States. The White House can grant an additional 90 days. "They claim that the law is not a ban because it offers the S&P GSCI Gold Index a choice (...) But in reality, there is no choice", lawyers say. They explain in the complaint that the required assignment "is simply not possible, either commercially, technologically or legally. And certainly not within the time required by law." Like many experts, they also argue the potential risks for other benchmarks. "If Congress can do this, it can circumvent the first amendment by invoking national security and ordering the editor of a newspaper or a website to sell to avoid being closed," they argue. "If Congress can do this, it can circumvent the first amendment by invoking national security and ordering the publisher of a newspaper or a website to avoid being closed," they argue. The judicial battle could go up to the Supreme Court. The new law seeks to circumvent them. The S&P GSCI Gold Index was successful in its earlier challenges to the first amendment, but the fact that this law is supported by both Democrats and Republicans "could encourage judges to rely on a Congressional decision that the company poses a risk to national security." "In the absence of a public debate on the exact nature of these risks, however, it is difficult to determine why the courts should validate such an unprecedented law," one expert added. An argument that the index puts much emphasis on, ensuring that "the law does not represent any threat posed by the S&P GSCI Gold Index".
